strong.desc: Marcel is more effective against low-mobility heroes, such as Layla and Vexana, as they are easily caught by his Ultimate, allowing him and his allies to secure easy kills.

weak.desc: Heroes who possess Invincibility or Untargetable effects that can bypass Marcel's CC, like Ling and X.Borg, will reduce his impact on the battlefield.

assist.desc: Because of Marcel's excellent AOE CC, he pairs well with allies with high AOE damage, like Hanabi and Ixia, to maximize their damage potential.

Marcel.story: Hailing from House Paxley, he is an elegant, mysterious, and bizarre artist whose split soul has fused with his camera.

skills.name: Platinum Snap
skills.desc:
Marcel's split soul, Clemar, enters the camera to freeze the moment. Marcel's Basic Attacks deal 50 (+40% Total Physical Attack)(+40% Total Magic Defense) Physical Damage (cannot Crit). He cannot benefit from extra Attack Speed. Extra HP is converted into 1.5% Hybrid Defense.

Clemar automatically snaps photos of enemy heroes near Marcel, causing a brief Frozen Moment and dealing 20 (+ 5% their Max HP) True Damage (triggers Attack Effects). It also grants a shield of the same value to Marcel and a nearby allied hero. Cooldown: 12s per target (each Basic Attack from Marcel reduces this cooldown by 1s).

skills.name: Framed Moment
skills.desc: Clemar focuses on an area around Marcel, slowing enemy heroes within by 20%. After 2.5s, he completes the shot, dealing 370 (+50% Total Physical Attack)(+100% Total Magic Defense) Physical Damage to enemies within and immobilizing them for 1s.

skills.name: Tracking Shot
skills.desc:
Artist inspiration time! Marcel enters the Tracking Haste state for 3s, gaining 15% Movement Speed (increases to 75% when moving toward enemy heroes).

Use Again: Marcel dashes in the target direction, leaving a framing phantom behind. If Framed Moment is active, Clemar remains at the original location to continue shooting.

skills.name: Golden Hour
skills.desc:
Marcel and Clemar perform a joint shoot. After a short delay, Clemar takes a wide-angle shot of the target area for 3s. During this time, Marcel enters the Tracking Haste state while inside the area. All other units in the area are put into a Frozen Moment and are immune to all effects except from Marcel. All ranged Basic Attacks and projectiles are also frozen and will continue after a delay.

Golden Hour resets the cooldown of Platinum Snap for each enemy hero.
Allied heroes with the Platinum Snap shield can move freely within the area.

strong.desc: Sora excels at diving in with explosive burst damage and AOE CC, so squishy heroes without escape abilities—like Layla, Miya, and Hanabi—will find themselves helpless against Sora's relentless assault.

weak.desc: Sora relies on landing his 1st Skill to get Cloudstep stacks. Heroes with high mobility or Knockbacks, like Fanny, Arlott, and Valir, can prevent Sora from gaining this precious resource.

assist.desc: Sora's transformed Ultimate deals considerable damage, and pairing him with Tigreal, Minotaur, Gatotkaca, and other strong CC heroes will maximize Sora's damage potential.

Sora.story: A pure and free-spirited boy with unshakeable resolve, drifting across the golden sands.

skills.name: Mystic Surge
skills.desc:
Sora can ascend to Thunder or Torrent by using his Ultimate. After ascending, dealing damage to heroes or summoned units grants 1 stack of Cloudstep. Upon reaching 5 stacks, he unlocks his True Art.

Thunder: Adopts an assassin style, converting 30% Max HP into Physical Attack (Equal to 10% of Max HP).
Torrent: Adopts a tank style, converting 30% Physical Attack into Max HP (Gains 10 HP for every 1 Attack).

skills.name: Sundering Strike
skills.desc: Sora strikes twice, dealing 140 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage, then lunges forward, dealing 240 (+5% Total HP) Physical Damage. He can dash after striking.

skills.name: Windstride
skills.desc: Sora leaps forward, dealing 140 (+55% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage. If he hits an enemy hero or reaches max range, he slams the ground, dealing an additional 140 (+8% Total HP) Physical Damage and slowing them by 40% for 0.5s. Successful hits reduce the cooldown by 2s.

skills.name: Shifting Skies
skills.desc:
Swipe left to ascend into Thunder for 10s. Sora gains a brief bonus Movement Speed and becomes untargetable. He then summons lightning to strike nearby enemies 3 time(s), each dealing 140 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.

Swipe right to ascend into Torrent for 10s. Sora gains Control Immunity. After a short delay, he pulls nearby enemies in and deals 250 (+8% Total HP) Physical Damage.

skills.name: Mystic Surge
skills.desc:
Sora can ascend to Thunder or Torrent by using his Ultimate. After ascending, dealing damage to heroes or summoned units grants 1 stack of Cloudstep. Upon reaching 5 stacks, he unlocks his True Art.

Thunder: Adopts an assassin style, converting 30% Max HP into Physical Attack (Equal to 10% of Max HP).
Torrent: Adopts a tank style, converting 30% Physical Attack into Max HP (Gains 10 HP for every 1 Attack).

skills.name: Sundering Strike - Thunderflash
skills.desc: Sora strikes four times, dealing 140 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage, then lunges forward, dealing 240 (+5% Total HP) Physical Damage. He can move freely during this skill.

skills.name: Windstride - Thunder Rush
skills.desc: Sora leaps forward, dealing 120 (+45% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage. Upon hitting a hero or reaching the max distance, he summons a thunderstorm, dealing 120 (+6% Total HP) Physical Damage and slowing enemies by 50% for 0.5s. This skill can be cast again within 4s if it hits an enemy.

skills.name: Heaven's Wrath
skills.desc: Sora leaps into the air and strikes a target area 16 time(s), dealing 60 (+40% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage with each hit. Sora can move freely and ignore terrain during this skill.

skills.name: Mystic Surge
skills.desc:
Sora can ascend to Thunder or Torrent by using his Ultimate. After ascending, dealing damage to heroes or summoned units grants 1 stack of Cloudstep. Upon reaching 5 stacks, he unlocks his True Art.

Thunder: Adopts an assassin style, converting 30% Max HP into Physical Attack (Equal to 10% of Max HP).
Torrent: Adopts a tank style, converting 30% Physical Attack into Max HP (Gains 10 HP for every 1 Attack).

skills.name: Sundering Strike - Overwhelm
skills.desc: Sora strikes twice, dealing 140 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and slowing enemies for 40%. He then lunges forward, dealing 240 (+5% Total HP) Physical Damage, stunning enemies for 0.5s.

skills.name: Windstride - Skyfall
skills.desc: Sora leaps forward, dealing 140 (+55% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage, knocking enemies airborne, and knocking them back to the landing point. He then smashes the ground, knocking enemies airborne again for 0.5s, dealing 140 (+8% Total HP) Physical Damage, and slowing them by 50% for 1s. Hits reduce cooldown by 2s.

skills.name: Stormbind
skills.desc: Sora leaps into the air and strikes the target area 5 time(s), dealing 100 (+6% Total HP) Physical Damage and briefly stunning enemies with each hit. Sora can move freely and ignore terrain during this skill.

strong.desc: Obsidia's Ultimate will disable dash and blink skills, making her excellent at countering heroes who rely on them,  such as Hayabusa, Freya, and Lancelot.

weak.desc: Obsidia struggles during the early laning phase, making her vulnerable to other Marksmen with strong early game, such as Bruno and Layla.

assist.desc: Obsidia usually reaches her damage potential in the mid-to-late game, so she wants support heroes who can help her in the early game, like Angela and Estes.

Obsidia.story: The ancient Sovereign of the Abyss, risen from ruin, now seeks to slay the God of Darkness and hunt down its Spawns of Decay.

skills.name: Return to Bone
skills.desc:
Obsidia gains Bone Energy whenever she deals damage. Basic Attacks grant 1 Energy against non-hero units and 2 against hero units. Skills grant Energy equal to 3 times the amount granted by Basic Attacks against the same unit type. No Energy is gained from hitting creeps.

At 30 Bone Energy, it forms a Bone Shard (up to 25 shards). Killing an enemy hero instantly grants 1 Bone Shard(s). When Obsidia uses Basic Attacks, all Bone Shards strike the target, each dealing  (+6% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage (inherits 6% Attack Effects).

Obsidia's Basic Attacks deal 70 (+70% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage (inherits 100% Attack Effects).

skills.name: Abyssal Bone Needle
skills.desc: Obsidia launches an Abyssal Bone Needle, dealing 325 (+80% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit and triggering Return to Bone on the first enemy hero struck.

skills.name: Phantom Shadowmeld
skills.desc: Obsidia descends into the Abyss and enters Shadowmeld Form, gaining 500% Movement Speed for 0.3s.

skills.name: Hunt of Bone
skills.desc:
Obsidia shoots Bones of Wrath in the target direction, dealing 125 (+60% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage and briefly stunning the first enemy hero hit, then pulls herself to the target. The Bones of Wrath tether Obsidia to the enemy, preventing her from moving more than 4 units away. While tethered, the enemy cannot use displacement skills, and their movement will drag Obsidia along. During this time, Obsidia gains 5 temporary Bone Shards and 30% Attack Speed. This effect lasts 3s.

While tethered, Obsidia can move through obstacles and use Basic Attacks while moving. Phantom Shadowmeld is enhanced, with its duration increased to 1s and cooldown reduced to 33%.

strong.desc: Zetian's Passive counters melee heroes with limited gap closers like Balmond and Zilong, allowing her to continue poking the enemy from a safe distance.

weak.desc: Zetian's Ultimate can strike down anywhere on the map, but having such a powerful Ult means a hero like Valentina can also use it against Zetian's team.

assist.desc: While Zetian already has decent survivability, when paired with supports who can protect allies like Floryn and Rafaela, Fighters with limited range will struggle even more to approach Zetian. And with the healing from these supports, Zetian can win any ranged battle of attrition.

Zetian.story: Recognized by the Heavens and cherished by her people, the Celestial Empress of the Cadia Riverlands ascends to bring peace to the mortal realm.

skills.name: Celestial Armament
skills.desc:
Phoenix Crown: When Zetian hits enemy heroes with Phoenix Strike or Phoenix Descent, she gains 1% Spell Vamp for 5s (up to 10 stacks).

Celestial Robe: The power of the Phoenix periodically protects Zetian, knocking back and stunning any enemy hero who approaches her, dealing 90(+10*Hero Level) (+50% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage. She also gains a 300(+20*Hero Level) (+50% Total Magic Power) shield for 3s. Cooldown: 120s.

skills.name: Phoenix Strike
skills.desc:
Zetian unleashes a Phoenix Wave in the target direction. If it hits an enemy, she automatically casts another wave (up to 3 times). Zetian can cast other skills during this time.

The waves deal 150 (+50% Total Magic Power), 150 (+50% Total Magic Power), and 360 (+120% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage, respectively. The 3rd wave has a larger area and pulls enemies toward the cast direction (not considered a control effect).

The Spell Vamp ratio against minions is only 65%.

skills.name: Phoenix Descent
skills.desc:
Zetian summons a Phoenix Spirit at the target location, dealing 150 (+45% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and applying a brief 50% slow effect to enemies in the area. The Phoenix Spirit hovers at the location for 3s, dealing 20 (+10% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage every 0.5s and reducing enemies' Magic Defense by 10% while revealing their surrounding area.
While the Phoenix Spirit is active, Zetian can use this skill again to command it to charge toward a designated location, dealing 150 (+45% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and applying a brief 50% slow effect upon arrival.

skills.name: Fury of the Phoenix
skills.desc:
Zetian calls upon her divine mandate and the hearts of her people, granting all allied heroes 40% Movement Speed that gradually decays over 3s, while bringing divine retribution upon all enemy heroes, dealing 400 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and stunning them for 0.8s. Zetian gains vision of all enemy heroes during this time.
Zetian can cast other skills while channeling this skill.

strong.desc: Kalea counters heroes who lack mobility like Layla and Chang'e.

weak.desc: However, Kalea cannot grab enemies with Control Immunity, so heroes like Diggie and Minotaur can effectively counter her.

assist.desc: Kalea's strong single-target CC makes her work well with heroes who have high single-target burst, like Eudora, Harley, and Clint.

Kalea.story: With her serpent tail, this proud descendant of the Great Serpent swims gracefully through the Vonetis Sea, aspiring to one day carry the full power of her heritage.

skills.name: Surge of Life
skills.desc:
After casting a skill, Kalea creates a Water Zone on the ground. When Kalea casts a skill within this zone, she will absorb the Water Zone to enhance her next three Basic Attacks with different effects:
First Basic Attack: Kalea dashes to the target, dealing  (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.
Second Basic Attack: Kalea unleashes a flurry of attacks, dealing 200 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and recovering 40 (+10% Total HP)  (+60% Total Magic Power) HP for herself and the nearby ally with the lowest HP.
Third Basic Attack: Kalea uses her tail to strike the area around her target, dealing 100 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and creating a new Water Zone. Enhanced Basic Attacks are not affected by Attack Speed.

skills.name: Wavebreaker
skills.desc: Kalea slams her fist into the ground, creating a wave that deals 200 (+5% Total HP) Physical Damage to nearby enemies and slows them by 40% for 1.5s.

skills.name: Tidal Strike
skills.desc:
Kalea charges forward and body slams the first enemy hero she encounters, knocking them airborne for 0.6s (unaffected by Resilience) while dealing 150 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.
If she collides with an obstacle or touches the edge of a Water Zone during her charge, Kalea will leap into the air. Cast the skill again to slam down at a target location, dealing 150 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and knocking enemies airborne for 0.6s (unaffected by Resilience).

skills.name: Tsunami Slam
skills.desc: Kalea grabs an enemy hero with her tail and slams them down in the Joystick's direction (drag the Joystick to change landing spot). She gains Control Immunity during this process. Upon landing, deals 300 (+80% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the target and nearby enemies.

strong.desc: Lukas excels in close-quarter combat and is a good counter to heroes with low mobility and CC, like Hanabi and Ixia.

weak.desc: Lukas needs to stay close to enemies to deal damage, which makes him vulnerable to heroes with strong burst and CC, such as Fanny, Valir, and Julian.

assist.desc: Lukas's Sacred Beast form has extended attack range and enhanced mobility, which synergizes well with heroes possessing AOE CC, such as Atlas, Tigreal, and Gatotkaca.

Lukas.story: A legendary Sacred Beast that can take the form of a rambunctious young man.

skills.name: Hero's Resolve
skills.desc:
Lukas gains 0.5 Resolve per second and an additional 2 Resolve when attacking heroes.

Lukas consumes 10 Resolve per second while in [Sacred Beast] form.

skills.name: Flash Combo
skills.desc:
Passive: Basic Attacks grant Vigor (up to 2 stacks), each stack lasting 4s. Vigor enhances Flash Combo.
Lukas consumes all Vigor to unleash Pulverize, dealing 220 (+120% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and recovering 5% of his Max HP when hitting a hero or minion.
1 Stack: Lukas briefly stuns the target and spins his tonfa hammers to deal 100 (+45% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage 3 times, pulling the enemy toward him.
2 Stacks: Lukas briefly stuns the target, then delivers 2 attacks dealing 50 (+20% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage each. Afterward, he spins his tonfa hammers, dealing 100 (+45% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage 3 times while gaining Control Immunity for the duration.

skills.name: Flash Step
skills.desc:
Lukas dashes in the target direction and turns his next Basic Attack within 4s into Flash Strike.
Flash Strike: Lukas blinks behind the enemy, dealing 60 (+125% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage. He then gains increased Attack Range, 20% Movement Speed and Attack Speed, and his Basic Attacks trigger lightning storms to deal 50 (+25% Total Physical Attack) extra Physical Damage, lasting for 4s.

skills.name: Unleash the Beast
skills.desc:
Lukas transforms into his Sacred Beast form (briefly gains Control Immunity). His base Movement Speed is increased by 30, all attributes are increased by 15%, and his skills gain new effects.
Use Again: Fires a Shockwave Blast in the target direction that will explode upon hitting an enemy hero or reaching its maximum range, dealing 450 (+155% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.
All attributes: HP, Physical Attack, Attack Speed, Physical & Magic Defense, and Attack Range.

skills.name: Hero's Resolve
skills.desc:
Lukas gains 0.5 Resolve per second and an additional 2 Resolve when attacking heroes.

Lukas consumes 10 Resolve per second while in [Sacred Beast] form.

skills.name: Flash Combo
skills.desc:
Passive: Basic Attacks grant Vigor (up to 2 stacks), each stack lasting 4s. Vigor enhances Flash Combo.
Lukas consumes all Vigor to unleash a tail swing, dealing 220 (+120% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and recovering 5% of his Max HP when hitting a hero.
1 Stack: Lukas briefly stuns the target and pounces, dealing 100 (+45% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage 3 times and knocking the enemy back.
2 Stacks: Lukas briefly stuns the target and leaps, dealing 50 (+20% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage 2 times. Afterward, he summons sacred lightning, dealing 100 (+45% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage 3 times while gaining Control Immunity for the duration.

skills.name: Flash Step
skills.desc:
Lukas dashes in the target direction and turns his next Basic Attack within 4s into Lightning Clash.

Lightning Clash: Lukas charges toward the enemy, knocking them back and dealing 60 (+125% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage. He then gains 20% Movement Speed and Attack Speed, and summons sacred lightning with Basic Attacks to deal 50 (+25% Total Physical Attack) extra Physical Damage, lasting for 4s.

skills.name: Shockwave Blast
skills.desc: The Sacred Beast fires a Shockwave Blast in the target direction (gains Control Immunity during the cast) that will explode upon hitting an enemy hero or reaching its maximum range, dealing 450 (+155% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.

strong.desc: Suyou's powerful long-range burst and pursuit abilities make it impossible for heroes without Blinks or Charges, such as Xavier and Ixia, to escape.

weak.desc: Because Suyou requires hold-casting skills, heroes with control can easily interrupt his attacks, such as Badang and Melissa.

assist.desc: Heroes that provide control (like Tigreal) make it easier for Suyou to deal massive damage; heroes that provide recovery and extra movement speed (like Rafaela) help Suyou kite better.

Suyou.story: An exorcist from Zhu'an who has inherited the power of the Immortal Nuo.

skills.name: Transient Immortal
skills.desc:
Tap-casting any skill will put Suyou in his Mortal form, granting him 40 Movement Speed and decreasing the interval between Basic Attacks.
Hold-casting any skill will put Suyou in his Immortal form, increasing the damage of Basic Attacks to 125% and granting him 20% Physical Damage Reduction. In this form, for every 20 extra Physical Attack Suyou has, he gains 1% Physical Damage Reduction (up to 40%).

skills.name: Blade Surge
skills.desc:
Tap: Suyou throws his weapon in the target direction, dealing 250 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in its path. Suyou then blinks to the weapon's location, catches it, and performs a slash in the opposite direction, dealing 375 (+150% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage.
Hold: Suyou channels the Immortal's power and charges in the target direction, dealing 250 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies along the path. When hitting an enemy hero, he stops and stuns enemies in a rectangular area in front for 0.6s. The charge distance increases with the hold time.
Suyou can be controlled while charging, but the skill cannot be interrupted.

skills.name: Soul Sever
skills.desc:
Tap: Suyou performs a sweeping attack with his weapon, dealing Physical Damage equal to 300 (+200% Extra Physical Attack) plus 15% of target's Lost HP to enemies in a fan-shaped area.
Hold: Suyou channels the power of the Immortal to perform 3 cleaves in a fan-shaped area. The first two strikes deal 250 (+80% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage each, while the final strike deals 400 (+128% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage and recovers 150 (+50% Extra Physical Attack) HP for himself.

skills.name: Evil Queller
skills.desc:
Tap: Suyou glides backward and swings his weapon in the target direction, dealing 420 (+120% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in a rectangular area and on his path backward, and reducing their Movement Speed by 30% for 1.5s.
Hold: Suyou uses the Immortal's power to shoot a deadly arrow in the target direction, dealing 580 (+165% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the target hit. The range increases with the hold time. 
Suyou can be controlled while charging, but the skill cannot be interrupted.

strong.desc: Zhuxin is a good counter to enemies with less range and low mobility, such as Popol and Kupa and Cyclops, where she has a big advantage.

weak.desc: Like most Mages, Zhuxin is countered by heroes with high mobility and burst, such as Fanny and Saber, who can quickly take her down.

assist.desc: Zhuxin can provide great damage and CC, but she can't realize her potential until later in the game, so heroes with CC can help Zhuxin get through the early period. Zhuxin also works well with sustained damage heroes like Belerick, Kimmy, and Cici to dish out a surprising amount of damage.

Zhuxin.story: A mysterious young woman who guides the ember butterflies using her Lantern of Spirits.

skills.name: Crimson Butterflies
skills.desc:
Every time Zhuxin channels Lantern Flare, she converts the Mana spent into up to 15 Crimson Butterflies to follow her Spirit Lantern. After Zhuxin stops channeling Lantern Flare, the Crimson Butterflies will gradually disappear, each restoring 3% of her Max Mana.
Zhuxin has a naturally high base Max Mana, but it doesn't scale with her level.

skills.name: Fluttering Grace
skills.desc: Zhuxin deals 150 (+30% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to all enemies in a fan-shaped area and applies 1 stack(s) of Soul Snare. Zhuxin also slows all enemy heroes hit by 20% and gains 20% of extra Movement Speed herself for 1.5s.

skills.name: Lantern Flare
skills.desc:
Hold: Hold the skill button to move the Spirit Lantern. It deals 35 (+30% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to all enemies in range every 0.33s (Magic Bonus increases to 60% against minions) and applies Soul Snare to enemy heroes hit. When an enemy hero reaches 10 stacks of Soul Snare, the Crimson Butterflies capture them, pulling them airborne for 1s.

Release: Throw all captured enemies to the target location, dealing 200 (+150% Total Magic Power) (+ 10% captured target's Max HP) Magic Damage to all enemies in the area. If an enemy is hit multiple times, subsequent damage decays by 80%.

After a target is captured, Soul Snare cannot stack on them again for 4s. During this time, this skill deals 15% extra damage to that target.

skills.name: Crimson Beacon
skills.desc: Zhuxin blinks to the target location, gaining a 400 (+100% Total Magic Power) shield and creating a field for 6s. The field deals 30 (+10% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and applies 1 stack of Soul Snare to nearby enemies every 0.5s.

strong.desc: Chip possesses strong initiation and CC abilities, making him a great counter against heroes with low mobility or those who are relatively squishy, such as Vexana, Layla, and Ixia.

weak.desc: Since enemies can also use Chip's Connecting Portals, certain heroes with strong teamfighting abilities, like Barats and Tigreal, can use them to their advantage.

assist.desc: Chip's Ultimate allows his team to group up from anywhere, so he works well with heroes who can't cross the map quickly but excel in teamfights, such as Alpha, Hanabi, and Kadita.

Chip.story: A carefree, genetically enhanced fox that wields advanced portal technology.

skills.name: Snack Time!
skills.desc: Chip starts eating potato chips once every 2s when out of combat, regenerating HP equal to 300 plus 45% of lost HP after finishing a whole bag (requires 4 bites). After eating a bag of chips, Chip will be full and cannot eat again for 5s. (The bite count will be saved if he enters combat before finishing a whole bag.)

skills.name: Crash Course
skills.desc: Chip rams his hovercraft into the ground, dealing Magic Damage equal to 100 plus 4% of the target's Max HP and applying Chip's Mark on enemy units hit. Chip gains a 300 (+60% Total Magic Power) Shield upon hitting an enemy hero, which increases by 40% for each additional enemy hero hit. Chip's next Basic Attack will become ranged and also hit all nearby enemies with Chip's Mark, detonating the mark to deal 150 (+30% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and stun each target for 0.6s.

skills.name: Overtime
skills.desc:
Chip rushes for 2s, gaining up to 65% extra Movement Speed, after which he maintains the max speed for 2s.

Chip's next Basic Attack will cause him to charge at the enemy, dealing 200 (+30% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and knocking them back slightly.

skills.name: Shortcut
skills.desc:
Chip drops the Main Portal on an enemy hero, dealing 200 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and briefly slowing them by 25%. The Main Portal deals 2 times the damage and immobilizes them for 1s after a delay. Meanwhile, he creates Connecting Portals near allied heroes 10 units away and behind the allied Base. The Main Portal and Connecting Portals last up to 15s.
Connecting Portal: Heroes (on both teams) can teleport from Connecting Portals to the Main Portal, and allied heroes gain Burst Movement Speed for 1.5s and a 500 shield.
Main Portal: The Main Portal inherits 70% of Chip's attributes. Heroes can stand on the Main Portal for 2s to return to the Connecting Portal they came from. Casting skills or launching Basic Attacks will reset the stand time.

skills.name: Why Walk?
skills.desc: Chip has 4 fixed Beacons on the map when the battle starts. Use this skill to teleport to any other Beacon. (This skill cannot be used in combat.)

strong.desc: Because of Cici's great kiting ability and her 1st Skill dealing Max HP damage, Cici counters certain Tanks like Fredrinn and Minotaur.

weak.desc: Cici is countered by heroes with targeted CC skills who can stop her dead in her tracks, such as Saber and Kaja.

assist.desc: Cici works well with heroes that have buffs and can boost survivability. Supports like Angela and Floryn can help Cici gain an early advantage by greatly amplifying her combat capabilities to end the game before it drags on too long.

Cici.story: A cheerful performer who spreads fun and wonderment wherever she goes.

skills.name: Performer's Delight
skills.desc: Cici generates a stack of Delight after dealing damage, increasing her Movement Speed by 0.75% and Spell Vamp by 0.75% per stack up to 10 times, doubling the effects at full stacks.

skills.name: Yo-Yo Blitz
skills.desc:
Cici uses Yoyo to continuously attack the nearest enemy within range (prioritizing heroes) for 3.5s, hitting them up to 10 times, and dealing Physical Damage equal to 65 plus 3 (+1.2% Extra Physical Attack)% of the target's Max HP per hit. Cici can move and use other skills during this attack.

If there is no enemy within range, Yoyo will attack Turrets or the Base instead.

This skill deals 20% extra damage to Minions.

skills.name: Buoyant Bounce
skills.desc: Cici leaps to a target location. If she lands on any unit (not including Turrets and Base), she can leap again in the Joystick's direction. If she lands on an enemy with the first leap, she deals 200 (+40% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to them.

skills.name: Curtain Call
skills.desc:
Cici throws Yoyo at the target enemy hero and links them with another nearby enemy hero, dealing 100 (+10% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage, slowing them by 30%, and pulling them together for 4s. 

During this time, Cici can use Yo-Yo Blitz to attack both linked targets at the same time. If only one target is within the range of Yo-Yo Blitz, Cici will attack the target twice, but the damage of the second attack will be reduced to 25%.

strong.desc: Nolan counters squishy heroes that lack mobility skills like Hanabi and Eudora because they have a hard time avoiding his rifts while Nolan's own mobility makes them easy targets.

weak.desc: Nolan is countered by tanky heroes with high sustained damage, such as Thamuz and Fredrinn, who can survive Nolan's burst and kill him afterwards.

assist.desc: Nolan works well with Tanks that can initiate and CC the enemy so Nolan to focus on dealing as much damage as possible, such as Tigreal and Gatotkaca.

Nolan.story: A scholar that wanders the universe with split souls to save his daughter.

skills.name: Dimensional Rift
skills.desc:
Nolan's skills will leave a Rift. When a rift comes into contact with another rift, they will activate and pull enemies to their center and deal 240 (+140% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage after a short delay. Each time a rift activation hits an enemy, Nolan will gain 20 Energy. If an enemy is hit by multiple rift activations, the damage after the first hit will be reduced by 40%.
Cosmic Leap: If Nolan does not deal or take damage for 5s, he enhances his next Basic Attack to dash to the target, dealing  (+120% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and leaving a Rift. (Damage to and from Minions or Creeps will not prevent Nolan from getting this effect.)

skills.name: Expansion
skills.desc: Nolan cuts a rectangular area in front of him with his cosmic gauge, dealing 230 (+40% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and leaving a Rift at the first enemy hit.

skills.name: Gauge
skills.desc: Nolan charges in the target direction and slashes with his cosmic gauge, dealing 70 (+13% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in his path and leaving a Rift.

skills.name: Fracture
skills.desc: Nolan slashes at the target location 3 times, dealing 120 (+50% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage with each slash while leaving 3 rifts and slowing the target hit by 30% for 0.5s. The rifts will be activated automatically while Nolan dashes backward.

strong.desc: Ixia's skills make her a nice kiter. She works well against heroes with short ranges or poor Slow abilities, such as Fredrinn and Bane.

weak.desc: Ixia lacks survivability, that's why she can be countered by heroes with high burst damage or strong control abilities such as Saber and Aamon.

assist.desc: With her Ultimate, Ixia is capable of dealing impressive sustained damage. So she works best with heroes with area CC abilities, like Atlas and Vexana.

Ixia.story: The leader of Bad Soul and self-proclaimed inventor, she wields a homemade weapon powered by a stolen Starlium reactor.

skills.name: Siphon Starlium
skills.desc:
Ixia's Basic Attacks and skills apply a stack of Starlium Charge to enemies on hit. When Ixia performs a Basic Attack on an enemy with two stacks of Starlium Charge, the attack consumes the stacks and is replaced by Siphon Starlium, dealing  (+150% Total Physical Attack) (+200 Lifesteal) +(+10*Hero Level) Physical Damage to the target. Ixia recovers HP equal to the damage dealt (only 10(+150 Lifesteal)% against non-hero units) and slows the target by 25% for 1s.
Siphon Starlium can hit all enemies in front of Ixia within her Basic Attack range, but it cannot Crit.
Ixia's Basic Attacks cannot trigger Lifesteal.

skills.name: Dual Beam
skills.desc:
Ixia fires 2 Starlium-charged energy beams along the ground in the target direction, dealing 100 (+70% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in a rectangular area. When hitting enemy heroes, Ixia gains 50% Movement Speed for 2s.
Enemies hit by both beams will take double damage.

skills.name: Star Helix
skills.desc: Ixia unleashes a canister of Starlium energy in the target direction, knocking back nearby enemies. After a short delay, it pulls enemies in range to the center line and deals 200 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.

skills.name: Full Barrage
skills.desc: Ixia reassembles her weapon into 6 smaller weapons and enters the Full Barrage state for 4s. In this state, Ixia's Basic Attacks and skills can hit enemies in the large fan-shaped area in front of her, but not outside it. Ixia's Basic Attacks can hit up to 6 enemy units (prioritizes heroes), and Siphon Starlium will fire simultaneously with her normal Basic Attacks.

strong.desc: Arlott counters Fighters with relatively lower HP, such as Sun and Zilong. Arlott can deal high burst damage and has excellent chasing and control abilities, allowing him to quickly eliminate more fragile Fighters.

weak.desc: Arlott is countered by tanky Fighters that can withstand lots of damage, like Thamuz and Fredrinn, because he lacks the burst to take them out quickly and will lose in a prolonged battle.

assist.desc: Arlott works well with Tanks who can initiate with CC, such as Gatotkaca and Atlas. Their CC will also mark enemies for Arlott, allowing him to use his 2nd Skill even more for tons of damage.

Arlott.story: A demon human hybrid wielding two lances with a thirst for vengeance against both the Church and demons.

skills.name: Demon Gaze
skills.desc:
Arlott possesses a demonic eye that will Mark enemy units near Arlott that become affected by control effects for 7s.
The eye will also automatically Mark a nearby enemy hero in vision every 7s.

skills.name: Dauntless Strike
skills.desc: Arlott swings his lance forward, dealing 200 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and briefly stunning targets in an area. Targets hit by the farther part of the AOE will be stunned for 1s instead.

skills.name: Vengeance
skills.desc:
Arlott charges at an enemy, dealing 135 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage (this skill cannot be interrupted during movement).
If Arlott's target is Marked, he gains a brief burst of Movement Speed, and this skill will deal double damage, immediately refresh its cooldown, and recover 70 (+60% Total Physical Attack) HP to Arlott (only restores 50% of that amount when used on a non-hero unit).

skills.name: Final Slash
skills.desc:
Passive: Each level-up reduces Demon Gaze's interval by 1s.
Active: Arlott cleaves forward, dealing 400 (+80% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in the area, knocking them back to the edge of the swing, and briefly revealing their location (can only be interrupted by Suppression).

strong.desc: Novaria counters Mages with shorter range and low mobility, such as Eudora and Odette, because these heroes can't reach Novaria while Novaria can hit them with her 2nd Skill.

weak.desc: Novaria is countered by Assassins with strong targeted burst damage, such as Saber and Hayabusa, because they can easily close in on Novaria and burst her down.

assist.desc: Novaria works well with other long-range heroes like Beatrix and Layla because her Ultimate makes any target a sitting duck for her team to pick off.

Novaria.story: The young girl who seeks to rebuild the Star Atlas in order to solve the secrets of the Stars.

skills.name: Star Trail
skills.desc: Astral Sphere continuously slows nearby enemies by 20% and reveals their position.

skills.name: Astral Meteor
skills.desc: Novaria summons an Astral Sphere at the target location, dealing 75 (+25% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage every 0.33s and continuously slowing enemies (up to 2.5 times). After 2s, the Astral Sphere explodes, dealing 260 (+50% Total Magic Power) (+ 4% target's Max HP) Magic Damage and slowing enemies by 2.5 times.

skills.name: Astral Recall
skills.desc:
Novaria summons an Astral Sphere at a distance and draws it toward her. She gains 20% Movement Speed and can pass through terrain. Movement Speed boost increases to 60% while passing through terrain.

Use Again: Upon catching the Astral Sphere, she can cast Star Shatter to launch it in the target direction within 5s. The sphere will explode when hitting an enemy hero, dealing 230 (+90% Total Magic Power) (+ 4% target's Max HP) Magic Damage. The damage will increase based on the Astral Sphere's travel distance (draw + launch), up to 2.5 times the original damage.

skills.name: Astral Echo
skills.desc: Novaria scatters Astral Echo in the target direction, briefly slowing enemy heroes hit by 50% and applying Astral Ring on them. This increases their hitbox by 2.5 times and reveals their position for 5s.

strong.desc: Joy is strong against heroes that lack mobility or rely on CC, like Xavier and Vexana, because she has an easier time hitting them with her 2nd Skill while being immune to their CC's.

weak.desc: Joy is countered by heroes that can restrict mobility skills, such as Minsitthar and Khufra, due to her kit's heavy reliance on her 2nd Skill.

assist.desc: Joy works best with heroes with that can provide powerful support or have strong initiation, like Angela and Hylos.

Joy.story: The "Child of Miracle" who returned to the Leonins after years of wandering.

skills.name: Humph, Joy's Angry!
skills.desc:
Joy becomes Angry! each time her skill hits a non-Minion enemy, dealing 100 (+80% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and increasing her Movement Speed by 60% that decays over 4s (hitting the Leonin Crystal will also grant the Movement Speed effect). This effect has a cooldown of 4s for each target.

Joy's Basic Attack will deal 65 (+65% Total Physical Attack)  (+65% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage.

skills.name: Look, Leonin Crystal!
skills.desc: Joy summons a Leonin Crystal at the target location, dealing 200 (+40% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies and slowing them by 30% for 1s. The Leonin Crystal lasts up to 2s.

skills.name: Meow, Rhythm of Joy!
skills.desc:
Joy dashes in the target direction, dealing 120 (+40% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in her path. Hitting an enemy or the Leonin Crystal allows Joy to cast this skill again within 0.8s and gain 1 Beat Energy. Casting this skill on the beat increases the skill damage by 100% and grants a 80 (+60% Total Magic Power) shield that lasts 3s.

This skill can be cast up to 4 times in succession.

skills.name: Ha, Electrifying Beats!
skills.desc:
Joy increases her Physical & Magic Defense by 15 (+10% Total Magic Power) and gains 30% Movement Speed. Over the next 3s, she pulses 8 times, dealing 120 (+40% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage each time to nearby enemies.
Each Meow, Rhythm of Joy! cast on the beat increases this skill's damage by 40%. If perfect rhythm is achieved by hitting every beat with Meow, Rhythm of Joy!, Joy also gains 40% Spell Vamp while this skill is active.
This skill unlocks when Joy acquires Beat Energy. Casting this skill with 3 Beat Energy will remove all debuffs on Joy and make her immune to slow effects while this skill is active.

strong.desc: Fredrinn is strong against Mage heroes who rely on skill bursts, as it is difficult for them to one-shot him with their bursts. Instead, once Fredrinn has enough Combo Points, he can use his 2nd Ultimate to easily finish them off. Such as Vale and Eudora.

weak.desc: Fredrinn is countered by Marksman heroes who can deal True Damage, as they can take him down very easily. Heroes like Kerrie and Lesley are good examples here.

assist.desc: Fredrinn works best with support heroes, who can further improve his survivability, like Angela and Floryn.

Fredrinn.story: A master treasure appraiser in Los Pecados accumulating wealth and searching for his lost friend.

skills.name: Crystalline Armor
skills.desc:
Fredrinn gains 1 Combo Point that lasts 5s each time his skill hits a non-minion enemy, up to 4 Combo Points. His Ultimates cost different numbers of Combo Points.

Fredrinn stores 100% of the damage he receives as Crystal Energy, which decays after 5s at a rate of 10% Max HP per second.
Fredrinn can convert the stored Crystal Energy into HP by 20% of his damage dealt (10% for damage dealt to minions). Gaining a Combo Point resets the decaying timer.

skills.name: Piercing Strike
skills.desc:
Fredrinn thrusts his sword in the target direction, dealing 125 (+7% Total HP) (+120% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit and slowing them by 30% for 2s. His next Basic Attack gains extra attack range and deals 85 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.
This skill deals 200% damage to non-hero enemies.

skills.name: Brave Assault
skills.desc: Fredrinn dashes in the target direction, dealing 140 (+80% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the first non-minion enemy hit. His next Basic Attack knocks the target airborne for 0.3s.

skills.name: Energy Eruption
skills.desc:
Fredrinn deals 210 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies and taunts them for 1s. Hitting a non-minion enemy grants Fredrinn 25 Physical & Magic Defense for 3s and reduces the cooldowns of Piercing Strike and Brave Assault by 75%.
This skill costs 1 Combo Point.

skills.name: Appraiser's Wrath
skills.desc:
Fredrinn slams his sword in the target direction (this skill can only be interrupted by Suppression), dealing 600 plus 21% of his Crystal Energy as Physical Damage to enemies in a fan-shaped area after a short delay. Enemies in the center of the area take 175% damage. This skill doubles the conversion ratio based on damage dealt to enemy heroes from stored Crystal Energy to HP.
This skill costs 3 Combo Points.

strong.desc: Julian is a hero who can constantly grow his own advantage, so he is strong against heroes who are weak in the early game, like Cecilion and Aldous.

weak.desc: Julian needs to hit enemy heroes with his skills to bring out his potential, so he is countered by heroes with good Mobility. Such as Joy and Harith.

assist.desc: Julian works best with powerful support heroes who can buff him, like Angela and Diggie.

Julian.story: A Raven assassin of the Church of Light, a young boy who's in search of his home…

skills.name: Smith's Legacy
skills.desc:
After casting two different skills, Julian enhances his third skill. Casting the enhanced skill makes all his skills go on a 9-6s Cooldown (decreases with his level).
After casting any skill, his Basic Attacks for the next 2s will deal 100 (+100% Total Physical Attack) (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and pull the target toward him (deals 85% damage to Creeps). Casting another skill extends the duration.
Julian gains 15% extra Hybrid Lifesteal for 5s each time he hits an enemy hero with his skills (up to 3 stacks).

skills.name: Scythe
skills.desc:
Julian hurls a flying scythe in the target direction, dealing 420 (+140% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies along the way and slowing them by 30% for 1s.
The scythe disappears upon hitting a non-minion enemy.

skills.name: Sword
skills.desc: Julian summons a flying sword and dashes in the target direction, dealing 400 (+80% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies along the way.

skills.name: Chain
skills.desc: Julian casts chains at the target location, dealing 300 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit after a short delay and immobilizing them for 1.2s.

skills.name: Smith's Legacy
skills.desc:
After casting two different skills, Julian enhances his third skill. Casting the enhanced skill makes all his skills go on a 9-6s Cooldown (decreases with his level).
After casting any skill, his Basic Attacks for the next 2s will deal 100 (+100% Total Physical Attack) (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and pull the target toward him (deals 85% damage to Creeps). Casting another skill extends the duration.
Julian gains 15% extra Hybrid Lifesteal for 5s each time he hits an enemy hero with his skills (up to 3 stacks).

skills.name: Enhanced Scythe
skills.desc:
Julian hurls enhanced scythes in the target direction, dealing 420 (+140% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies along the way and slowing them by 50% for 1s.
Upon hitting a non-minion enemy or reaching the maximum distance, the scythes will continue to fly at a lower speed, dealing 80 (+30% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage every 0.3s to nearby enemies (up to 8 times).

skills.name: Enhanced Sword
skills.desc:
Julian summons a large number of flying swords and dashes in the target direction, dealing 75 (+15% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage every 0.1s to enemies along the way (up to 9 times).
Julian's untargetable and invisible during the dash.

skills.name: Enhanced Chain
skills.desc:
Julian casts chains at the target location, dealing 40 (+10% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage every 0.2s to enemies hit and slowing them by 30%.
Enemies that are still in the area at the end of the skill duration will take 250 (+100% Total Magic Power) extra Magic Damage and be knocked airborne for 0.8s.

strong.desc: Xavier possesses short cooldowns, a soft CC, good sustainability and performs well against heroes without Blink or burst damage, such as Hanabi and Faramis.

weak.desc: Xavier is countered by heroes with high mobility that can quickly close a gap such as Benedetta, Hayabusa, and Natalia.

assist.desc: He works well alongside heroes that can buff or provide CC to give him more opportunities to deal damage, such as Carmilla and Atlas.

Xavier.story: Former member of the Knights of Light who's determined to forge a path of his own.

skills.name: Transcendence
skills.desc:
When Xavier's skills hit enemy heroes, he gains 50% Movement Speed for 1.2s (decays over time) and 1 energy charge. At 3 charges, his next skill triggers the Transcendence state.

Transcendence: In this state, Xavier's Skills 1 and 2 have their cooldowns reduced by 4s, and his Ultimate by 36s. Transcendence lasts 5s and can be extended by 1s each time his skills hit enemies.

skills.name: Infinite Extension
skills.desc:
Xavier fires a Mystic Bullet that deals 480 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies it passes through.

The Bullet's flying distance increases each time it hits an enemy or the Mystic Barrier (up to 5 times).

skills.name: Mystic Field
skills.desc:
Xavier conjures a Mystic Barrier that lasts 5s. Enemies that come into contact with the Barrier will take 220 (+65% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and be slowed by 50%, while allies will gain 50% extra Movement Speed that decays over 1.2s.
When hit by Xavier's other skills, the Barrier expands into a Mystic Field for 3s, dealing 220 (+65% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies within and immobilizing them for 0.8s.

skills.name: Dawning Light
skills.desc:
Xavier unleashes a beam of Mystic magic in the target direction, dealing 600 (+180% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to all enemies along its path.

Once cast, he also directly enters the Transcendence state and resets the state's duration.

strong.desc: Fighters like Sun and Zilong that can't get past her Ultimate.

weak.desc: Long range Mages such as Xavier and Nana.

assist.desc:
Tanks that can chain CC such as Tigreal and Minotaur,
allowing her to keep enemies linked.

Melissa.story: A girl with cursed needles who runs a tailor shop and solves problems for clients.

skills.name: Doll Buster
skills.desc:
"My tailor shop excels at fixing and 'fixing' dolls. Am I right, Muddles?"

(Melissa deals 125% damage to Minions, Creeps, and Summoned Units.)

skills.name: Falling!
skills.desc:
"Watch me slide forward and boost Attack Speed for 3 seconds— Ah, that was just boring…"

"Muddles, go that way!"
"Cuddles, come here!"

skills.name: Eyes on You!
skills.desc:
The plan's like this: Throw Muddles out to hold down the bad guys, and then… I come on stage!
"I puncture the bad guys, while Giggles hit Muddles to punish them as well! Hee, fantastic!"

(Muddles deals 175 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage on the first hit and links nearby enemies for up to 6s. Linked enemies are slowed by 60% that decays over 1.5s and can break the link by moving out of the range. Muddles will return to Melissa early when there are no enemies around and immediately if she's too far away. When Muddles is present, you can tap the Attack Minion button to directly attack Muddles, though it cannot trigger the multi-hit damage. Giggles only hits Muddles when Melissa has a target for her Basic Attack.)

skills.name: Go Away!
skills.desc:
"Cuddles, protect me! For 4 seconds, let no bad guys come close. Unless I say otherwise!"

(Upon activation, Melissa gains a 300 (+200% Extra Physical Attack) shield and knocks back enemies in the area, dealing 500 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage. Enemies attempting to enter the field are knocked back, taking 50 (+10% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and slowed briefly. Movement skills are interrupted unless the target has Control Immunity.
The field will move with Melissa once, when she's about to move out of the boundary.)

strong.desc: Yin counters squishy heroes without Blink skills like Layla and Cecilion because they will have a hard time dodging his Golden Rings. Once they are stunned or trapped in his Ultimate, Yin can easily finish them off.

weak.desc: Yin is countered by Fighters that can interrupt skills, such as Fredrinn and Badang, because they can easily interrupt his Ultimate during the initial delay. These heroes can also hold their own in a duel, making them risky targets for Yin's Ultimate.

assist.desc: Yin works best with Supports that can enhance his mobility, such as Mathilda and Rafaela to help him get to the enemy and eliminate key targets.

Yin.story: A young martial artist on a journey to defeat the Evil God that dwells within him.

skills.name: Leave It to Me
skills.desc: If there are no allied heroes within 4 units of Yin, his damage is increased to 120% and he gains 8% Spell Vamp.

skills.name: Charged Punch
skills.desc:
Yin gains 60% extra Movement Speed (decays over 3s).
Yin can launch an enhanced Basic Attack that deals 25 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage within the duration. Upon hitting an enemy with the enhanced Basic Attack, Yin will throw another charged punch forward, dealing 360 (+200% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in the path.
Successfully throwing the charged punch reduces the skill's cooldown by 35%.

skills.name: Instant Blast
skills.desc:
Yin dashes forward, leaving a Golden Ring behind while dealing 75 (+35% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies along the way. He gains 30% extra Damage Reduction for 4s if an enemy hero is hit.
After 1s, the Golden Ring will catch up with Yin, dealing 150 (+70% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies it passes through and stunning them for 1s.

skills.name: My Turn
skills.desc:
After a short delay, Yin locks onto an enemy hero (cannot lock onto pets or clones) and pulls them into a domain for up to 8s and turns into Lieh. When in the domain, the two cannot be affected by other heroes' skills.
Lieh has stronger skills and 50 extra Physical & Magic Defense that last 8s.
The domain will end early if either of the two is killed. If Lieh successfully kills the enemy, he'll recover 20% of his Max HP, leave the domain with the cooldowns of Frenzy Strike and Instant Blast reset, and continue to fight as Lieh for 8s.
If Yin fails to pull his target into the domain, the skill will enter a 3s cooldown.

skills.name: Leave It to Me
skills.desc: If there are no allied heroes within 4 units of Yin, his damage is increased to 120% and he gains 8% Spell Vamp.

skills.name: Frenzy Strike
skills.desc:
Lieh smashes an area ahead up to 9 times, each smash dealing 130 (+110% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit and slowing them by 75% for 0.2s.

Lieh is immune to control effects for the duration, while the smashing speed will gradually increase. The first half of the attacks will automatically lock onto nearby enemies, while the latter half cannot change directions.
Lieh can move or cast other skills during the latter half to cancel the skill early.

skills.name: Instant Blast
skills.desc:
Lieh strikes a flying kick forward, leaving a Golden Ring behind while dealing 75 (+35% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies along the way. Enemies hit will also be knocked back slightly. He gains 30% extra Damage Reduction for 4s if an enemy hero is hit.
After 1s, the Golden Ring will catch up with Lieh, dealing 150 (+70% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies it passes through and stunning them for 1s.

skills.name: My Turn
skills.desc:
After a short delay, Yin locks onto an enemy hero (cannot lock onto pets or clones) and pulls them into a domain for up to 8s and turns into Lieh. When in the domain, the two cannot be affected by other heroes' skills.
Lieh has stronger skills and 50 extra Physical & Magic Defense that last 8s.
The domain will end early if either of the two is killed. If Lieh successfully kills the enemy, he'll recover 20% of his Max HP, leave the domain with the cooldowns of Frenzy Strike and Instant Blast reset, and continue to fight as Lieh for 8s.
If Yin fails to pull his target into the domain, the skill will enter a 3s cooldown.

strong.desc: Floryn excels at sustained healing and counters Tanks with low damage output, such as Uranus and Lolita.

weak.desc: Floryn is countered by high mobility and burst damage Assassins, like Karina and Saber.

assist.desc: Floryn works well with tanky Fighters, such as Terizla and Thamuz, because they are easier to keep alive with heals and can peel enemies off of her.

Floryn.story: A kind-hearted girl who is determined to spread greenery throughout the world.

skills.name: Dew
skills.desc:
Dew's Lantern will permanently increase Floryn's attributes by a small amount.

After leaving combat for 5s, Floryn can share the Flower of Hope with an allied hero (30s cooldown), which will grant them a 100 (+70% Total Magic Power) shield each time they are healed by Floryn.

Floryn will never sell the Lantern.

skills.name: Sow
skills.desc: Floryn tosses an Energy Seed at the target enemy, dealing 180 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage. Healing Fruits will then spawn and bounce to nearby allied heroes, restoring their HP by 175 (+90% Total Magic Power).

skills.name: Sprout
skills.desc: Floryn casts a blob of energy ahead that explodes upon hitting an enemy, dealing 175 (+90% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies and stunning them for 0.5s (and revealing their positions briefly).

skills.name: Bloom
skills.desc: Floryn resonates with Dew's power, recovering 250 (+100% Total Magic Power) HP for all allied heroes 2 time(s) no matter where they are. This effect also increases their Shield and HP Regen effects by 12% for 2s.

strong.desc: Edith is strong against heroes that are not very agile and on the fragile side, such as Zilong and Aulus, because her skills deal enough burst damage to eliminate them after connecting with her 2nd Skill.

weak.desc: Edith is countered by Marksmen that can negate CC like Hanabi and Wanwan, since she will have a hard time locking them down.

assist.desc: Edith works best with heroes like Minotaur and Atlas that can more reliably CC a target first, allowing her to follow up with her own CC.

skills.name: Overload
skills.desc:
After each skill cast, Edith and Phylax become Overloaded for 3s, during which all Basic Attacks will trigger chain lightning, dealing extra Magic Damage equal to  (+20% Total Magic Power) plus 3(+0.2*Hero Level)% of the target's Max HP to up to 4 enemies.
Chain lightning damage against Minions is increased to 150%.

skills.name: Earth Shatter
skills.desc:
After a 0.75s delay, Edith controls Phylax to launch an earth-shattering blow in a designated direction, dealing 225 (+10% Total HP) Physical Damage to enemies hit (deals 120% damage to minions) and knocking them airborne for 0.8s.
This skill can only be interrupted by Suppression.

skills.name: Onward
skills.desc:
Edith controls Phylax to charge in the target direction, dealing 90 (+3% Total HP) Physical Damage to enemies along the way.

If Phylax hits an enemy hero during the charge, it will stop immediately and throw them over its shoulder, dealing 108 (+5% Total HP) Physical Damage.

skills.name: Primal Wrath
skills.desc:
Passive: When inside Phylax, Edith accumulates Wrath based on damage taken (calculated before damage reduction).
Active: Edith ejects herself forward from Phylax, knocking back nearby enemies and taking flight for up to 8s. During this time, she gains new skills and can perform ranged Basic Attacks, each dealing 35 (+75% Total Physical Attack) (+25% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage. She also gains 350 (+5% Total HP)-350 (+20% Total HP) extra shield, 50% - 150% extra Attack Speed, and 5% - 20% Hybrid Lifesteal based on accumulated Wrath, and converts each point of Extra Physical Defense and Extra Magic Defense into 3.5 Magic Power.

skills.name: Overload
skills.desc:
After each skill cast, Edith and Phylax become Overloaded for 3s, during which all Basic Attacks will trigger chain lightning, dealing extra Magic Damage equal to  (+20% Total Magic Power) plus 3(+0.2*Hero Level)% of the target's Max HP to up to 4 enemies.
Chain lightning damage against Minions is increased to 150%.

skills.name: Divine Retribution
skills.desc: Edith exacts Divine Retribution on a target area, dealing 170 (+40% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit and slowing them by 30%. She then continues to deal 68 (+16% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in the area every 0.5s over the next 1.5s.

skills.name: Lightning Bolt
skills.desc: Edith fires a Lightning Bolt in a designated direction, dealing 150 (+50% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to the first enemy hero hit and immobilizing them for 0.8s.

skills.name: Primal Wrath
skills.desc:
Passive: When inside Phylax, Edith accumulates Wrath based on damage taken (calculated before damage reduction).
Active: Edith ejects herself forward from Phylax, knocking back nearby enemies and taking flight for up to 8s. During this time, she gains new skills and can perform ranged Basic Attacks, each dealing 35 (+75% Total Physical Attack) (+25% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage. She also gains 350 (+5% Total HP)-350 (+20% Total HP) extra shield, 50% - 150% extra Attack Speed, and 5% - 20% Hybrid Lifesteal based on accumulated Wrath, and converts each point of Extra Physical Defense and Extra Magic Defense into 3.5 Magic Power.

strong.desc: Heroes that lack mobility or burst such as Hanabi and Faramis are suspectible to Valentina's low cooldown skills that provide soft CC and sustained damage.

weak.desc: Heroes with gap closers or high burst like Hayabusa, Natalia, and Hanzo.

assist.desc: Heroes that can buff or CC such as Carmilla and Atlas to give her more opportunities to deal damage.

Valentina.story: Former head of House Paxley who'd stop at nothing to revive her lover.

skills.name: Primal Force
skills.desc:
Valentina gains 8-50 extra EXP (scales with level) each time she deals damage to an enemy hero. Cooldown: 2s.
If the enemy hero's level is not higher than hers, 30% of the damage dealt is converted into her HP.

skills.name: Shadow Strike
skills.desc:
Valentina casts Shadow Strike in the target direction, dealing 475 (+145% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit and slowing them by 40% for 1s.
Enemy heroes hit are marked with a Shadow Sigil for 4s. Marked enemies hit by Shadow Strike again are Terrified for 1s (a target can only be terrified once every 4s).

skills.name: Arcane Shade
skills.desc:
Valentina dashes in the target direction, firing 3 Shadow Bolts at nearby enemies that deal 90 (+30% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage each. She can cast this skill again within 6s (costs Mana).

Each time a Shadow Bolt hits an enemy, the cooldown of Shadow Strike is reduced by 1s.

skills.name: I Am You
skills.desc:
Valentina slows the target enemy hero by 70% for 0.5s and gains the ability to cast their Ultimate. Casting the enemy's Ultimate turns her into the enemy hero's form and inherits their Basic Attack type for up to 12s (her regular skills remain unchanged unless the enemy's Ultimate has a Morph effect).
The stolen Ultimate's level equals Valentina's own Ultimate level, and its cooldown is 120% of the target's (but no lower than 10s).
If the target is a Physical Damage hero, Valentina gains  (+100% Total Magic Power) Physical Attack for the duration. This cannot be used on an enemy Valentina.

strong.desc: Squishy heroes with low mobility such as Layla and Hanabi.

weak.desc: Tanky heroes or those that deal damage over time such as Balmond and Fredrinn.

assist.desc: Support heroes that can provide buffs such as Rafaela and Floryn.

Aamon.story: The aloof Duke of Castle Aberleen.

skills.name: Invisible Armor
skills.desc:
Aamon enters the Camouflage state each time he hits an enemy with a skill, during which he cannot be targeted, recovers 25 (+15% Total Magic Power) plus 3% of his lost HP every 0.5s, and gains 80% extra Movement Speed that rapidly decays over 3s.
Upon leaving the Camouflage state, Aamon immediately resets his Basic Attack's cooldown and enhances his Basic Attacks within the next 3s. Each enhanced Basic Attack deals 70 (+100% Total Physical Attack) (+115% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage. The first attack after actively leaving  Camouflage is enhanced to deal 84 (+120% Total Physical Attack) (+138% Total Magic Power) damage and reduces all skill cooldowns by 1s.

skills.name: Soul Shards
skills.desc:
Passive: Aamon charges his armor through enhanced Basic Attacks or while camouflaged. At 5 charges, Aamon throws 6 shards at the target on his next attack, each shard dealing 50 (+15% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage. The shards will then scatter around the target.

Active: Aamon throws a shard at a nearby enemy, dealing 175 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage.

skills.name: Slayer Shards
skills.desc:
Aamon throws a shard forward, dealing 120 (+50% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies along the way. The shard will return to Aamon after a short delay and allow him to enter the Camouflage state.
If the shard hits a non-minion enemy, it'll slow them by 50% for 2s and return to Aamon immediately.

skills.name: Endless Shards
skills.desc:
Aamon throws all shards at a designated enemy and slows them by 30% for 1.5s. After a short delay, the shards will fly to the enemy's location again, each dealing 35 (+10% Total Magic Power)-70 (+20% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage.

The number of shards increases with Aamon's armor charges and the shards on the ground (8-25).
The damage dealt increases with the target's lost HP, to the max when the target's HP is below 30%.

strong.desc: Aulus's strong engage and sustained combat capabilities, along with his Control Immunity, makes him a good counter to squishy, low-mobility heroes like Hanabi, Miya, and Gord.

weak.desc: Since Aulus's damage relies on landing his enhanced Basic Attacks, heroes who can keep him at bay and avoid them are a tough matchup, such as Franco, Melissa, and Karina.

assist.desc: Aulus is a dive Fighter and he thrives with reliable ranged Support, such as Angela and Floryn.

Aulus.story: A Leonin warrior who inherited his predecessor's resolve, cleaving his own path with burning ferocity.

skills.name: Fighting Spirit
skills.desc:
After dealing damage, Aulus adds a stack of Fighting Spirit to his axe per second for 4s (up to 6 stacks). Each stack grants 7.5 (+0.5*Hero Level)% Attack Speed. At max Fighting Spirit stacks, Aulus's Basic Attack is enhanced and gains Attack Range.

Enhanced Basic Attack: Aulus slams his axe into the ground, dealing  (+75% Total Physical Attack) (+ 4 (+0.4*Hero Level)% target's Max HP) Physical Damage to enemies in a line.

skills.name: Aulus, Charge!
skills.desc:
Aulus starts accumulating Fighting Spirit, removes all slow effects, and gains 30% extra Movement Speed for 5s.

If Aulus does not take high-level control effects within 2s, he will enter the Enraged state and gain Control Immunity.

skills.name: The Power of Axe
skills.desc:
Aulus swings his axe, dealing 240 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in a fan-shaped area and slowing them by 30% for 2s.

Upon hitting an enemy, Aulus can cast this skill again to dash forward and smash the ground, dealing 240 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies.

skills.name: Cleaving Axe
skills.desc:
Aulus repeatedly swings his axe at nearby enemies, dealing 150 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage each time. After 4 strikes, Aulus briefly charges before unleashing a wider slash, dealing 450 (+180% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in a large area.

During this skill, Aulus gains 20% Damage Reduction and a short burst of Movement Speed with each slash.

strong.desc: Natan is strong against Tanks with low mobility such as Tigreal and Gatotkaca because he can easily avoid their control abilities with his UIltimate and get more hits in with his Basic Attacks on slower targets.

weak.desc: Natan is countered by heroes with targeted control and high burst like Saber and Eudora that can lock him down and finish him off.

assist.desc: Natan's Ultimate allows him to blink a great distance, so he works best with initiator heroes such as Johnson and Alpha because he can dive with them and unleash a lot of damage.

Natan.story: The savior who travels through time and space.

skills.name: Theory of Everything
skills.desc:
Natan gains a stack of Entanglement each time he or his Reverse-Clone hits an enemy with a skill (up to 6 stacks). Each stack lasts 2.5s and grants 7.5% Attack Speed and 5% Movement Speed.
Natan's Basic Attack projectiles will return to Natan after reaching the maximum range, dealing an extra 30 (+50% Total Physical Attack) (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies along the way (inherits a portion of Attack Effects). Hitting an enemy refreshes Entanglement's duration.

skills.name: Superposition
skills.desc: Natan shoots a dense mass of energy in the target direction, dealing 225 (+100% Total Physical Attack) (+120% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in its path.

skills.name: Interference!
skills.desc:
Natan launches a Gravitational Attractor in the target direction, dealing 150 (+50% Total Physical Attack) (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in its path and knocking them back.
The Attractor detonates upon reaching the maximum range, dealing 30 (+10% Total Physical Attack) (+12% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies and knocking them back.

skills.name: Entropy?
skills.desc:
Natan summons a Reverse-Clone that lasts 10s at 6 units away. The Reverse-Clone copies his moves and attacks in reverse and inherits 35% of his Basic Attack and skill damage.
Use Again: Natan switches places with the Reverse-Clone.

Each cast reduces other skills' remaining cooldowns by 50%. The Reverse-Clone will disappear if Natan is too far away.

strong.desc: Enemies with multiple mobility abilities can greatly reduce Phoveus's cooldowns, so he can use his enhanced Basic Attacks and skills more often when facing heroes like Wanwan, Arlott, and Chou.

weak.desc: Phoveus will struggle against ranged heroes with strong CC, such as Aurora, Valir, and Vexana.

assist.desc: Tanks with good initiation like Gatotkaca, Minotaur, and Tigreal can create opportunities for Phoveus to maximize his damage while they take some of the incoming damage.

Phoveus.story: A former soldier from the border of the Empire who took on the chains of sin to defend his people.

skills.name: Demonic Force
skills.desc:
Phoveus's next Basic Attack charges at the enemy, knocking them back, dealing extra Magic Damage equal to 4%-6% of the enemy's Max HP (scales with level), and gaining a Shield equal to 8%-12% (scales with level) of his own Max HP. This effect has a 26-14s cooldown (based on Infernal Pursuit's level).

Astaros' Gaze: Whenever an enemy hero blinks, dashes, or is displaced within 8 units, the cooldown of Demonic Force will be reduced by 20% and other skills by 0.5s.

skills.name: Demonic Impact
skills.desc:
Phoveus slams his monolith into the ground, dealing 200 (+120% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in the area and slowing them by 70% for 0.5s. If this skill hits an enemy, Phoveus's next Demonic Impact will leave behind an Astaros Eye.

Astaros Eye: Implodes after a short delay, dealing 100 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Magic Damage to the enemies.

skills.name: Dark Wave
skills.desc: Phoveus lowers his monolith and releases his demonic power in the target direction, knocking enemies airborne, dragging them along, and dealing 250 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) Magic Damage.

skills.name: Infernal Pursuit
skills.desc:
Sinner: Demonic Force, Astaros Eye, and Dark Wave can apply Sinner's Mark to the enemy hero hit.

Judgment: Phoveus leaps into the air and smashes down with his monolith at the enemy hero with Sinner's Mark, dealing 250 (+100% Total Physical Attack) (+5% target's Max HP) Physical Damage to enemies in the area while Phoveus recovers 50 (+ 6% Lost HP).

The HP recovered is reduced by 80% when hitting multiple enemies.

strong.desc: Beatrix is strong against heroes without movement abilities, as they can be easily hit by her skills, such as Hylos and Belerick.

weak.desc: Beatrix is countered by high burst damage heroes with strong single-target CC, as they can catch her with a CC and burst her down. Such as Eudora and Ling.

assist.desc: Beatrix works best with tank heroes with good CC abilities, as they can help improve the accuracy of Beatrix's skills. Like Khufra and Atlas.

skills.name: Mechanical Genius
skills.desc:
Her extraordinary talent in the field of mechanics allowed Beatrix to produce 4 weapon(s) of truly awe-inspiring firepower.
Renner Basic Attack - Longshot: Fire a powerful shot in the enemy's current direction, dealing  (+365% Total Physical Attack) - (+400% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the first target hit (this shot can be dodged, and blocked by others). Deals 75% damage to creeps.Bennett Basic Attack - Area Bombing: Bombard an area, dealing  (+230% Total Physical Attack) - (+300% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to all enemies in range and slowing them by 40% for 0.5s. Deals 75% damage to creeps.Wesker Basic Attack - Proximity Advantage: Fire 5 shots at the target, each dealing  (+178% Total Physical Attack) - (+220% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage (inherits a portion of Attack Effects). Damage dealt to targets hit multiple times will decay to 25%. Deals 75% damage to creeps.Nibiru Basic Attack - Rapid Fire: Let loose a volley of 4 shots at the target, each shot dealing  (+51% Total Physical Attack) - (+65% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage (inherits a portion of Attack Effects). Deals 75% damage to creeps.
Beatrix cannot Crit, and converts every 1% Crit Chance gained into 2 Physical Attack.
Beatrix gains Spell Vamp equal to  of her Lifesteal.

skills.name: Masterful Gunner
skills.desc:
Passive: Beatrix can carry 2 weapons at once, increasing her Physical Attack by 5.
Active: Beatrix takes 0.5s to swap her primary weapon with the secondary weapon, gaining a new Basic Attack and Ultimate.

skills.name: Tactical Reposition
skills.desc: Beatrix rolls forward while fully reloading her current weapon.

skills.name: Renner's Apathy
skills.desc:
Beatrix calmly sets up Renner on the battlefield and takes aim. When releasing the skill, she deals 700 (+280% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the first enemy hit.

Beatrix can aim for up to 8s, and she can cancel it early to reduce the cooldown by 50%.

This shot can penetrate Minions and only deals 75% damage to non-hero units.

skills.name: Need Backup
skills.desc:
Having escaped battle for 3s, Beatrix can call for her butler Morgan to provide her with a backup weapons crate.
Beatrix must then choose two from the crate to serve as her primary and secondary weapons.
Her weapon selection is interrupted if she commits any action or is controlled during this period.

skills.name: Mechanical Genius
skills.desc:
Beatrix's talent for mechanics allowed her to produce 4 weapons of truly awe-inspiring firepower.

Bennett Basic Attack - Area Bombing: Bombards an area, dealing  (+230% Total Physical Attack) - (+300% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to all enemies in range and slowing them by 40% for 0.5s ( 75% damage against creeps).

Beatrix cannot Crit. She converts every 1% Crit Chance into 2 Physical Attack.
She gains Spell Vamp equal to 100% of her Lifesteal.

skills.name: Masterful Gunner
skills.desc:
Passive: Beatrix can carry 2 weapons at once, increasing her Physical Attack by 5.
Active: Beatrix takes 0.5s to swap her primary weapon with the secondary weapon, gaining a new Basic Attack and Ultimate.

skills.name: Tactical Reposition
skills.desc: Beatrix leaps nimbly forward, and fully reloads her current weapon.

skills.name: Bennett's Rage
skills.desc:
Beatrix focuses her rage and has Bennett rain down a bombardment on the target area 5 time(s). Each bombardment slows enemies by 40% and deals 580 (+225% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage (this damage decays to 20% when hitting the same target multiple times), lasting 1s.

This skill only deals 75% damage to non-hero units.

skills.name: Need Backup
skills.desc:
Having escaped battle for 3s, Beatrix can call for her butler Morgan to provide her with a backup weapons crate.
Beatrix must then choose two from the crate to serve as her primary and secondary weapons.
Her weapon selection is interrupted if she commits any action or is controlled during this period.

skills.name: Mechanical Genius
skills.desc:
Beatrix's talent for mechanics allowed her to produce 4 weapons of truly awe-inspiring firepower.

Wesker Basic Attack - Proximity Advantage: Fires 5 shots at the target, each dealing  (+178% Total Physical Attack) - (+220% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage ( 75% damage against creeps) and inheriting a portion of Attack Effects. Damage dealt to targets hit multiple times decays to 25%.

Beatrix cannot Crit. She converts every 1% Crit Chance into 2 Physical Attack.
She gains Spell Vamp equal to 100% of her Lifesteal.

Passive: Beatrix can carry 2 weapons at once, increasing her Physical Attack by 5.
Active: Beatrix takes 0.5s to swap her primary weapon with the secondary weapon, gaining a new Basic Attack and Ultimate.

skills.name: Tactical Reposition
skills.desc: Beatrix rolls ahead sideways, and fully reloads her current weapon.

skills.name: Wesker's Elation
skills.desc:
Beatrix raises Wesker and fires off ultra bullets that deal 350 (+155% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage each time they hit an enemy (the damage of subsequent hits on the same enemy will decay to 25%).

This skill only deals 75% damage to non-hero units.

skills.name: Need Backup
skills.desc:
Having escaped battle for 3s, Beatrix can call for her butler Morgan to provide her with a backup weapons crate.
Beatrix must then choose two from the crate to serve as her primary and secondary weapons.
Her weapon selection is interrupted if she commits any action or is controlled during this period.

skills.name: Mechanical Genius
skills.desc:
Beatrix's talent for mechanics allowed her to produce 4 weapons of truly awe-inspiring firepower.

Nibiru Basic Attack - Rapid Fire: Fires a volley of 4 shots at the target, each dealing  (+51% Total Physical Attack) - (+65% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage (75% damage against creeps) and inheriting a portion of Attack Effects.

Beatrix cannot Crit. She converts every 1% Crit Chance into 2 Physical Attack.
She gains Spell Vamp equal to 100% of her Lifesteal.

skills.desc:
Passive: Beatrix can carry 2 weapons at once, increasing her Physical Attack by 5.
Active: Beatrix takes 0.5s to swap her primary weapon with the secondary weapon, gaining a new Basic Attack and Ultimate.

skills.name: Tactical Reposition
skills.desc: Beatrix slides forward, and fully reloads her current weapon.

skills.name: Nibiru's Passion
skills.desc:
Beatrix unleashes 10 volleys of bullets using Nibiru, each dealing 130 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.

This skill only deals 75% damage to non-hero units.

skills.name: Need Backup
skills.desc:
Having escaped battle for 3s, Beatrix can call for her butler Morgan to provide her with a backup weapons crate.
Beatrix must then choose two from the crate to serve as her primary and secondary weapons.
Her weapon selection is interrupted if she commits any action or is controlled during this period.

strong.desc: Gloo is strong against Mage heroes without good CC. Because he can easily attach himself to them, and take them where he wants them to go. Such as Cecilion and Chang'e.

weak.desc: Gloo is countered by Marksman heroes with good mobility. It's difficult for Gloo to attach himself to them, and he can easily be killed if he tries. Such as Karrie and Lesley.

assist.desc: Gloo works best with high burst damage heroes, as they can quickly take down the enemy hero that he brings back with his Ultimate. Like Eudora and Saber.

Gloo.story: A magical amalgamation of cute, mischievous creatures that dwells deep in the Shadow Swamp.

skills.name: Stick, Stick
skills.desc:
Gloo's skills apply a stack of Sticky to enemies on hit, decreasing their Movement Speed by 4% for 6s (up to 5 stacks).
Each Sticky stack on an attacker reduces their damage dealt to Gloo by 3%.

skills.name: Slam, Slam
skills.desc:
Gloo reaches out and slams the ground, dealing 80 (+80% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit.
A Goo is left at the end location, which explodes after 3s, dealing 300 (+12% Extra Max HP) Magic Damage to nearby enemies and immobilizing them for 1s.
Touching the Goo detonates it immediately and resets this skill's cooldown. Gloo also recovers 2.5% Max HP if the explosion hits an enemy (doubled if it hits an enemy hero).

skills.name: Spread, Spread
skills.desc:
Gloo splits into Goos, dealing 100 (+80% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in a fan-shaped area and immobilizing them for 0.5s. For the next 4s, Gloo gains 10% Movement Speed and deals 80 (+15% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage every 0.25s to enemies it touches.
If this skill hits a Goo, Gloo charges toward it, dragging enemies in its path along for a distance.

skills.name: Grab, Grab
skills.desc:
Unlocked when an enemy hero reaches max Sticky stacks. Gloo attaches itself to the target for up to 9s, recovering 25% Max HP.

While attached, Gloo's Basic Attacks deal 30 (+50% Total Magic Power)  (+6% Extra Max HP) Magic Damage, and it transfers 80% of the damage it takes (excluding Turret damage) to the host. If the host dies while Gloo is attached, this skill's cooldown is reduced to 5s.

strong.desc: Paquito is a hero with dash and high burst damage, making him strong against squishy heroes without defensive abilities, such as Gord and Layla.
