weak.desc: Paquito is an agile hero, but he is also kind of squishy, which makes him weak against heroes with strong CC abilities. Such as Franco and Kaja.

assist.desc: Paquito works best with support heroes who can buff him, like Angela and Rafaela.

Paquito.story: The hero who uses his two fists to protect hope and love.

skills.name: Champ Stance
skills.desc:
After every 3 skill casts, Paquito enters the Champ Stance, gaining an enhanced skill ignoring his current cooldowns. He gains 60% extra Movement Speed that decays over 1.8s after casting the enhanced skill.
Paquito's Basic Attacks only deal  (+85% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.

skills.name: Heavy Left Punch
skills.desc:
Paquito throws a punch in the target direction, dealing 210 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit. If this skill hits an enemy hero or Creep, Paquito will also gain a 150 (+135% Total Physical Attack) shield for 2.5s.

Champ Stance: The damage is increased to 350 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and the shield value is increased to 140%. The normal and enhanced shields can stack.

skills.name: Jab
skills.desc:
Paquito dashes in the target direction and throws a jab, dealing 200 (+165% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit. Paquito will stop and throw the jab immediately upon colliding with an enemy hero or Creep during the dash.

Champ Stance: The damage is increased to 350 (+230% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.

skills.name: Knockout Strike
skills.desc:
Paquito launches an elbow strike in the target direction, dealing 250 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit and knocking them back. He then throws a haymaker, dealing 300 (+90% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit and slowing them by 75% for 1s, as he darts backward.

Champ Stance: Instead of a haymaker, Paquito launches an uppercut after the elbow strike, dealing 400 (+120% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit and knocking them airborne for 0.5s.

strong.desc: Mathilda excels in harassment and single-target CC, making her a great counter to squishy heroes with no escape skills, like Gord and Vale.

weak.desc: Mathilda is countered by Tanks with multiple CC skills, such as Franco and Minsitthar.

assist.desc: Mathilda works well with high burst damage heroes who can use Guiding Wind and follow up her Ultimate to secure kills, such as Saber and Eudora.

Mathilda.story: A brave girl who stands up and doesn't fear power.

skills.name: Ancestral Guidance
skills.desc: Mathilda gains Ancestral Guidance while moving. When it's fully charged, her next Basic Attack will be enhanced, dealing 200 (+100% Total Physical Attack) (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to the target and granting her 80 extra Movement Speed that decays over 2.5s.

skills.name: Soul Bloom
skills.desc:
Mathilda resonates with the power of her ancestors for 4s, attracting wisps as her movement distance increases (up to 6 wisps).

At the end of the skill's duration, or when Mathilda casts this skill again, the wisps will seek and attack nearby enemies, each dealing 275 (+80% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and revealing the enemies' positions briefly. Subsequent damage dealt by wisps to the same target decays to 40%.

skills.name: Guiding Wind
skills.desc:
Mathilda leaps in the target direction and creates a field around her, gaining a 350 (+180% Total Magic Power) shield and 25% extra Movement Speed for 3s. Allied heroes who come into contact with the field will receive a 50% shield and Guiding Wind. The first allied hero that triggers Guiding Wind will be guided to Mathilda, and both of them will gain the same Movement Speed boost.

When casting this skill during Circling Eagle, Mathilda won't change her position.
Guiding Wind expires 2s after the hero leaves Mathilda's field.

skills.name: Circling Eagle
skills.desc:
Mathilda applies a Soul Mark to the target enemy hero and circles around them for up to 3.5s, during which her wisps will fly into nearby enemies, each dealing 40 (+15% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage. The Soul Mark will also slow the target by 15% and Soul Bloom will prioritize the target with Soul Mark.
Within the duration, Mathilda can select an area to dash to. At the end of the duration, she'll dash at the target with Soul Mark, dealing 320 (+120% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage while knocking enemies in her path airborne for 0.6s, transferring the Soul Mark to the first enemy hero that she knocks airborne, and reducing the skill cooldown by 40%.
Mathilda gains Control Immunity and a 400 (+160% Total Magic Power) shield for the skill duration.

strong.desc: Yve counters Tanks and Fighters with low mobility like Fredrinn, Barats, and Aulus due to her long range skills.

weak.desc: Yve's low survivability makes her a target for divers and burst damage heroes like Yin, Saber, and Karina.

assist.desc: Yve's works best with Tanks and Supports with strong CC to lockdown enemies for her Ultimate, like Minotaur, Gatotkaca, and Atlas.

Yve.story: Keeper of the equilibrium of the universe.

skills.name: Galactic Power
skills.desc: When Yve deals damage to enemy heroes with her skills, she gains Galactic Power (cooldown: 0.5s), up to 3 stacks. Each stack provides Yve with a 120(+10*Hero Level) (+50% Total Magic Power) shield for 3s.

skills.name: Void Blast
skills.desc:
Yve detonates Galactic Energy at the target location, dealing 290 (+150% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in the area.
Successfully hitting an enemy grants Yve 55% extra Movement Speed (halved if a non-hero enemy is hit) that decreases over 1s.

skills.name: Void Crystal
skills.desc:
Yve summons a Void Crystal at the target location, dealing 220 (+40% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies.

Use Again: Yve commands the Crystal to fire materialized energy in the target direction for 2.7s, dealing 75 (+10% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage every 0.45s to enemies hit and slowing them by 20%. Enemies hit multiple times are slowed by an extra 4% for each hit after the first hit (up to 40%).

skills.name: Real World Manipulation
skills.desc:
Yve creates a Starfield that lasts 15s. Within the duration, you can Tap or Swipe the screen to attack up to 20 times.
Tap: Deals 340 (+150% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in the target area.
Swipe: Deals 85 (+10% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage every 0.45s to enemies in the target area for 2.7s and slows them by 40%.
Enemies leaving or entering the Starfield will be immobilized for 0.8s (only once against the same target).
Yve cannot be displaced while the Starfield is active, and the Starfield will remain even if Yve is controlled (except for Suppression).

strong.desc: Brody is strong against heroes with poor survivability in the early game and heroes without dash, because he can easily lock on them and attack beyond their reach. Like Aamon and Yin.

weak.desc: Brody is countered by heroes with CC immunity and multiple movement abilities, because they can quickly close in on Brody, giving him no chance to stack the Abyss Mark. Such as Martis and Wanwan.

assist.desc: Brody works best with heroes who can perform lock-on attacks or heroes with area CC abilities, as they can give Brody more room to deal damage and farm better. Such as Hylos and Minsitthar.

Brody.story: Mysterious man whose right hand was corroded and who lost his memory.

skills.name: Abyss Corrosion
skills.desc:
With his Basic Attacks resonated with the Abyss Power within his body, Brody is able to move while winding up his Basic Attacks (which will be interrupted when casting skills), at the cost of having longer Basic Attack Animation and lower Attack Speed Bonus. Each Basic Attack deals  (+140% Total Physical Attack) (+45*Hero Level) Physical Damage (of which the damage scaling with level cannot be critical), increases Movement Speed by 30% (which will decay rapidly in 1.2s), and applies 1 stack of Abyss Mark on the enemy, capped at 4 stacks.
Each stack of Abyss Mark increases Brody's damage against the target by 5% and his Movement Speed by 5%.

skills.name: Abyss Impact
skills.desc:
Brody launches a shockwave in the target direction, dealing 200 (+170% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and slowing enemies by 30% for 1.2s while applying one stack of Abyss Mark.

For each enemy hit by the shockwave, they take an extra 5% slow and one extra Abyss Mark stack (capped at 45% slow and 4 stacks).

skills.name: Corrosive Strike
skills.desc:
Brody dashes to an enemy, dealing 200 (+70% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the target, stunning the target for 0.8s, and applying 1 stack of Abyss Mark on the target.
Upon hitting the target, he is able to move one more time in the movement direction, gaining 45% Movement Speed that will decay rapidly in 1.2s.

skills.name: Torn-Apart Memory
skills.desc: Brody locks on all the targets within 8 unit(s), dealing 216 (+165% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to them. If the target has an Abyss Mark, all Abyss Marks will be reset, dealing Physical Damage equal to 94 (+72% Extra Physical Attack) plus 4% of the target's lost HP for each stack the target has.

strong.desc: Barats counters Tank heroes that rely on CC skills, such as Belerick and Lolita, since the extra Resilience provided by his Passive will greatly reduce the duration of their CC.

weak.desc: Barats is countered by agile Marksmen who can deal high damage to Tanks, such as Karrie and Moskov, because Barats lacks mobility and his skills can be avoided, making these heroes very difficult to kill.

assist.desc: Barats works best with Supports who can give him Movement Speed to make up for his lack of mobility, such as Mathilda and Angela.

skills.name: Big Guy
skills.desc: Detona gains a stack of Big Guy for 12s each time it or Barats damages an enemy with their skills (up to 10 stacks). Each stack of Big Guy increases Detona's size and grants 4-10 extra Hybrid Defense (scales with the level of Detona's Welcome). At max stacks, Detona gains 20% extra Resilience, and its Basic Attacks are replaced with Trample, which deals  (+140% Total Physical Attack) (+3.5% Total HP) Physical Damage to enemies in a circular area, slows them by 40% for 0.2s, and refreshes the duration of Big Guy with each hit.

skills.name: So-Called Teamwork
skills.desc:
Detona spits contaminated oil in the target direction, dealing 60 (+7% Total HP) Physical Damage to enemies in a fan-shaped area. Barats then ignites the oil with a firecracker, dealing 120 (+180% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in the area (only 50% damage to Creeps).

The area of effect of So-Called Teamwork increases with the number of Big Guy stacks.

skills.name: Missile "Expert"
skills.desc: Barats fires two missiles at the target location, dealing 100 (+75% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and slowing enemies by 40% for 1s. The missiles expel fumes from their exhausts upon landing, dealing 100 (+75% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in a rectangular area and pushing them toward Barats.

skills.name: Detona's Welcome
skills.desc:
After a short delay, Detona charges in the target direction, dealing 120 (+70% Total Physical Attack) (+1.2% Total HP) Physical Damage to the first enemy hit, devouring and suppressing them for 1.2s, then spitting them out. If the spat‑out target hits an enemy hero or a wall, it triggers an explosion. The target and nearby enemies take an additional 180 (+105% Total Physical Attack) (+5% Total HP) Physical Damage and are stunned for 1s.

During the skill, Barats becomes immune to control effects. If the cast is canceled or the skill misses, 50% of the skill cooldown is refunded.
If Detona successfully devours an enemy, he immediately gains max stacks of Big Guy.

strong.desc: Khaleed counters heroes who lack mobility like Cyclops and Xavier since they will have difficult time avoiding his combo.

weak.desc: Khaleed is countered by heroes who have the ability to knock him back or airborne to interrupt his 2nd Skill, such as X.Borg and Chou.

assist.desc: Because Khaleed can deal a lot of AOE damage, he works well with heroes like Roger and Karina who excel at finishing off multiple low HP targets.

skills.name: Sand Walk
skills.desc: Khaleed accumulates Desert Power while moving. After Desert Power is fully charged, he will glide on sand, increasing Movement Speed by 35%, making his next Basic Attack deal 50 (+140% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the target (cannot Crit), and reducing the target's Movement Speed by 40% for 1.5s.

skills.name: Desert Tornado
skills.desc:
Khaleed whirls his scimitar, dealing 200 (+140% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in the area and pulling them toward him. This skill can be used up to 3 time(s) in a row.

This skill deals 50% damage to Minions and Creeps.

skills.name: Quicksand Guard
skills.desc:
Khaleed protects himself with the power of the quicksand, restoring 60 plus 5% of his lost HP and 10 Desert Power every 0.5s, reducing damage taken by 25% for up to 4s.

While channeling, quicksand appears under his feet, slowing the enemy by 60%.
This skill is a Focused Cast. Any other controls will interrupt this skill.

skills.name: Raging Sandstorm
skills.desc:
Khaleed rides the sandstorm and charges toward a designated location, dealing 100 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies along the way and knocking them toward his destination. Khaleed is immune to control effects during this process.

Upon reaching the destination, Khaleed will smash the ground, dealing 450 (+160% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in the area and stunning them for 1s, and fully charge his Desert Power.

strong.desc: Benedetta's 2nd Skill can effectively block enemy CC, making her a great counter to heroes with predictable CC skills, such as Jawhead and Guinevere.

weak.desc: Benedetta is countered by heroes with skills that shutdown mobility skills, such as Minsitthar and Phoveus, since all of Benedetta's skills are considered mobility.

assist.desc: Benedetta works well with strong CC initiators, like Johnson and Gatotkaca, because they allow her to maximize the effects of her Ultimate.

Benedetta.story: Female warrior defending the Barren Lands against demon invasion.

skills.name: Elapsed Daytime
skills.desc:
Hold the Basic Attack button to enter the Swordout state and charge Sword Intent. Once fully charged, release it to perform a Swordout Slash in the direction she's facing and dash forward, dealing  (+215% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies on the path (counts as skill damage and is reduced to 50% against minions and creeps).

Benedetta can only cast Swordout Slash when her Sword Intent is full and she is in the Swordout state.

Benedetta also gains Sword Intent by dealing damage with her Basic Attacks and skills.

skills.name: Phantom Slash
skills.desc:
Benedetta withdraws swiftly and leaves a shadow in front. After a short delay, her shadow slashes forward in a fan-shaped area, dealing 200 (+75% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and slowing the enemies by 60% for 0.5s.

Meanwhile, Benedetta dashes forward to slash, dealing 75 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage. (If the target is hit by her shadow, damage caused by Benedetta herself will increase to 200%.)

skills.name: An Eye for An Eye
skills.desc:
Benedetta raises her weapon to defend, gaining Control Immunity and blocking damage from any source for 0.8s. After this, she stabs in the designated direction, dealing 300 (+80% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.

If Benedetta successfully fends off any damage while defending, she will gain full Sword Intent. If she fends off any control effects, her subsequent stab stuns the target for 1.5s.

skills.name: Alecto: Final Blow
skills.desc: Benedetta clenches Alecto, dashes forward (Benedetta is untargetable during the dash), and performs a slash after a short delay, detonating the Sword Intent along the path. For 2.5s, the Sword Intent deals up to a total of 1560 (+1105% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies within and slows them by 50%.

strong.desc: Luo Yi counters heroes with low mobility such as Eudora and Gord because they are easy targets for her Sigils and Yin-Yang Reactions.

weak.desc: Luo Yi is countered by Fighters or Tanks with high HP recovery like Uranus and Fredrinn because she can't deal enough damage to finish them off quickly.

assist.desc: Luo Yi works best with Junglers who are often looking for fights around the map, such as Aamon and Karina, because she can use her Ultimate to cut down their travel time.

Luo Yi.story: Seeking to revive an ancient past, she is the sole being who has mastered the secrets of Yin and Yang.

skills.name: Duality
skills.desc:
Luo Yi's skills can create Sigils of Yin/Yang on the battlefield. Each Sigil lasts up to 6s.
Sigils of opposite attributes will trigger Yin-Yang Reaction when they are within a certain distance, dealing 300 (+25*Hero Level) (+190% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to the marked enemies, stunning them for 0.3s, and pulling them toward each other.
Each time Luo Yi applies a new Sigil to a marked enemy, she gains a 300 (+10*Hero Level) (+150% Total Magic Power) shield (up to 3 stacks) and 50% extra Movement Speed that decays over 2s.

skills.name: Dispersion
skills.desc:
Luo Yi casts the energy of Yin/Yang in the target direction, dealing 285 (+135% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to the first enemy hit and those behind them in a fan-shaped area. Enemy heroes hit are marked with a Sigil of Yin/Yang.
Luo Yi stores up to 4 Dispersion charges and gains 1 charge every 8s. She also immediately gains 1 charge each time Yin-Yang Reaction is triggered.
The state of this skill changes on each cast.

skills.name: Rotation
skills.desc:
Luo Yi summons Fire of Yang / Aqua of Yin at the target location, dealing 250 (+50% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit and slowing them by 60% for 0.5s.
Fire of Yang / Aqua of Yin then remains at the location for 6s, dealing 50 (+10% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage every 0.7s to nearby enemies. It can also trigger Yin-Yang Reaction with Sigils of opposite attributes.
The state of this skill changes on each cast.

skills.name: Diversion
skills.desc:
Passive: Luo Yi's Cooldown Reduction is increased by 6%.
Active: Luo Yi creates a Teleport Circle around herself and marks the target location (within 28 units) as its destination. After a 3s delay, allied heroes within the Circle will be teleported to the location.

strong.desc: Yu Zhong counters squishy Fighter heroes such as Aulus and Zilong, because they can't easily dodge Yu Zhong's skills and have a hard time with him in the early game.

weak.desc: Yu Zhong is countered by nimble Marksman heroes such as Lesley and Karrie, since it's hard for Yu Zhong to land his skills, making him a vulnerable and easy target.

assist.desc: Yu Zhong works well with Mages that can initiate with CC such as Vexana and Xavier. Their control effects allow Yu Zhong to land his skills more easily.

Yu Zhong.story: Yu Zhong, the reincarnation of the Black Dragon, intends to unify the Cadia Riverlands through conquest and usher in an era of prosperity.

skills.name: Cursing Touch
skills.desc: Yu Zhong applies Sha Residue to enemy heroes every time he deals damage to them. Sha Residue erupts upon reaching 5 stacks on a target. Then it begins consuming 1 stack every 0.4s to deal Physical Damage equal to 20 (+20% Extra Physical Attack) plus 2.5% of the target's lost HP to the target. Each stack consumed restores 6% of Yu Zhong's lost HP. When Sha Residue erupts, Yu Zhong also gains 30% Movement Speed for 3s.

skills.name: Dragon Tail
skills.desc: Yu Zhong infuses his power into his cloak and turns it into a weapon, which starts to attack nearby enemies after a short delay. The inner area of the cloak deals 220 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage, whereas the sharpened edge deals 275% damage and applies 2 stacks of Sha Residue.

skills.name: Soul Grip
skills.desc:
Yu Zhong unleashes the Dragon Soul, dealing 150 (+120% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to all enemies in front and slowing them by 60% for 1s. 

Upon hitting an enemy, Yu Zhong's next Basic Attack will be enhanced, dealing 108 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage. Upon hitting multiple enemies, the enhanced Basic Attack will apply multiple stacks of Sha Residue and deal up to 200% damage.

skills.name: Furious Dive
skills.desc:
Yu Zhong leaps towards the designated area with his full strength, dealing 180 (+90% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage. He is able to dash one more time in the movement direction. 
After a short delay, enemies within the area of effect will be knocked airborne for 0.6s and take 180 (+90% Extra Physical Attack) additional Physical Damage.

skills.name: Black Dragon Form
skills.desc:
After channeling for 0.6s, Yu Zhong transforms into his Black Dragon Form. For up to 7s, he cannot use any skills but can move freely and will knock back enemy heroes in his path while dealing 180 (+80% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage. He also gains 1000 (+200% Extra Physical Attack) Max HP, becomes immune to control effects, and ignores obstacles in this form.
Upon exiting the Black Dragon Form, Yu Zhong enters Dragonoid Form, gaining increased range on all of his skills for 10s.

strong.desc: Kupa is powerful in combat, especially against Marksmen with lower sustainability such as Lesley and Brody.

weak.desc:
But since Popol and Kupa don't have any Blink skills
and Popol is quite squishy, they are at the mercy of more agile Assassins like Gusion and Karina.

assist.desc: Popol and Kupa are best with Fighters that can charge into enemies' midst and ensure the survival of Popol and Kupa after controlling and killing enemies. For example, Alpha and Khaleed.

Popol and Kupa.story: Boy and an Icefield Wolf, partners who understand each other well.

skills.name: We Are Friends
skills.desc:
Kupa recovers 10% Max HP per second after not taking damage for 5s. When Kupa is killed, Popol can Pray for 3s to bring Kupa back to the battlefield. Cooldown: 30s.
Every 3 consecutive attacks from Kupa will enhance Popol's next Basic Attack to deal  (+220% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage. Kupa has 2500 (+1200% Total Physical Attack) Max HP and inherits 100% of Popol's other attributes and passive equipment effects (excluding Jungling and Roaming equipment effects).
Kupa deals an additional 120% damage against Creeps.

skills.name: Bite 'em, Kupa!
skills.desc:
Popol throws a spear at the target enemy, dealing 80 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage while simultaneously commanding Kupa to lunge at the target, dealing an additional 70 (+75% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage. Kupa will continue to attack the target for 3s.
Alpha Wolf Form: Kupa stuns the enemy for 1s and bites them 3 times in quick succession.

skills.name: Kupa, Help!
skills.desc:
Popol summons Kupa back to his side, gaining a 240 (+200% Extra Physical Attack) shield while dealing 240 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies and slowing them by 35% for 0.5s. Kupa will then assist Popol to attack nearby enemies for 3s.
Alpha Wolf Form: Kupa darts back to Popol, knocking nearby enemies airborne for 0.2s. The shield and damage are increased to 125%.

skills.name: Popol's Surprise
skills.desc:
Popol sets a trap at the target location that will detonate after a short delay once triggered, dealing 70 (+20% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to the enemy that steps on it, immobilizing them for 1s, and creating a frost zone for 4s. Enemies in the frost zone will have their Movement Speed reduced by 20%.
Popol gains a trap charge every 22s (up to 3). Up to 3 traps can exist at the same time on the battlefield, each lasting up to 60s.

skills.name: We Are Angry!
skills.desc:
Popol and Kupa enter the Enraged state for 12s, gaining 15% extra Movement Speed and increasing their Attack Speed to 1.3 times.
Kupa turns into Alpha Wolf Form, gaining 1500 extra Max HP, fully restoring its HP, while enhancing its other skills.

skills.name: We Are Friends
skills.desc:
Kupa recovers 10% Max HP per second after not taking damage for 5s. When Kupa is killed, Popol can Pray for 3s to bring Kupa back to the battlefield. Cooldown: 30s.
Every 3 consecutive attacks from Kupa will enhance Popol's next Basic Attack to deal  (+220% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage. Kupa has 2500 (+1200% Total Physical Attack) Max HP and inherits 100% of Popol's other attributes and passive equipment effects (excluding Jungling and Roaming equipment effects).
Kupa deals an additional 120% damage against Creeps.

skills.name: Bite 'em, Kupa!
skills.desc:
Popol throws a spear at the target enemy, dealing 80 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage while simultaneously commanding Kupa to lunge at the target, dealing an additional 70 (+75% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage. Kupa will continue to attack the target for 3s.
Alpha Wolf Form: Kupa stuns the enemy for 1s and bites them 3 times in quick succession.

skills.name: Kupa, Help!
skills.desc:
Popol summons Kupa back to his side, gaining a 240 (+200% Extra Physical Attack) shield while dealing 240 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies and slowing them by 35% for 0.5s. Kupa will then assist Popol to attack nearby enemies for 3s.
Alpha Wolf Form: Kupa darts back to Popol, knocking nearby enemies airborne for 0.2s. The shield and damage are increased to 125%.

skills.name: Popol's Surprise
skills.desc:
Popol sets a trap at the target location that will detonate after a short delay once triggered, dealing 70 (+20% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to the enemy that steps on it, immobilizing them for 1s, and creating a frost zone for 4s. Enemies in the frost zone will have their Movement Speed reduced by 20%.
Popol gains a trap charge every 22s (up to 3). Up to 3 traps can exist at the same time on the battlefield, each lasting up to 60s.

skills.name: We Are Angry!
skills.desc:
Popol and Kupa enter the Enraged state for 12s, gaining 15% extra Movement Speed and increasing their Attack Speed to 1.3 times.
Kupa turns into Alpha Wolf Form, gaining 1500 extra Max HP, fully restoring its HP, while enhancing its other skills.

strong.desc: Atlas counters squishy heroes without mobility skills like Vexana and Eudora because they cannot reliably escape his CC.

weak.desc: Atlas is countered by heroes who can knock him away, such as Valir and Moskov, since they can easily stop his 2nd Skill which Atlas relies on to land his other skills.

assist.desc: Atlas works well with heroes with high AOE damage like Vale and Ixia because Atlas can pull enemies together and keep them CC'd.

Atlas.story: Abysmal Sea creature who broke free from the seal.

skills.name: Frigid Breath
skills.desc:
Atlas generates Frigid Breath around him for 5s each time he casts a skill. Enemies affected by Frigid Breath will have their Movement Speed gradually reduced over 2s, after which they'll be frozen for 0.5s.

Atlas gains 20 (+0.5% Extra Max HP) extra Physical & Magic Defense when Frigid Breath is present.

skills.name: Annihilate
skills.desc:
Atlas smashes the ground and causes 3 explosions, dealing 400 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies.

Ejected State: If Atlas is ejected from his Mecha Sentry, both he and his Mecha will cast Annihilate.

skills.name: Perfect Match
skills.desc:
Atlas enters Ejected State, gaining 40% Movement Speed and immunity to Slowing Effects. In this state, the Mecha Sentry will follow the pilot at an increasing speed (can only be controlled by Suppression). Once they meet, Atlas will return to the Mecha, dealing 320 (+80% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies nearby and stunning them for 0.8s.
Ejected State: Atlas and his Mecha share the same HP bar and reduce 50% of the damage taken in this state.

skills.name: Fatal Links
skills.desc:
Atlas hurls chains around and starts to channel (can only be interrupted by Suppression), dealing 225 (+75% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemy heroes hit and slowing them by 40% for 3s.
Use this skill again while channeling: Atlas will drag the targets toward him and plunk them down on the designated location, dealing 360 (+180% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage.
Use this skill in Ejected State: The Mecha Sentry will return to Atlas immediately, but the stun effect will be replaced by 40% Slowing Effect.

strong.desc: Carmilla counters melee Fighters with low durability, such as Dyrroth and Lapu-Lapu, because she can rapidly steal their Defenses with her 1st Skill and Passive while healing herself.

weak.desc: Carmilla is countered by heroes that can ignore or remove her CC, such as Hanabi and Wanwan, since they can't be locked down by Carmilla's 2nd Skill or Ultimate.

assist.desc: Carmilla is best paired with sustained damage Mages with CC abilities like Cecilion and Aurora, as her Ultimate can greatly amplify their strengths.

Carmilla.story: The girl who became a Blood Demon for love.

skills.name: Vampire Pact
skills.desc: Each time Carmilla deals damage to an enemy hero, she steals 7-11 Physical & Magic Defense (scales with level) from them. The stolen Physical & Magic Defense can stack up to 5 times and lasts 5s. However, Carmilla can only steal defense from the same enemy hero once every 3s.

skills.name: Crimson Flower
skills.desc:
Carmilla summons three Crimson Flowers that circle around her for 5s, dealing 65 (+30% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage each time they hit an enemy and slowing them by 6% for 0.8s (the slow effect stacks up to 30%).

Carmilla recovers 45 (+21% Total Magic Power) HP each time a Crimson Flower deals damage (only recovers 30% of that amount when hitting minions).

skills.name: Bloodbath
skills.desc:
Carmilla accumulates Bloodbath Energy, gaining 70% Movement Speed that decays over 4.5s.

Use Again: Carmilla unleashes the accumulated Bloodbath Energy on the target enemy hero or Creep, dealing 150 (+120% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and stunning them for 0.6s. The damage and stun duration scale with the accumulation time (up to 100%).

skills.name: Curse of Blood
skills.desc:
Carmilla casts Curse of Blood on the target location, slowing enemies hit by 50%. After 0.8s, enemies still within the area will take 450 (+130% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage, and will be immobilized for 1s, slowed by 30% and linked for 5s.

When a linked enemy takes damage or becomes controlled, all the other linked targets take 50% of the damage and become controlled for 100% of the duration. Enemies can move away from one another to break the link.

strong.desc: Cecilion counters less mobile Fighters like Balmond and Terizla because these heroes have a hard time getting close to Cecilion while Cecilion can more easily hit them with his skills.

weak.desc: Cecilion is countered by Assassins who can quickly close the gap like Ling and Fanny because Cecilion lacks defensive tools and his normal skills cannot hit enemies in melee range.

assist.desc: Cecilion works well with Tanks with sustained CC abilities, such as Carmilla and Minotaur, since their CC can help Cecilion land his skills.

skills.name: Overflowing
skills.desc:
Cecilion gains 10 extra Max Mana and recovers 10 Mana each time his skill hits an enemy. Cooldown: 1s.

Cecilion has higher Max Mana and Mana Regen, and his skill damage scales with his Max Mana.

skills.name: Bat Impact
skills.desc:
Cecilion commands a giant bat to dive at the target location, gaining 30% extra Movement Speed for 1s, dealing 85 (+70% Total Magic Power) (+7% Total Mana) Magic Damage to enemies in its path. The bat also deals 170 (+140% Total Magic Power) (+14% Total Mana) extra Magic Damage to those near the landing point, and applies Overflowing effect once.

Each subsequent cast within 6s increases this skill's Mana cost by 1.5 times (up to 4 stacks).

skills.name: Sanguine Claws
skills.desc: Cecilion summons a pair of opposing claws at the target location that will clasp together after a short delay, pulling enemies in their paths to the center while dealing them 200 (+45% Total Magic Power) (+1.2% Total Mana) Magic Damage and immobilizing them for 1s.

skills.name: Bats Feast
skills.desc:
Cecilion unleashes his Blood Demon power, gaining 60% extra Movement Speed (decays over the duration) and Slow Immunity for 1.5s. Meanwhile, he fires 36 bolts of blood energy at nearby enemies, each dealing 35 (+8% Total Magic Power) (+0.3% Total Mana) Magic Damage to the enemy hit and slowing them by 3% (up to 30%) for 1s.

Each bolt also restores 10 plus 1% of Cecilion's lost HP for him on hit.

skills.name: Moonlit Waltz
skills.desc:
When Carmilla is nearby, Cecilion can summon her to become a Vermil Shadow around him, granting him a 520-800 shield (scales with Carmilla's level).
Carmilla can choose to leave Cecilion and charge at the target location, gaining an equal shield while dealing 520-800 Magic Damage (scales with level) to nearby enemies and slowing them by 30% for 1s.

strong.desc: Silvanna has a long attack range and powerful crowd control abilities,which makes her stronger against back-row Mages and Marksmen,such as Layla and Valir.

weak.desc:
But she can be weak against Fighters with multiple CCs
like Martis because they can easily interrupt her 2nd Skill.

assist.desc:
Pairing Silvanna with Tanks gives her sufficient space to dish out damage.
For example, Atlas and Tigreal.

Silvanna.story: Princess of the Moniyan Empire and queen regent.

skills.name: Knightess' Resolve
skills.desc:
Silvanna's Basic Attacks deal 25 (+45% Total Physical Attack) (+75% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage.
Silvanna's attacks apply a mark on enemy heroes for 5s on hit, each mark reducing their Physical & Magic Defense by 3-6 (up to 5 stacks).
Silvanna's skills and Basic Attacks deal 30% extra damage to enemy heroes with the max number of marks.

skills.name: Cometic Lance
skills.desc: Silvanna dashes forward. Upon reaching the max range or hitting a non-Minion enemy, she swings her lance, dealing 250 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies and stunning them for 1s.

skills.name: Spiral Strangling
skills.desc:
Silvanna thrusts her lance in the target direction and spins it 6 times while gaining a 350 (+150% Total Magic Power) shield. Each spin deals 165 (+45% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and slightly pulls targets toward the center. Silvanna spins 1 additional time for every 50% of extra Attack Speed she possesses.

The skill deals only 50% damage against Minions.

Spiral Strangling counts as a Basic Attack but cannot Crit.

skills.name: Imperial Justice
skills.desc:
Silvanna leaps to the target location and creates a Circle of Light upon landing, dealing 350 (+110% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in the area, slowing them by 40% for 1.5s, and restraining the closest enemy hero to the area for 3.5s. (This skill cannot be interrupted.)

Silvanna gains 100% Attack Speed and 40% Lifesteal when in the Circle.

strong.desc: Wanwan is exceptional in dashing and powerful against heroes with short ranges or heavy weight, such as Fredrinn and Luo Yi.

weak.desc: However, due to her lack of durability and short attack range, Mages with longer ranges like Gord can poke her easily. She also lacks survival abilities when taking Burst damage and can be weak against Burst Assassins like Lancelot.

assist.desc: She's best with Supporting Tanks with powerful crowd control, like Franco and Belerick. They can help Wanwan hit more Weaknesses and unlock her Ultimate.

Wanwan.story: The Agile Tiger of the Oriental Fighters and a lively young girl who wields the Crossbow of Tang.

skills.name: Tiger Pace
skills.desc:
Enemy heroes hit by Wanwan's attacks will have their weaknesses and positions exposed for 5s. When hitting an enemy's weakness, Wanwan deals 67(+3*Hero Level) extra True Damage and increases their damage taken from her subsequent attacks by 10% for 5s.
Wanwan can dash a short distance after each arrow fired from her Basic Attack. The dash speed scales with her Attack Speed.

skills.name: Swallow’s Path
skills.desc: Wanwan throws a Fire Swallow in the target direction, triggering Tiger Pace while dealing 120 (+40% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in its path. The Fire Swallow then turns into 3 rotating Swallow Daggers. After a short delay, these Swallow Daggers will fly back to Wanwan one after another, dealing 120 (+40% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies on their way back and slowing them by 30% for 1.5s. Enemies hit twice by the Swallow Daggers will be immobilized for 0.5s.

skills.name: Needles in Flowers
skills.desc: Wanwan removes all control effects on herself and triggers Tiger Pace. Meanwhile, she shoots Deadly Needles in all directions, dealing 150 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit.

skills.name: Crossbow of Tang
skills.desc:
Wanwan leaps into the air and becomes untargetable, firing arrows at the target enemy hero for 2.5s in rapid succession, dealing 60 (+35% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage per arrow. If the target is killed during this attack, Wanwan will switch to another enemy hero in her attack range, gain 30% Attack Speed, and extend the skill duration by 1s (this effect can trigger up to 3 times). Wanwan can trigger Tiger Pace upon killing an enemy and at the end of the duration.
Crossbow of Tang can only be cast on enemy heroes with all their weaknesses hit and Wanwan gains 100% Movement Speed during the duration. This damage is regarded as Basic Attack damage, and the firing rate scales with Wanwan's Attack Speed.

strong.desc: Masha works well against Marksmen with low mobility, such as Ixia and Layla, as she can easily chase them down using her Ultimate.

weak.desc: Masha is countered by Fighters with strong Regen capabilities like Cici and Thamuz, because they can outlast Masha in a prolonged fight due to her comparatively lower Regen.

assist.desc: Masha can maximize her effectiveness and damage when paired with utility Supports that provide extra Movement Speed and CC, such as Angela and Rafaela.

skills.name: Ancient Strength
skills.desc: Blessed by the Bear King, Masha has 3 HP bars and won't die until all of them are depleted. She can ignore an instance of damage and remove all Control effects on herself each time she loses an HP bar. When Masha only has two HP bars left, skills will only require 50% of their original HP Cost to cast. When Masha only has one HP bar left, she doesn't need to spend HP to cast skills.

skills.name: Wild Power
skills.desc:
Passive: Masha gains Power of the Wild, causing her Basic Attacks to deal extra Physical Damage equal to 2 (+1% Extra Physical Attack)% of the target's Max HP (up to 60 against creeps; does not affect Turrets or the Base).
Active: Masha consumes 24% HP to empower herself, gaining 50% Movement Speed for 2s (this bonus decays over time). She also increases the extra Physical Damage from Power of the Wild by 3 (+1% Extra Physical Attack)% of the target's Max HP and gains 50% Attack Speed for her next 2 Basic Attacks.

skills.name: Howl Shock
skills.desc: Masha spends 24% HP to roar at enemies, dealing 200 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and reducing their Attack Speed by 50% for 2s.

skills.name: Thunderclap
skills.desc: Masha spends 34% HP to lunge at an enemy hero (immune to control effects during the lunge), dealing 400 (+220% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage, slowing them by 25% for 2s, and knocking back nearby enemies. The cooldown of this skill will be reset each time Masha loses an HP bar.

skills.name: Life Recovery
skills.desc:
Masha gains Energy when dealing damage to enemies and extra Energy when dealing damage with Wild Power.

After leaving combat for 3s, Masha can activate this skill to spend half a full bar of Energy to recover a full HP bar. Masha is invincible while casting this skill.

strong.desc: Baxia counters heroes with multi-hit skills or those who rely on shields or HP Regen, such as Chang'e and Silvanna, thanks to his Passive.

weak.desc: Baxia is countered by agile Marksmen who deal True Damage like Karrie and Lesley because they can easily stay out of Baxia's range while dealing massive damage to him.

assist.desc: Baxia can utilize his AOE damage and slow effect to help sustained damage heroes like Thamuz and Alpha stay on their targets.

Baxia.story: The Mystic Tortoise of the Oriental Fighters, a shield-wielding, unstoppable wheel of flames.

skills.name: Baxia Mark
skills.desc:
Baxia activates the "Baxia Mark" permanently, reducing the final damage received by 15(+1*Hero Level).
At the same time, Baxia will reduce the Shield and HP Regen of enemies hit by his skills by 40% for 4s.

skills.name: Baxia-Shield Unity
skills.desc:
Baxia retracts himself into his shield and accelerates forward. When hitting an enemy unit, he will deal 300 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to it and nearby enemies, stunning the target for 0.8s and slightly knocking nearby enemies back.
During the process of accelerating forward, use this skill again to launch Baxia upward so that he can cross obstacles and enemy minions. When leaping up, if there is an enemy hero under Baxia, he will strike this hero, dealing 375 (+75% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to this hero and nearby enemies, stunning the target hit for 1.5s, and slightly knocking other nearby enemy targets back.

skills.name: Shield of Spirit
skills.desc:
Baxia throws out his shield, dealing Magic Damage equal to 140 (+120% Total Magic Power) plus 6% of the target's Max HP to the enemies along the way, marking them for 5s, and slowing them by 50% for 1s. The shield disappears upon hitting the first enemy hero or creep.
Baxia can cast the skill again in a short while. If an enemy hero or creep is hit by the skill, the skill's cooldown will be reduced to 15%.

skills.name: Tortoise’s Puissance
skills.desc:
Baxia holds his shield to the front and begins sprinting frantically, gaining 30% extra Movement Speed for 10s while leaving a lava path behind. Enemies on the path will take 40 (+30% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and be slowed by 15% for 0.5s every 0.5s.
For the duration, the Damage Reduction effect from Baxia Mark is increased to 200%.

strong.desc: Lylia counters Tank heroes without Blink skills, such as Carmilla and Hylos, who cannot easily escape Gloom's AOE range and are more susceptible to Lylia's high sustained damage.

weak.desc: Since most of Lylia's damage comes from detonating her 2nd Skill, but setting up Gloom can be quite slow, nimble Assassins like Nolan and Ling counter Lylia as she'll have a tough time hitting them.

assist.desc: Lylia excels in prolonged battles, and works well with heroes with strong CC, like Franco and Minotaur.

Lylia.story: A funny and witted little witch.

skills.name: Angry Gloom
skills.desc:
Lylia gains power from Gloom, increasing her Movement Speed by 15%. Each Gloom upgrade further increases this bonus by 5%.
Gloom gains an upgrade each time he devours Shadow Energy, up to 5 times. Higher levels increase the detonation damage of Shadow Energy.

skills.name: Magic Shockwave
skills.desc:
Lylia sends out Gloom with a shockwave, dealing 250 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in a line and slowing them by 30% for 1.5s.
If Gloom touches Shadow Energy, he will devour and detonate it.

skills.name: Shadow Energy
skills.desc:
Lylia condenses Shadow Energy at the target area, dealing 90 (+50% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit and briefly slowing them by 80%.
Shadow Energy detonates when devoured by Gloom, dealing 220 (+115% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies. Each Gloom upgrade increases the detonation damage by 20% (up to 80%).
Gloom restores 20% of Shadow Energy's recharge time upon hitting an enemy hero.
Lylia starts with the Shadow Energy skill, storing one charge every 6s.

skills.name: Black Shoes
skills.desc:
Lylia immediately returns to the Black Shoes' location from 4s ago, restoring her HP and Mana to their previous states and recovering an extra 10% Max HP.
She also gains 20% extra Movement Speed for 2s.

strong.desc: Dyrroth has good chasing abilities and good melee burst damage, he is strong against heroes with poor Mobility, such as Gord and Hanabi.

weak.desc: Being a melee hero makes Dyrroth weak against heroes with Slow and kiting abilities, such as Vexana and Belerick.

assist.desc: Dyrroth's high melee burst damage works best with heroes with single-target CC, such as Franco and Kaja.

Dyrroth.story: Once a noble prince of the Moniyan Empire, he was abducted by Alice, the Queen of the Blood Demons, and transformed into the Prince of the Abyss.

skills.name: Wrath of the Abyss
skills.desc:
When Dyrroth's Rage reaches 50%, he enhances Burst Strike and Spectre Step.

After every 2 Basic Attacks, Dyrroth releases a Circle Strike, dealing  (+150% Total Physical Attack) -  (+180% Total Physical Attack) (scales with level) Physical Damage to enemies in the circle and recovering  7% -  10% Max HP (scales with level, halved against minions). This strike does not trigger Attack Effect.
Each time he damages an enemy, the cooldown of Burst Strike and Spectre Step is reduced by 1s.

Dyrroth recovers 2% - 5% Rage per second (scales with level).

skills.name: Burst Strike
skills.desc:
Dyrroth releases a burst strike in a designated direction. Each burst deals 240 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies and slows them by 25% for 1.5s. (The damage decays against the same target and decreases to only 75% on minions.)

Abyss Enhanced: Burst Strike has a longer range, deals 140% of the original damage, and its Slowing Effect is doubled.

skills.name: Spectre Step
skills.desc:
Dyrroth dashes in the designated direction. He will stop when he has traveled the max distance or hits one enemy hero or a Creep, dealing 230 (+60% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the target and slightly knocking them back. When he uses this skill again, he will lock onto a target and release a Fatal Strike, dealing 345 (+120% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage and reducing the target's Physical Defense by 40% for 4s.
Abyss Enhanced: Fatal Strike will reach further and deal 150% of the original damage, slow the target by an extra 90% and reduce the target's Physical Defense by 60% for 4s.

skills.name: Abysm Strike
skills.desc: After a short delay, Dyrroth launches a destructive strike in the target direction (this skill can only be interrupted by Suppression), dealing 650 (+250% Extra Physical Attack) plus 20% of the target's lost HP as Physical Damage to enemies in its path, and slowing them by 55% for 0.8s. This skill deals up to 1500 damage to non-hero enemies.

strong.desc: Ling's excellent pursuit abilities and high burst damage make him strong against squishy heroes, like Layla and Hanabi.

weak.desc: Ling is countered by heroes with strong single-target CC and high burst damage, as they can easily control Ling and burst him down. Such as Eudora and Jawhead.

assist.desc: Ling works best with support heroes with CC abilities, that make Ling more flexible in different situations. Such as Angela and Khufra.

Ling.story: A graceful, free-soaring Finch and the Oriental Fighter who inherited the Vermilion Bird's power.

skills.name: Cloud Walker
skills.desc:
Ling's superb Lightness Skill allows him to leap among walls. He gains 4 extra Lightness Points per second when he's on a wall and 5 extra Lightness Points each time he deals damage.

Ling gains 1.6 times Crit Chance from all sources but only has 140% Crit Damage.

skills.name: Finch Poise
skills.desc:
Passive: Ling's Crit Chance is increased by 2.5%.
Active: Ling leaps onto the designated wall and enters the Half-Stealth state, restoring Lightness Points faster and gaining 30% Movement Speed. Taking damage causes him to leave Half-Stealth. Being controlled makes him fall to the ground and slows him by 30% for 2s. When he uses this skill to jump from one wall to another, it does not trigger cooldown and refreshes Half-Stealth.

skills.name: Defiant Sword
skills.desc:
Ling charges in the target direction and stabs nearby enemies, dealing them 300 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.
If Ling casts this skill when he's on the wall (no energy cost), he'll dash to the target location on the ground, dealing 300 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in the area and slowing them by 30% for 1.5s. 
Defiant Sword is regarded as Basic Attack.

skills.name: Tempest of Blades
skills.desc:
Ling leaps into the air, becoming invincible and gaining 10% extra Movement Speed for 1.5s. He ignores obstacles when in the air. He then lands on the ground, dealing 250 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in the area and slowing them by 40% for 1.5s, knocking those in the center airborne for 1s, and creating a Sword Field for 8s.

Four Tempest of Blades will also appear on the edge of the Sword Field. Ling can touch them to reduce the cooldown of Finch Poise by 4s, reset the cooldown of Defiant Sword, and gain 25 Lightness Points.

strong.desc: X.Borg counters Fighters that lack mobility, such as Terizla and Aulus. His 1st Skill has a large range and can be cast while moving, making it easy for him to kite.

weak.desc: X.Borg is countered by Fighters with burst damage like Martis and Dyrroth. They can quickly burst down X.Borg when he is Armorless.

assist.desc: X.Borg pairs well with heroes who excel in burst damage, such as Karina and Martis. X.Borg's AOE damage will leave enemies low enough for his teammate to finishing off.

X.Borg.story: An uncontrollable flame that's determined to take revenge against Eruditio.

skills.name: Firaga Armor
skills.desc:
Firaga Armor inherits 160% of X.Borg's Max HP and absorbs all incoming damage when it's active. When destroyed, X.Borg rolls in the direction of the Joystick (he is invincible during this process). The Armor's energy will gradually regenerate while X.Borg is in the Armorless State. At full energy, X.Borg re-equips the Armor and restores it to 30% HP.
X.Borg's attacks cause enemies to enter Overheated state. Enemies in Overheated state will drop Firaga Supplies when they take damage from X.Borg. Picking up supplies restores 10% of the Armor's HP (or 10 energy while in the Armorless State).
X.Borg's skills deal True Damage to enemies in Overheated state.

skills.name: Fire Missiles
skills.desc:
X.Borg sprays fire, dealing 25 (+60% Total Physical Attack) (+40% Total Magic Power) Physical Damage 7 times over 2s to enemies in front of him (75% damage to minions). 

Armorless State: The attack area becomes narrower but longer, while the damage is reduced to 60%.

skills.name: Fire Stake
skills.desc:
X.Borg fires 5 Fire Stakes forward and recalls them after 1.7s. Each stake deals 50 (+20% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and pulls enemies and Firaga Supplies along its path back to him.

Armorless State: X.Borg adjusts the Fire Stake launcher, making the stakes travel farther and closer together.

skills.name: Last Insanity
skills.desc:
X.Borg charges in the target direction while spinning and spraying fire (cannot pick up Firaga Supplies during this time), dealing 90 (+60% Extra Physical Attack) (+45% Total Magic Power) Physical Damage 12 times and slowing enemies hit by 25%. He stops upon touching an enemy hero and will slow them by an additional 40%. After 3s, his mecha suit detonates, dealing 300 plus 15% of the target's Max HP as True Damage. If his armor is destroyed, it will immediately explode but damage drops to 50%. The detonation destroys his Armor, removes all debuffs, and collects all nearby Firaga Supplies. Cannot be used in Armorless State.

Use Again: Immediately stop spraying fire and detonate early.

strong.desc: Terizla is strong against light melee fighters cause they'll have a hard time surviving his heavy skill damage, such as Zilong and Sun.

weak.desc: Terizla is countered by heroes with good Mobility or long attack range, because it will be diffcult to hit them with his skills. Such as Arlott and Lesley.

assist.desc: Terizla works best with heroes who have strong initiation skills that allow him to deal more damage with his skills, heroes like Gatotkaca and Atlas are good examples here.

Terizla.story: Once an eccentric master smith, but now an avenger siding with the Abyss.

skills.name: Body of Smith
skills.desc:
Terizla gains 1% extra Damage Reduction (up to 20 (+1*Hero Level)%) for every 2.5% HP lost.
He cannot gain extra Attack Speed and will convert every 1% extra Attack Speed he's supposed to receive into 1 extra Physical Attack.

skills.name: Revenge Strike
skills.desc: Terizla cracks the ground with his hammer and the fissure will spread out, dealing 300 (+80% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage. After the fissure hits the first target, it will drill into the target and slow them by 15%. At the same time, Terizla's Movement Speed will be increased by 15% for 3.5s. The fissure on target will then explode, dealing 300 (+80% Extra Physical Attack) plus 20% of the enemy's lost HP Physical Damage.

skills.name: Execution Strike
skills.desc:
Terizla swings his hammer in a fan-shaped area ahead. This skill can be cast up to 3 times. The first two attacks deal 90 (+135% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage each, while the third deals 225 (+220% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage. Enemies hit will also be slowed by 20% for 1.25s (the Slowing Effect can stack).
Deals only 80% damage to Minions.

skills.name: Penalty Zone
skills.desc: Terizla jumps to the designated area, dealing 300 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and knocking enemies airborne for 0.6s. He then summons Scaffold and slows enemies by 25%. Scaffold will send out a hook at enemy heroes in range and pull them several times while dealing 150 (+30% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage each time.

strong.desc: Esmeralda works well against heroes with shields, like Akai and Lolita, because her skills not only ignore their shields, but she also converts them into her own.

weak.desc: Esmeralda is countered by highly mobile Marksmen, such as Karrie and Lesley, because she lacks the burst damage to finish them off quickly and can't keep up with their mobility.

assist.desc: Esmeralda works best with heroes that can pull enemies together like Atlas and Tigreal to allow her to hit more targets with her Ultimate.

skills.name: Starmoon Casket
skills.desc:
Esmeralda manipulates Stardust and Frostmoon: Stardust deals Physical Damage while Frostmoon deals Magic Damage and gives the target a shield equal to 135% of Frostmoon's damage dealt. Each Basic Attack deals  (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and 100 (+75% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage.
The damage dealt by Esmeralda ignores all shield effects and gradually converts her shield into HP.

skills.name: Frostmoon Shield
skills.desc: Esmeralda gains 400 (+150% Total Magic Power) Shield and 40% Movement Speed which rapidly decays over its duration for 4s. Meanwhile, she gradually transforms the shields of her nearby enemies into her own shield, capped at 50% of her Max HP.

skills.name: Stardust Dance
skills.desc: Esmeralda waves Stardust and Frostmoon, dealing 240 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and 300 (+120% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies. Each time she deals damage to a hero, the cooldown of Frostmoon Shield is reduced by 1.5s (damaging non-hero units grants a 0.5s reduction) and the enemy's Movement Speed is reduced by 10% for 1.5s.

skills.name: Falling Starmoon
skills.desc:
Esmeralda coheres the power of Astrospace into her two weapons - Stardust and Frostmoon. The longer this skill is channeled, the longer the cast range.

Use Again: Esmeralda casts Stardust at the target location, dealing 350 (+90% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage, and then blinks to the target location and casts Frostmoon, dealing 350 (+140% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and immobilizing enemies in the area for 1s.

strong.desc: Guinevere is strong against heroes with poor sustainability or who are simply weak in the early game. This is because Guinevere can easily deliver her poking combo and escape. Heroes like Zilong and Silvanna are good examples here.

weak.desc: But Guinevere is countered by heroes with CC immunities or strong CC skills. Since she needs to chain her CC to unleash her full damage potential, such as Badang and Chou.

assist.desc: Guinevere works best with heroes with good single-target CC that will give her a better environment to farm and deal damage, such as Franco and Hylos.

Guinevere.story: Aristocratic girl with a strange temperament, loved by her elder brother Lancelot.

skills.name: Super Magic
skills.desc:
Guinevere deals 25% extra damage to airborne enemies and deals  (+80% Total Physical Attack) (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage with her Basic Attacks. 
Guinevere builds Super Magic each time she deals damage. Once fully charged, her next Basic Attack becomes guided, dealing 80 (+100% Total Physical Attack) (+90% Total Magic Power) Magic damage on hit and restoring 80 plus 8% of her lost HP.
Enemies hit by Guinevere's skills or enhanced Basic Attack will be marked for 5s. Those with 3 marks will be knocked airborne when hit by her Violet Requiem.

skills.name: Energy Wave
skills.desc:
Guinevere releases an energy orb in the target direction, dealing 300 (+125% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and slowing enemies by 25% for 1.2s.
Successfully hitting an enemy reduces all her skill cooldowns by 1s.

skills.name: Spatial Migration
skills.desc:
Guinevere leaps to the target location, dealing 250 (+80% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage. Enemy heroes and Creeps hit will be knocked airborne for 1s and take 125 (+50% Total Magic Power) additional Magic Damage.
Use Again: Guinevere blinks toward the target direction and leaves an illusion behind. If the illusion takes damage by hero, it restores Super Magic for Guinevere and explodes after 1.5s, dealing 100 (+30% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies.

skills.name: Violet Requiem
skills.desc:
Guinevere creates a force field around her, gaining Control Immunity while attacking nearby enemies 11 times within 2s, dealing 880 (+385% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage in total.
Airborne enemies hit will be knocked airborne again, up to 8 additional times.

strong.desc: Granger counters squishy heroes who lack mobility, such as Chang'e and Cecilion, who have a hard time dodging his 1st Skill and are vulnerable to the high damage of his Ultimate.

weak.desc: Granger is countered by heroes with strong single target killing power. Despite having good mobility, Granger lacks other defensive measures. Heroes like Saber and Hayabusa can quickly get the jump on Granger and finish him off.

assist.desc: Granger pairs well with heroes that have strong CC abilities, such as Franco and Kaja, who can hold enemies in place for Granger's entire 1st Skill.

Granger.story: A mysterious demon hunter that operates outside the conventions of the Church of Light. Always seen with a violin case in hand, he hunts down demons with his two soul-devouring weapons.

skills.name: Capriccio
skills.desc:
Granger's unique Demon Hunting Gun and Cannon are powered by Demonic Energy. Both his weapons start with 800 max Energy. His Gun restores 8% Energy per second while his Cannon restores 2% Energy per second. Killing a Minion / Creep permanently increases their max Energy by 6 / 6, and instantly restores their Energy by 5% / 5%, respectively. Each kill or assist on an enemy hero permanently increases both weapons' max Energy by 12 and instantly restores 10% Energy.

Every 1% Cooldown Reduction converts to 2 Physical Attack. Demonic Energy costs are not affected by Energy Cost Reduction.

skills.name: Rhapsody
skills.desc:
Granger rapidly fires 6 shot(s) with his Gun, with each shot dealing 60 (+45% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and consuming 70 Gun Energy. The last shot is guaranteed to Crit. While firing, Granger's Movement Speed is increased by 10%.

Granger stops firing when he runs out of Energy, or when the skill is cast again. Rhapsody has no cooldown and can be cast once his Gun Energy is over 420.

skills.name: Rondo
skills.desc:
Granger uses his Demon Hunter agility to dash in the target direction, instantly restoring 15% Energy for his Gun.

Rondo can be cast while channeling Rhapsody. Each time Rhapsody hits an enemy, Rondo's Cooldown is reduced by 0.5s.

skills.name: Death Sonata
skills.desc:
Granger fires his Cannon in the target direction, dealing 150 (+110% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the first enemy hero hit and other targets in the blast radius. Targets hit will be briefly slowed. Granger can roll in the Joystick's direction after each shot.
Death Sonata can be cast while firing Rhapsody. Death Sonata has a brief cooldown and consumes 250 of Granger's Cannon Energy per shot.

strong.desc: Khufra's 2nd Skill can be used to interrupt movement abilities, making him strong against heroes who fight with movement abilities, such as Joy and Lancelot.

weak.desc: Khufra is countered by resilient tank heroes, as his CC abilities pose no threat to these heroes. Heroes like Fredrinn and Gloo are good examples here.

assist.desc: Khufra works best with heroes with strong CC abilities that make it easier for his 1st Skill to hit, such as Eudora and Pharsa.

skills.name: Spell Curse
skills.desc:
Khufra activates the Spell Curse left by Esmeralda every 8s, enhancing his next Basic Attack. The enhanced Basic Attack gains extra attack range and deals Magic Damage equal to  (+120% Total Physical Attack) plus 6% of the target's Max HP. He also slows the target by 30% for 1.5s and heals 8% of his Max HP on hit.
Each time Khufra applies a control effect to an enemy hero with his skill, the cooldown of Spell Curse is reduced by 4s.

skills.name: Tyrant's Revenge
skills.desc: Khufra pulls the bandage on his arms to launch himself in the specified direction, dealing Physical Damage equal to 50 plus 7 (+1.5% Extra Physical Attack)% of his Max HP to all enemy units on the path. When blinking to the furthest distance or encountering a first enemy hero, Khufra will immediately stop, dealing Physical Damage equal to 50 plus 7 (+1.5% Extra Physical Attack)% of his Max HP to enemies nearby and then knocking them in the air for up to 1s.

skills.name: Bouncing Ball
skills.desc:
Khufra uses bandage to wrap himself into a magic bouncing ball, increasing his own Physical & Magic Defense by 30%. Enemies trying to use blink skills to move across Khufra will be knocked airborne. 
Each time the magic bouncing ball hits the ground, it will deal Magic Damage equal to 50 plus 3 (+2% Extra Physical Attack)% of his Max HP to enemies nearby and slow them by 80% for 0.2s.

skills.name: Tyrant's Rage
skills.desc: Khufra pushes back all enemy targets around him toward his front (this skill can only be interrupted by Suppression), dealing 300 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and slowing them down for 1.25s. If the enemies are knocked against walls, extra Physical Damage equal to 150% of this skill's damage will be inflicted upon them, and they will be stunned, instead of slowed.

strong.desc: Badang counters squishy heroes with low mobility like Layla and Odette who have a hard time escaping from his 2nd Skill into Ultimate combo.

weak.desc: Badang is countered by agile heroes such as Wanwan and Ling who can dodge or escape his 2nd Skill, greatly diminishing his damage and CC.

assist.desc: Badang works best with heroes that can pull enemies together to make it easier for him to land his 2nd Skill and Ultimate combo, such as Tigreal and Luo Yi.

Badang.story: Island hero who gained supernatural power after defeating the water monster.

skills.name: Chivalry Fist
skills.desc:
Every time Badang's Basic Attack hits a target, he gains a stack of Chivalry Fist. After gaining 4 stacks, his next Basic Attack deals 50 (+30% Total Physical Attack) extra Physical Damage to enemies hit and knocks them back. Enemies that are knocked into obstacles will be stunned for 0.6s (Minions and Creeps are stunned directly).
Fist Wind generated by Badang's skills will trigger this effect directly, and Fist Wind from his Ultimate increases the stun duration to 1s.

skills.name: Fist Wind
skills.desc:
Badang unleashes a gust of Fist Wind in the target direction, dealing 125 (+90% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit, slowing them by 30% for 1.5s, and knocking them back. The Fist Wind will explode upon hitting an obstacle, dealing 100 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies. Fist Wind can trigger attack effects and grants Badang 1 Chivalry Fist stack upon cast.
Badang stores up to 2 Fist Wind charges and restores 1 charge every 10.4s.

skills.name: Fist Break
skills.desc:
Badang dashes in the target direction, dealing 230 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in his path while gaining a 350 (+150% Total Physical Attack) shield that lasts 5s. He stops immediately upon hitting an enemy hero, slightly knocking them back while creating an obstacle that lasts 4s behind them. Enemies hit by the obstacle are dealt 130 (+30% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.

Use Again: Badang removes the obstacle he created.

skills.name: Fist Crack
skills.desc:
Badang throws a flurry of punches in the target direction, dealing 60 (+54% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit multiple times (can trigger attack effects). The Fist Wind generated by the punches will explode upon hitting an obstacle, dealing 30 (+27% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies.
Badang is immune to control effects for the duration.

strong.desc: Faramis' ability to temporarily prevent teammates from being killed makes him a good pick against heroes that go all-in to burst down a target, like Saber and Eudora.

weak.desc: Faramis is countered by high mobility heroes that are difficult for him to catch with his 1st Skill, such as Novaria and Pharsa.

assist.desc: Faramis works well with heroes that are designed to dive into the enemy, such as Leomord and Martis, as he'll be able to maximize the life-saving effect of his Ultimate.

Faramis.story: A necromancer in Necrokeep who's determined to find a cure for the Mist.

skills.name: Vicious Retrieval
skills.desc: Every 4s, Faramis' next skill against enemy heroes and summoned objects generates a soul fragment on the spot. Faramis can absorb the soul fragment to restore 100 (+50% Total Magic Power) HP and gain 2 extra Magic Power (up to 40 stacks). Upon death, he consumes the absorbed soul fragments to reduce the respawn timer, each reducing the timer by 3% (up to 90%). Enemies that die around Faramis also have a chance to drop soul fragments.

skills.name: Shadow Stampede
skills.desc:
Faramis enters the Shadow state for 3s, gaining 70% Movement Speed, 25 Physical & Magic Defense, and 20% Cooldown Reduction of this skill. He also gains the ability to move through terrain. Enemy heroes he touches take 250 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and are marked with a Nether Mark. Upon leaving the Shadow state, Faramis pulls all the marked enemy heroes toward him, dealing 250 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage. 
Use Again: Ends the Shadow state early to pull marked enemy heroes toward him.
Soul Fragment absorption range is increased while this skill is active.

skills.name: Ghost Bursters
skills.desc: Faramis gathers Nether energy in a fan-shaped area ahead, dealing 330 (+144% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies within. The energy will then split and bounce to nearby enemies, dealing 200 (+86% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage (splits up to 2 time(s) on enemy hero hits and 1 time(s) on non-enemy hero hits).

skills.name: Nether Realm
skills.desc: Faramis turns his surroundings into the Nether Realm for 6s. Allied heroes within (including himself) enter the Specter state, gaining 400 (+ 10 (+5% Total Magic Power)%) extra HP and 50% extra Movement Speed for 1s. When the Specter state breaks, heroes within clear all debuffs and enter a brief Resurrection state for 1.3s. The Specter state immediately ends if the allied hero leaves the Nether Realm.

strong.desc: Kadita excels at countering heroes that lack mobility, such as Aurora and Eudora, because it's difficult for them to escape Kadita's 2nd Skill, and knocking them airborne sets them up for her Ultimate.

weak.desc: Kadita is countered by heroes with long range and high mobility, such as Clint and Beatrix, since it's difficult for Kadita to close in on them or hit them with her 2nd Skill.

assist.desc: Kadita works well with heroes who possess strong CC abilities, such as Atlas and Tigreal, as their CC will make it easier for Kadita to hit more enemies with her Ultimate.

skills.name: Thalassophobia
skills.desc:
Kadita gains the Blessing of the Ocean every 30s. Taking damage from an enemy hero triggers the Blessing for 4s.

When the duration ends, she recovers 65% of the HP lost during this time (damage from minions and creeps does not trigger this effect).

skills.name: Ocean Ode
skills.desc:
Kadita summons an ocean wave to carry herself in the target direction and back, dealing 250 (+90% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit and slowing them by 30%. While inside the wave, she gains Control Immunity and 50% Damage Reduction. This skill can pass through obstacles and terrain.

Use Again: Kadita exits the wave without interrupting the wave's movement.

skills.name: Breath of the Ocean
skills.desc:
After a short delay, Kadita summons an ocean wave to erupt at the target location, dealing 350 (+140% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit and knocking them airborne for 1s.

Kadita can cast this skill during Ocean Ode to summon an ocean wave beneath her with a shorter eruption delay.

skills.name: Rough Waves
skills.desc:
Kadita dives into the ground and sends ocean waves in all directions (this skill can only be interrupted by Suppression), each dealing 270 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit and slowing them by 30%. Enemies hit by multiple waves take increased damage. After a short period, the waves return to Kadita, each dealing 180 (+40% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit.

When underground, Kadita becomes untargetable and gains 60% Movement Speed.

strong.desc: Minsitthar is strong against heroes that rely on mobility skills since his Ultimate disables them. Minsitthar can instantly negate the advantages of heroes like Lancelot, Benedetta and Arlott.

weak.desc: Minsitthar is countered by heroes with high Movement Speed, such as Zilong and Aulus, who can easily dodge his 1st Skill and run out of his Ultimate.

assist.desc: Minsitthar works best with high damage Marksmen, such as Melissa and Hanabi, because he can create more opportunities for them to deal damage.

Minsitthar.story: A young king that led the people of Mahar Pura to victory against invaders.

skills.name: Mark of the King
skills.desc: Minsitthar's Basic Attacks and skills apply a stack of Mark of the King, up to 5 stacks. Upon reaching max stacks, the next attack detonates all Mark of the King stacks, dealing Physical Damage equal to 400 (+6% Total HP) of Minsitthar's Max HP and stunning the target for 0.8s while healing Minsitthar for 400 (+6% Total HP) HP. Can only trigger once every 6s on the same target.

skills.name: Spear of Glory
skills.desc: Minsitthar thrusts forward with the Spear of Glory, dealing 175 (+40% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and slowing enemies hit by 30%. Enemy heroes hit by the tip of the spear will be stunned for 0.6s. After a short delay, Minsitthar pulls back the spear, dealing 175 (+40% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to all enemies along the way and dragging enemy heroes hit to him.

skills.name: Shield Assault
skills.desc: Minsitthar awakens the power in his shield and releases it, dealing 100 (+45% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to all enemies in a rectangular area and slowing them by 80% (decaying rapidly over 2s). Meanwhile, he enters Phalanx state for 3s, reducing damage taken from the front by 25% and enhancing his Basic Attack to swiftly strike an area in front of him for 100 (+45% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage per hit.

skills.name: King's Calling
skills.desc:
Minsitthar charges forward and calls 4 Royal Guards to form a field around him. The Royal Guards cannot move but will attack enemies in the field and deal  (+40% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to them.
Enemies in the field are slowed by 30% and cannot use movement skills.

strong.desc: Harith is a very agile hero and his Ultimate reduces the duration of control effects, so he is excellent at countering heroes who rely on CC and have relatively long casting animations, like Belerick and Johnson.

weak.desc: Harith is countered by heroes who can quickly escape from his Ultimate, such as Novaria and Pharsa.

assist.desc: Harith pairs well with heroes who have AOE CC and can make it harder for enemies to escape his Ultimate's range, such as Minsitthar and Minotaur.

Harith.story: Leonin boy who came to the Moniyan Empire from Azrya.

skills.name: Key Insight
skills.desc: Harith gains insight from his Key. He gains 7% Hybrid Lifesteal for each nearby enemy hero, stacking up to 35%.

skills.name: Synchro Fission
skills.desc:
Harith creates a phantom of himself in a distant location. Harith and his phantom then release Synchro Fission toward each other, dealing 150 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to all enemy units on the path.

When the 2 energy waves collide, they explode, dealing 450 (+180% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to all enemies in the area.
This skill deals 60% extra damage to creeps (up to 1500 damage).

skills.name: Chrono Dash
skills.desc:
Harith dashes towards the target direction, gaining a 120 (+120% Total Magic Power) shield and enhancing his next Basic Attack. Harith's enhanced Basic Attack deals 200 (+100% Total Physical Attack)(+70% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and slows enemies by 40%. Hitting an enemy with Harith's enhanced Basic Attack reduces the cooldown of Chrono Dash by 3s. This shield can stack up to 3 times.

The enhanced Basic Attack only deals 50 (+100% Total Physical Attack)(+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to Turrets.

skills.name: Zaman Force
skills.desc:
Harith uses his Key to summon the Zaman Force, creating a multidimensional rift. Upon appearance, it reduces the cooldown of Chrono Dash by 4s. The rift continuously slows enemy heroes in the area by 40%.

When Harith comes into contact with the rift using Chrono Dash, he absorbs its energy, reducing the cooldown of Synchro Fission by 1s and Chrono Dash by 3s. Harith gains 15% Resilience while on the rift.

strong.desc: Thamuz is good against other sustained damage Fighters like Zilong and Sun because he has the top notch laning power.

weak.desc: Thamuz is countered by agile Marksmen, such as Karrie and Irithel, since he lacks CC and relies on Basic Attacks, making him an easy target to kite.

assist.desc: Thamuz can play to his strength when working with Supports who provide Movement Speed or CC, such as Angela and Rafaela, because they enable him to stay on his targets.

Thamuz.story: The lord of the Fire Demons who seeks to reignite the flames of the Abyss through war and violence.

skills.name: Grand Lord Lava
skills.desc:
Thamuz's Basic Attacks have a chance of 10%–60% (scaling with Lava's Rage) to conjure Lava Energy beneath the target that erupts after a brief delay, dealing 55 (+60% Total Physical Attack)(+8*Hero Level) True Damage. Basic Attacks that don't trigger Lava Energy increase Thamuz's Lava's Rage.

While Thamuz is without his Scythes, he gains 25% Movement Speed. After retrieving his Scythes, his next Basic Attack slows the target by 30% and is guaranteed to trigger Lava Energy.

skills.name: Molten Scythes
skills.desc:
Thamuz tosses Scythes in the target direction. After traveling a set distance or hitting the first enemy hero, they deal 120 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage. The Scythes will then slow nearby enemies by 30% and deal 45 (+20% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage every 0.5s.

After leaving Thamuz for a short time or reaching a set distance, the Scythes return to him, dragging enemies along their path toward Thamuz. Thamuz can move toward the Scythes or cast the skill again to retrieve them.

skills.name: Chasm Trample
skills.desc:
Thamuz jumps to a designated area to deal 230 (+60% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage and slow the enemy units by 25% for 2s upon landing. 
If the Scythes are still travelling, they will return to Thamuz and Molten Scythes will reset its CD instantly.

skills.name: Cauterant Inferno
skills.desc:
Thamuz spouts the lava within, dealing 300 (+80% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies and creating a Cauterant Zone around himself. The Cauterant Zone lasts 9s and deals 30 (+10% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage every 0.5s to enemies in the area.

Meanwhile, Thamuz gains 800 (+50% Extra Max HP) extra Max HP, immediately healing for the same amount, and gains 60% Attack Speed. Each time his Basic Attacks or Scythes deal damage, he recovers 1.5% Max HP.

strong.desc: Kimmy's ability to Basic Attack while moving makes her effective against heroes who rely on skillshots like Granger and Cecilion.

weak.desc: As a Marksman who cannot lock onto targets with Basic Attacks, Kimmy can be countered by heroes with high burst damage and mobility, such as Saber and Lancelot.

assist.desc: Kimmy excels at controlling and grouping enemies while dealing substantial damage, so she works well with heroes who have AOE CC like Badang and Ruby.

Kimmy.story: Born into a military family, she earned recognition through her own merit as the bold yet laid-back "tough girl" of the Border Guard.

skills.name: Aerial Dominance
skills.desc:
Kimmy can move and aim in different directions while using her Spray Gun, but she cannot lock onto a specific target. Each Chemical Bolt deals  (+18% Total Physical Attack) (+24% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage. Hitting enemy heroes or non-hero units restores 6 / 3 Starlium, respectively. At max Starlium, she continuously consumes it to enhance her Basic Attacks, making them deal  (+22.5% Total Physical Attack) (+30% Total Magic Power) damage and pierce targets (enhanced Basic Attacks only benefit from 50% Spell Vamp).
Kimmy can keep moving while shooting and does not benefit from Attack Speed Bonus. Every 1% Attack Speed gained is converted to 1 Magic Power.
Kimmy's Basic Attacks only deal 60% damage to Turrets.

skills.name: Anti-Grav Thruster
skills.desc:
Kimmy uses her jetpack to take flight, removing and becoming immune to slow effects. She can pass through terrain and gains 120% extra Movement Speed that decays over 1s. During the flight, she continuously fires 4 Chemical Bolts at nearby enemies, each dealing 20 (+25% Total Physical Attack) (+30% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and slowing them by 50% for 0.5s.
Kimmy does not consume Starlium during flight.

skills.name: Starlium Beam
skills.desc:
Kimmy fires a Starlium Beam in the target direction, immobilizing enemies in its path and dealing 300 (+50% Total Physical Attack) (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage for 1s.
Each enemy hero hit grants 20 Starlium.

skills.name: Traction Pulse
skills.desc:
