Kimmy charges and fires a Traction Pulse in the target direction. Upon hitting an enemy or reaching its max range, it explodes to deal 150 (+45% Total Physical Attack) (+54% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies and creates a force field that slows enemies within by 50%.

After 1s, the field contracts, pulling all enemy heroes to its center and dealing 15% of their current HP as Magic Damage while slowing them by 50% for 2s. Each enemy hero hit grants 40 Starlium.

strong.desc: Belerick excels at controlling enemies at close range, making him a great counter against squishy Assassins who need to get close to deal damage, like Lancelot and Ling.

weak.desc: Belerick is countered by heroes with long-range attacks like Valir and Xavier.

assist.desc: Belerick works well with heroes who can dish out a lot of damage against enemies CC'd by his taunt, such as Miya and Natan.

Belerick.story: A kind and fraternal elemental creature.

skills.name: Deadly Thorns
skills.desc:
Whenever Belerick takes 50 or more damage, he has a 25% chance to shoot the nearest enemy unit, dealing Magic Damage equal to 55(+5*Hero Level) plus 1.8% of his Max HP (scales with level). This attack can be triggered once every 0.4s.
His HP obtained from Equipment and Emblems is increased by 30%.

skills.name: Ancient Seed
skills.desc:
Belerick releases vines in a designated direction, dealing 200 (+50% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies along the path and slowing them by 25%. Ancient Seeds left by the vines will erupt into thorns after 1s, dealing 200 (+50% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies on the path and taunting them for 1.2s.

Deals 80% extra damage against Minions.

skills.name: Nature's Strike
skills.desc:
Belerick increases his Movement Speed by 80% and enhances his next Basic Attack. (The Movement Speed gained will decrease in 2s.)
This enhanced Basic Attack deals 300 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and slows the target by 60% for 1.4s. Belerick also recovers HP equal to 240 plus 10% of his lost HP.
Each time Deadly Thorns is triggered, reduce the cooldown of this skill by 1s.

skills.name: Wrath of Dryad
skills.desc: Belerick conjures spreading vines around him, dealing 100 (+30% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in the area 4 times, taunting and slowing them for 1.5s.

strong.desc: Hanzo's Ultimate enables him to poke or dive with relative safety, making him a serious threat to squishy heroes without mobility, like Layla, Ixia, and Zhuxin, while also disrupting the enemy in teamfights.

weak.desc: Hanzo fares less favorably against heroes with lots of mobility, such as Fanny, Lancelot, and Arlott, since it's difficult to build stacks on them, thus limiting Hanzo's damage.

assist.desc: Hanzo has high burst potential but needs time to stack his Passive, so he works best with heroes who can keep the enemy CC'd, like Aurora, Belerick, and Tigreal.

Hanzo.story: Genius ninja in the Shadow Sect who is at the crossroads.

skills.name: Ame no Habakiri
skills.desc: Damage dealt by Hanzo's Basic Attacks and skills inflicts a stack of Dark Ninjutsu on the target, up to 5 stacks. Ninjutsu: Demon Feast can only be cast on targets with full stacks of Dark Ninjutsu.

skills.name: Ninjutsu: Demon Feast
skills.desc:
The skill can only be used on targets with max stacks of Dark Ninjutsu. When targeting a creep, Hanzo will immediately devour it and digest it over a period of time to recover 20% HP.
When targeting the Turtle, Lord, or enemy heroes, he deals 600 (+120% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage, recovers 10% HP, and reduces this skill's cooldown by 60%.

skills.name: Ninjutsu: Dark Mist
skills.desc:
Hanzo dashes in the target direction, leaving dark mist in front that deals 90 (+45% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in the area every 0.5s, up to 5 times (this damage counts as Basic Attack damage but cannot Crit).
The dark mist slows enemies in the area by 40% and grants Hanzo 20% Movement Speed.

skills.name: Kinjutsu: Pinnacle Ninja
skills.desc:
Hanzo charges into battle as Hanekage, consuming 170 Energy per second. He gains 20 Energy when a nearby non-hero unit dies and 200 Energy if it's a hero. He gains 15 Energy when attacking a hero, and 45 Energy when attacking the Turtle or the Lord. When Energy runs out, Hanzo's body will be slowly drawn to Hanekage's position.

While in Hanekage form, Hanzo's body is invincible, while Hanekage ignores obstacles and gains 50% Movement Speed and 30% Attack Speed.

Use Again or Upon Death: Hanekage returns to Hanzo's body, recovering 16% HP and becoming untargetable during the return. If Hanekage is killed, Hanzo will be slowed by 50% and revealed for 6s.

skills.name: Ame no Habakiri
skills.desc: Damage dealt by Hanzo's Basic Attacks and skills inflicts a stack of Dark Ninjutsu on the target, up to 5 stacks. Ninjutsu: Demon Feast can only be cast on targets with full stacks of Dark Ninjutsu.

skills.name: Forbidden Ninjutsu: Soul Snatch
skills.desc: Hanekage dashes to an enemy hero, dealing 300 (+60% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the target and its surrounding units after a brief delay. Each stack of Dark Ninjutsu on the target increases the damage by 20% (surrounding units are not affected by this bonus). When the target has max stacks of Dark Ninjutsu, it deals additional damage equal to 15 (+2% Extra Physical Attack)% of the target's Max HP (this damage ignores 30% of the target's Physical Defense) and recovers 20% HP for Hanzo.

skills.name: Forbidden Ninjutsu: Black Mist
skills.desc:
Hanekage dashes in the target direction, briefly knocking enemies in his path airborne for 0.4s and leaving dark mist in the area he passes through (recovers 200 Energy when hitting an enemy hero). The mist deals 135 (+45% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in the area every 0.5s, up to 5 times (this damage counts as Basic Attack damage but cannot Crit).
The dark mist slows enemies in the area by 40%.

skills.name: Ninjutsu Flee: Return
skills.desc: Hanekage returns to Hanzo's body and exits the Hanekage form.

strong.desc: Lunox counters heroes that lack burst damage and healing, such as Yve and Lylia, because Lunox can use her self-healing to get the upper hand in a trade.

weak.desc: Lunox is countered by heroes with high mobility since her 2nd Skill has limited range and she lacks CC. Nimble heroes like Nolan and Ling can get in and out of her range all too easily.

assist.desc: Lunox is best paired with heroes with strong CC abilities like Franco and Minotaur. While Lunox lacks hard CC, if she can spam her skills on a stationary target, she can deal a ton of damage.

Lunox.story: A miraculous creature born in Twilight Orb.

skills.name: Dreamland Twist
skills.desc:
Lunox is twisted by the powers of Chaos and Order.
When she uses the Power of Order, she gains 0.5% Spell Vamp for every 1% Magic Penetration.
When she uses the Power of Chaos, she gains 0.5% Magic Penetration for every 1% Spell Vamp.

skills.name: Starlight Pulse
skills.desc:
Lunox summons a rain of starlight upon nearby enemies, dealing 200 (+110% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage. The starlight then returns to Lunox, each recovering 75 (+30% Total Magic Power) HP to her (doubled when hitting enemy heroes).

This skill shares a cooldown with Chaos Assault. It grants Power of Order and ends the Power of Chaos state.

skills.name: Chaos Assault
skills.desc:
Lunox unleashes Chaos Energy at an enemy, dealing Magic Damage equal to 250 (+120% Total Magic Power) plus 2.5% of the target's Max HP (1.5% of Max HP against Creeps).

This skill shares cooldown with Starlight Pulse. Grants one stack of Power of Chaos after use.

skills.name: Cosmic Fission
skills.desc: Lunox channels the power of Order and Chaos in the target direction, dealing 300 (+150% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in its path and slowing them by 40% for 2s.

skills.name: Order & Chaos
skills.desc:
Lunox is twisted by the powers of Chaos and Order.

When Power of Order is dominant, she can cast Brilliance to become invincible and deal continuous damage to nearby enemies.
When Power of Chaos is dominant, she can cast Darkening to blink in a target direction and greatly reduce the cooldown of Chaos Assault for a brief period.

skills.name: Dreamland Twist
skills.desc:
Lunox is twisted by the powers of Chaos and Order.
When she uses the Power of Order, she gains 0.5% Spell Vamp for every 1% Magic Penetration.
When she uses the Power of Chaos, she gains 0.5% Magic Penetration for every 1% Spell Vamp.

skills.name: Starlight Pulse
skills.desc:
Lunox summons a rain of starlight upon nearby enemies, dealing 200 (+110% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage. The starlight then returns to Lunox, each recovering 75 (+30% Total Magic Power) HP to her (doubled when hitting enemy heroes).

This skill shares a cooldown with Chaos Assault. It grants Power of Order and ends the Power of Chaos state.

skills.name: Chaos Assault
skills.desc:
Lunox unleashes Chaos Energy at a target enemy, dealing 250 (+120% Total Magic Power) (+2.5% target's Max HP) Magic Damage (+1.5% target's Max HP against creeps).

While enhanced by Power of Chaos, this skill has a brief cooldown, and its cast interval is unaffected by Cooldown Reduction.

skills.name: Cosmic Fission
skills.desc: Lunox channels the power of Order and Chaos in the target direction, dealing 300 (+150% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in its path and slowing them by 40% for 2s.

skills.name: Power of Order: Brilliance
skills.desc: Lunox creates a Light Barrier, becoming invincible and untargetable for 2.5s, and gaining 30% Movement Speed. The barrier moves with her, dealing 100 (+50% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies every 0.5s.

skills.name: Dreamland Twist
skills.desc:
Lunox is twisted by the powers of Chaos and Order.
When she uses the Power of Order, she gains 0.5% Spell Vamp for every 1% Magic Penetration.
When she uses the Power of Chaos, she gains 0.5% Magic Penetration for every 1% Spell Vamp.

skills.name: Starlight Pulse
skills.desc:
Lunox summons a rain of starlight upon nearby enemies, dealing 200 (+110% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage. The starlight then returns to Lunox, each recovering 75 (+30% Total Magic Power) HP to her (doubled when hitting enemy heroes).

This skill shares a cooldown with Chaos Assault. It grants Power of Order and ends the Power of Chaos state.

skills.name: Chaos Assault
skills.desc:
Lunox unleashes Chaos Energy at an enemy, dealing Magic Damage equal to 250 (+120% Total Magic Power) plus 2.5% of the target's Max HP (1.5% of Max HP against Creeps).

This skill shares cooldown with Starlight Pulse. Grants one stack of Power of Chaos after use.

skills.name: Cosmic Fission
skills.desc: Lunox channels the power of Order and Chaos in the target direction, dealing 300 (+150% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in its path and slowing them by 40% for 2s.

skills.name: Power of Chaos: Darkening
skills.desc:
Lunox blinks in the target direction, dealing 250 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies and slowing them by 30% for 4s.

For the next 4s, Lunox enhances her Power of Chaos, greatly reducing the cooldown of Chaos Assault (unaffected by Cooldown Reduction). Using Starlight Pulse ends this enhanced state.

strong.desc: Mages without mobility such as Odette, Pharsa, Yve and Cecilion will fall to Leomord's high movement speed and burst damage.

weak.desc: Heroes with CC like Phoveus, Franco, Akai and Nana.

assist.desc: Heroes with AOE or CC like Pharsa and Vexana that can follow up after Leomord breaks the enemy formation.

Leomord.story: A sworn knight who vowed to guard Necrokeep until his last breath.

skills.name: The Oath Keeper
skills.desc:
Leomord's Basic Attacks and skills grant him 25 Oath Keeper energy each time they deal damage to targets. When the energy reaches 100, his next Basic Attack deals 200% Crit Damage and restores 100 (+10*Hero Level) (+15% Extra Max HP) HP.
Leomord's Basic Attacks cannot normally Crit, and every 1% Crit Chance is converted into 1% Crit Damage.
Mounted State: Each Oath Keeper energy gain is increased by 200%.

skills.name: Momentum
skills.desc:
Leomord begins channeling, slowing enemies in the target direction by 25%. When the channeling is complete or interrupted, he thrusts his sword in the same direction, dealing up to 500 (+180% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit (scales with channeling time; increased to 160% against creeps except the Turtle and Lord) and slowing them by 40% for 1s.

Use Again: Cast the skill again to cancel the channeling and immediately perform the thrust.

skills.name: Decimation Assault
skills.desc: Leomord charges in a designated direction, dealing 300 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies along the way and slowing them by 30% for 1s.

skills.name: Phantom Steed
skills.desc:
Leomord summons Barbiel to rush to him, knocking back and dealing 100 (+40% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to all enemies in its path.
If Barbiel comes in contact with Leomord, they will enter the Mounted State, gaining 50% Movement Speed that decays over 2s.
Mounted State: Leomord gains a brand-new set of skills, and his Basic Attack can be used while moving and hits a circular area to deal AOE damage. Leomord also gains 70 Movement Speed and 30 Physical & Magic Defense. This lasts 10s.

skills.name: The Oath Keeper
skills.desc:
Leomord's Basic Attacks and skills grant him 25 Oath Keeper energy each time they deal damage to targets. When the energy reaches 100, his next Basic Attack deals 200% Crit Damage and restores 100 (+10*Hero Level) (+15% Extra Max HP) HP.
Leomord's Basic Attacks cannot normally Crit, and every 1% Crit Chance is converted into 1% Crit Damage.
Mounted State: Each Oath Keeper energy gain is increased by 200%.

skills.name: Phantom Stomp
skills.desc: Barbiel leaps forward, dealing 325 (+120% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in the landing area and in front of him, while slowing them by 40% for 1s.

skills.name: Phantom Charge
skills.desc: Barbiel charges forward in a designated direction, and knocks back all enemies on the path, dealing 300 (+80% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.

skills.name: Phantom Steed
skills.desc:
Leomord summons Barbiel to rush to him, knocking back and dealing 100 (+40% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to all enemies in its path.
If Barbiel comes in contact with Leomord, they will enter the Mounted State, gaining 50% Movement Speed that decays over 2s.
Mounted State: Leomord gains a brand-new set of skills, and his Basic Attack can be used while moving and hits a circular area to deal AOE damage. Leomord also gains 70 Movement Speed and 30 Physical & Magic Defense. This lasts 10s.

strong.desc: Vale counters slow Fighters like Terizla and Hilda because he can chip away their HP with his skills from afar while using his high Movement Speed to dodge their skills.

weak.desc: Vale is countered by agile Assassins that can become untargetable like Hayabusa and Ling, as it will be difficult to catch them with his CC while they can eliminate Vale easily with their high damage.

assist.desc: Vale works well with frontline Tanks like Lolita and Belerick who can protect him while he continuously pokes the enemy.

Vale.story: The heir to Wind Fort, a wind mage.

skills.name: Windtalk
skills.desc: Vale gains 1 stack of Windtalk each time he gets a kill or an assist that increases his Movement Speed by 8. This effect can stack up to 10 times.

skills.name: Wind Blade
skills.desc: Vale unleashes two wind blades toward the left and right of a target area, each dealing 250 (+80% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit.

skills.name: Windblow
skills.desc: Vale sends a whirlwind in the target direction, dealing 350 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit and knocking them airborne for 0.8s (each enemy can only be knocked airborne 1 time). When the whirlwind reaches its destination, it will remain for 1.6s while continuously dealing a total of 150 (+30% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in range.

skills.name: Windstorm
skills.desc:
Vale summons a windstorm at the target location, dealing a total of 150 (+30% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in the area and slowing them by 20% while continuously pulling them into its center. After 1s, the windstorm will explode, dealing 900 (+240% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage.

The remaining whirlwinds will also be pulled into the center.

strong.desc: Claude is strong against heroes with weak CC abilities and poor Mobility, so he can easily dodge their attacks with his 2nd Skill, such as Vexana and Luo Yi.

weak.desc: Claude is countered by heroes with powerful CC abilities. Their CC abilities will cause Claude to lose his Mobility advantage. Like Eudora and Franco.

assist.desc: Claude works best with heroes who can buff him, like Angela and Faramis.

Claude.story: An excellent thief who comes and goes without a trace.

skills.name: Battle Side-by-side
skills.desc: Dexter assists Claude on each of his Basic Attacks, dealing 25 (+25% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the same target (inherits a portion of Attack Effects).

skills.name: Art of Thievery
skills.desc:
Claude emits a disruption wave in the target direction, dealing 250 (+40% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in a fan-shaped area, reducing their Movement Speed by 20% and Attack Speed by 10%.
For each enemy hit, he gains 4% Movement and Attack Speed (doubled if hitting enemy heroes) for 6s (up to 5 stacks).

skills.name: Battle Mirror Image
skills.desc:
Claude conjures a mirror image of Dexter at the target location that automatically attacks nearby enemies for 5.5s. The mirror image's Basic Attacks deal 25 (+25% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and trigger Claude's Attack Effects.
Use Again: Claude switches places with the mirror image.

skills.name: Blazing Duet
skills.desc: Claude and Dexter rapidly fire at nearby enemies for 3s, dealing 120 (+10% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to up to two enemies with each shot (counts as Basic Attack Damage). Claude also gains a 20 (+3% Total Physical Attack) shield each time the Ultimate deals damage to an enemy.

strong.desc: Aldous is strong against heroes who cannot pressure him in the early game, so that he can easily build up the damage buff for his 1st Skill. Such as Zilong and Leomord.

weak.desc: Aldous is countered by heroes who are strong in the early game. They would make it very difficult for Aldous to build up the damage buff he needs. Heroes like Martis and Dyrroth are good examples here.

assist.desc: Aldous works well with heroes that are strong in the early game so that he can farm efficiently, such as Balmond and Bruno.

Aldous.story: The guardian of the Minoan Labyrinth.

skills.name: Contract: Transform
skills.desc:
After every 2 Basic Attacks, Aldous gains a 500 (+3 times the number of Soul Steal stacks) shield for 3s on his next Basic Attack.
This effect can only be triggered once every 5s.

skills.name: Contract: Soul Steal
skills.desc:
Aldous unleashes his inner energy to enhance his next Basic Attack to deal 200 (+100% Total Physical Attack) (+ 5 per Soul Steal stack) Physical Damage. This attack deals 300% extra Base Damage to minions or creeps.

Killing an enemy hero or siege minion with this attack grants 10 Soul Steal stacks, while killing other non-hero units grants 4 stacks (up to 650 stacks).

Aldous also gains 5 stacks when a nearby enemy siege minion dies, and 2 stack(s) when any other nearby non-hero unit dies.

skills.name: Contract: Explosion
skills.desc: Aldous assumes a defensive stance, gaining 30% Damage Reduction and 20% extra Movement Speed for 2s or until manual cancellation. He then deals 100 (+20% Total Physical Attack)-200 (+40% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies and stuns them for 0.5-1s (scales with defensive stance duration).

skills.name: Contract: Chase Fate
skills.desc: Aldous gains vision of all enemy heroes for 5s. During this time, if Aldous uses this skill again, he can dash toward an enemy hero. When Aldous hits that enemy hero, he will deal Physical Damage equal to 250 plus 8% of the target's Max HP and knock back the target for 1s. (Aldous can interrupt target's Recall during the dash.)

strong.desc: Selena is strong against squishy heroes with poor Mobility, simply because it's hard for them to dodge her 2nd Skill. Such as Gord and Layla.

weak.desc: But Selena is weak against heroes that can resist or remove her CC abilities, such as Hanabi and Wanwan.

assist.desc: Selena works well with ranged heroes that can deal high burst damage, such as Xavier and Beatrix.

Selena.story: Dark Elf girl who fell into the Abyss.

skills.name: Symbiosis
skills.desc:
Selena can switch freely between her two forms.
Elven Form: Selena applies Abyssal Mark when her skills deal damage. Marks last 8s and can stack up to 2 times.
Abyssal Form: Selena's Basic Attacks become melee and deal  (+25% Total Magic Power) additional Magic Damage. Her skills deal 240-450 (+40% Total Magic Power) additional Magic Damage to enemies with Abyssal Mark and consume their mark stacks by 1. This damage is increased to 250% against Creeps and Minions.

skills.name: Abyssal Trap
skills.desc:
Selena summons an Abyssal Devil to lurk underground. The Abyssal Devil will attach itself to any enemy that approaches, inflicting a stack of Abyssal Mark and reducing their Movement Speed by 50% for 1s. The Abyssal Devil then explodes, dealing 350 (+150% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in the area and reducing their Movement Speed by 50% for 1s.

Each additional Abyssal Devil on an enemy increases the final damage by 50%.

Each Abyssal Devil can lurk up to 60s and up to 3 Abyssal Devils can exist at the same time.

skills.name: Abyssal Arrow
skills.desc:
Selena fires an abyssal wraith in the target direction that stops when it hits an enemy hero, Creep, or Summon, dealing 250 (+20% Total Magic Power) to 500 (+40% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to the target hit and stunning them for 0.5s to 2.5s while revealing their locations (the damage and stun duration increase with the wraith's travel distance).

The wraith will pick up Abyssal Trap as it travels over it and will apply the trap to the enemy target upon stunning them.

If the wraith stuns an enemy for 0.9s or longer, Selena will gain 60% Movement Speed for 2s.

skills.name: Primal Darkness
skills.desc:
Passive: Selena gains 5 Magic Power.
Active: Selena enters Abyssal Form, gaining 20% Movement Speed for 0.8s and immediately resetting the cooldowns of Soul Eater and Garotte.

skills.name: Symbiosis
skills.desc:
Selena can switch freely between her two forms.
Elven Form: Selena applies Abyssal Mark when her skills deal damage. Marks last 8s and can stack up to 2 times.
Abyssal Form: Selena's Basic Attacks become melee and deal  (+25% Total Magic Power) additional Magic Damage. Her skills deal 240-450 (+40% Total Magic Power) additional Magic Damage to enemies with Abyssal Mark and consume their mark stacks by 1. This damage is increased to 250% against Creeps and Minions.

skills.name: Soul Eater
skills.desc:
Selena strengthens her claws with the power of the Abyss, causing the next Basic Attack to dash to a target and deal an additional 200 (+130% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage while granting her a 300 (+150% Total Magic Power) shield.

If this damage detonates an Abyssal Mark, Garotte's cooldown will reset.

skills.name: Garotte
skills.desc:
Selena charges in the target direction, dealing 150 (+50% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in her path.

If this skill's damage detonates an Abyssal Mark, its cooldown will reset.

skills.name: Blessing of the Moon Goddess
skills.desc:
Passive: Gains 5 Magic Power.
Active: Selena enters Elven Form, gaining 20% Movement Speed for 0.8s and immediately resetting the cooldowns of Abyssal Trap and Abyssal Arrow.

strong.desc: Kaja counters Assassins that rely on constant movement, such as Fanny and Lancelot, as his Ultimate will instantly shutdown their mobility.

weak.desc: Kaja is countered by Marksmen or Mages with long range like Layla and Pharsa because the short range of his Ultimate will put him at the mercy of their attacks.

assist.desc: Kaja works best with high burst damage heroes like Eudora and Aamon that can quickly kill a target suppressed by his Ultimate.

Kaja.story: Leader of Nazar who guards the Celestial Palace.

skills.name: Wrath Sanction
skills.desc:
Kaja achieves Wrath Sanction every 6s. His next Basic Attack will send lightning at enemies, dealing Magic Damage equal to 100 (+100% Total Magic Power) plus 4% of targets' Max HP and paralyzing them. The lightning will jump to enemies nearby as well (up to 3 times).

Increases damage by 200% of targets' Max HP on Minions and Creeps.

Paralyze: Slows enemy heroes and summons while reducing their damage by 2.5% (up to 6 stacks) for 5s.

skills.name: Ring of Order
skills.desc:
Kaja releases a Ringed Electric Blade that quickly expands and contracts, dealing 135 (+70% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit on its way out and back and paralyzing them. Enemies hit will also have their positions revealed briefly.
Each time Electric Blade deals damage, Kaja restores 100 HP (decays to 30% after multiple hits).

skills.name: Lightning Bomb
skills.desc: Kaja dashes in the target direction while leaving 3 Lightning Bombs along the way. The Bombs will explode upon enemy contact, dealing 150 (+35% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to the enemies and paralyzing them.

skills.name: Divine Judgment
skills.desc:
Kaja deals 200 (+80% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to a designated enemy hero, suppresses and paralyzes them for 1.5s.

During the suppression, Kaja can drag the enemy hero along with him.

strong.desc: Chang'e is strong against squishy heroes who have low mobility and short damage range, such as Eudora and Odette. Chang'e can easily hit them with her long-range skills while dodging their attacks.

weak.desc: Chang'e is countered by agile Assassins with high burst like Hanzo and Harley, as they can avoid Chang'e's skills and use their high burst damage to kill her since she lacks self-protection.

assist.desc: Chang'e works well with Tanks who can pull enemies together, like Gatotkaca and Tigreal, because Chang'e can follow them into teamfights to deal massive damage.

Chang'e.story: Naughty and cute disciple of the Great Dragon.

skills.name: Trouble Maker
skills.desc: Each time Chang'e deals damage to an enemy, she leaves a mark on them that reduces their Movement Speed by 1% (up to 20%).

skills.name: Starmoon Shockwave
skills.desc:
Chang'e sends an energy sphere in the target direction, dealing 350 (+130% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in its path and slowing them by 10% for 1.5s.

Crescent Moon: The Crescent Moon sends 4 extra energy spheres along with Chang'e's, each dealing 20% damage and slowing enemies hit. The slow effect can stack (up to 20%).

skills.name: Crescent Moon
skills.desc:
Chang'e summons the Crescent Moon, gaining a permanent 300 (+150% Total Magic Power) shield and 10% Movement Speed until the shield is broken. Each time she casts a skill, she gains an extra 50% Movement Speed that decays over 2.5s.
Crescent Moon: Enhances her Basic Attacks to deal 20 (+35% Total Magic Power) extra Magic Damage 2 times.

Each time Chang'e deals Magic Damage with her Basic Attacks or skills, this skill's cooldown is reduced by 0.2s.

skills.name: Meteor Shower
skills.desc:
Chang'e gains 10% Movement Speed for 4s. During this time, she fires 30 meteors in the target direction, each dealing 60 (+21% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to the first enemy hit. This skill deals 100% extra damage to minions and creeps.

Crescent Moon: Fires meteors alongside Chang'e, each dealing 33% damage.

strong.desc: Hanabi counters heroes that rely heavily on CC abilities, such as Tigreal and Belerick, since Hanabi can use her 1st Skill to ignore CC.

weak.desc: Hanabi is weak against heroes with high burst damage, such as Lancelot and Zilong, because they can quickly take down her shield.

assist.desc: Hanabi works well with heroes who can initiate with CC, such as Carmilla and Atlas, so she can focus on maximizing her bounce and Ultimate damage.

Hanabi.story: Leader of the Scarlet Sect, in the Scarlet Shadow of the Cadia Riverlands.

skills.name: Ninjutsu: Petal Barrage
skills.desc: Hanabi's Basic Attacks and skills will launch Petal Blades after hitting a target, dealing 30% damage, and bouncing up to 4 times to nearby enemies. Bounces from her Basic Attacks inherit her Attack Effects.

skills.name: Ninjutsu: Equinox
skills.desc:
Passive: Whenever Hanabi has a shield from any source, she is immune to control effects.
Active: Hanabi gains a 300 (+40% Total Physical Attack) shield for 5s. When the shield is active, she gains 20% Movement Speed and 25% Attack Speed, and 20% of her damage dealt to enemy heroes is added to this shield (only 10% of the damage dealt to non-hero units is added). This shield cannot exceed 50% of her Max HP.

skills.name: Ninjutsu: Soul Scroll
skills.desc: Hanabi fires the energy Kunai in the targeted direction, dealing 350 (+80% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies along the way and reducing their Movement Speed by 60% for 1s. Enemy units hit will also be marked, allowing Hanabi's next Bounce Damage from Basic Attacks to deal 30 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage on them with no damage decay.

skills.name: Forbidden Jutsu: Higanbana
skills.desc: Hanabi throws Higanbana in the targeted direction, dealing 300 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the first enemy hero hit and immobilizing them for 0.8s. After 1s, Higanbana will bloom at the location of the first hit, dealing 300 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies. Subsequent hits on the same target decay to 30% damage.

strong.desc: Uranus has great sustainability, making him strong against heroes with low-damage abilities, such as Tigreal and Franco.

weak.desc: Uranus lacks Mobility and CC abilities which makes him an easy target for ranged heroes, such as Layla and Lesley.

assist.desc: Uranus becomes much more powerful when working with high-damage heroes like Beatrix and Hanabi.

Uranus.story: Guardian made from lightning and air, guards the Celestial Palace.

skills.name: Radiance
skills.desc:
Each time Uranus takes damage, he gains a stack of Radiance. Each stack recovers 1.2-11.2 HP (increases with level) every 0.8s for 10s, up to 20 stacks.
Uranus's Basic Attacks deal  (+80% Total Physical Attack) (+ 4-8 × Radiance stacks) Magic Damage (increases with level).

skills.name: Ionic Edge
skills.desc:
Uranus releases 2 energy blades that orbit around him, dealing 155 (+30% Total Magic Power) (+6 × Radiance stacks) Magic Damage to enemies hit and slowing them by 30% for 2s.
Each blade can only damage the same target once.

skills.name: Transcendent Ward
skills.desc:
Uranus charges to the target location, dealing 80 (+20% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies along the way, and 160 (+80% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies at the target location and slowing them by 25%. Uranus also generates a 300 (+200% Total Magic Power) shield for 4s.

The shield explodes at the end of its duration or when destroyed, dealing 300 (+150% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies nearby.

skills.name: Consecration
skills.desc:
Uranus unleashes the energy stored within his body, removing slow effects on himself, recovering 200 HP, and gaining 60% Movement Speed for 8s (decays over time).

Uranus directly gains 5 stacks of Radiance, increasing shields received and HP Regen by 20% for 8s.

strong.desc: Martis counters squishy heroes with no mobility skills, such as Layla and Odette, who have a hard time avoiding his 1st Skill - 2nd Skill combo. Their relatively low HP also makes it easier for Martis to put them into execute range for his Ultimate.

weak.desc: Martis is countered by Fighters with high combat sustainability, such as Thamuz and Fredrinn, because they can survive Martis' burst damage and defeat him in prolonged battles.

assist.desc: Martis works best with Fighters that initiate teamfights like Alpha and Khaleed who he can follow up to secure a kill and chain more kills with his Ultimate.

skills.name: Asura's Wrath
skills.desc: Each time Martis uses a skill, his Attack Speed will be increased by 30%, 120% maximum. Lasts 4s. He gains (+8*Hero Level) extra Attack at full stacks.

skills.name: Asura Aura
skills.desc: Martis draws enemies to a fan-shaped area in front of him and deals 350 (+130% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage, slowing them by 40% for 2s.

skills.name: Mortal Coil
skills.desc:
Martis strikes in the target direction three times, each time dealing 225 (+120% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit. Enemies hit by the first and third strike are also knocked back. The attack direction can be changed once using the Joystick.

Use Again: Martis leaps in the target direction, dealing 300 (+150% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in his path and knocking them airborne.
Martis gains Control Immunity and 60% Damage Reduction when casting this skill.

skills.name: Decimation
skills.desc:
Martis lunges at a designated enemy hero, dealing 700 (+120% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage (deals True Damage if the enemy's HP is below 50%).
If the enemy is killed by this skill, Martis can cast the skill again within 10s and gains 100% extra Movement Speed that gradually decays over 5s. Each successive cast also increases the skill's damage by 15% (up to 30%).

strong.desc: Valir excels in both sustained damage and Crowd Control. He counters heroes lacking mobility like Balmond and Fredrinn.

weak.desc: Valir is countered by agile Assassins like Lancelot and Fanny because they can easily evade his 1st Skill.

assist.desc: Teaming up with Fighters with strong initiation like Khaleed and Yu Zhong can simultaneously enhance the entire team's combat potential.

Valir.story: Heir to Fire Throat, fire mage.

skills.name: Ashing
skills.desc:
Valir's skills apply a stack of Ablaze to enemies hit, dealing them Magic Damage equal to 1 (+0.2% Total Magic Power)% of their Max HP per second for 3s.

At 3 stacks, the fire detonates, dealing Magic Damage equal to 5 (+1% Total Magic Power)% of the target's Max HP and stunning them for 1s (a target can only be stunned by Valir's skills once every 7s).

skills.name: Burst Fireball
skills.desc:
Valir launches a fireball that explodes upon hitting the first target, dealing 235 (+80% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and slowing them by 30% for 1s. The flame on the ground explodes after 1s, dealing 118 (+40% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage.

Valir recharges 1 fireball every 9s (up to 2 charges). Hitting an enemy hero or legend creep with this skill immediately restores 1 charge, while hitting a minion restores 4s of the recharge time.

Vengeance Flame: Increases the cast range of Burst Fireball by 30%.

skills.name: Searing Torrent
skills.desc:
Valir unleashes a torrent of flames in the target direction, dealing 200 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and knocking them back to the end of its path.

Vengeance Flame: The torrent forms a firewall at the end of its path that lasts 4s, dealing 100 (+30% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies every 1s.

Enemies hit by the torrent or the firewall are slowed by 25% for 1s.

skills.name: Vengeance Flame
skills.desc: Valir removes all debuffs on himself and gains 40% Movement Speed that decays over 3s. Meanwhile, he conjures 4 Vengeance Flames that enhance Burst Fireball and Searing Torrent for 9s.

strong.desc: Gusion is strong against squishy heroes who lack movement abilities, so that Gusion can easily chase them down. Like Layla and Hanabi.

weak.desc: Gusion is countered by tank heroes with good single-target CC. Once controlled by them, Gusion will have a hard time escaping. Such as Franco and Kaja.

assist.desc: Gusion works well with heroes with strong opening CC, which will allow him to hit the target more easily with his 1st Skill, such as Vexana and Pharsa.

skills.name: Dagger Specialist
skills.desc: Each skill cast adds a rune to Gusion's dagger (stacks up to 4 times). Each stack enhances Gusion's next Basic Attack to deal additional damage equal to 3% of the target's Max HP and restore 50 (+25% Total Magic Power) HP to himself.

skills.name: Sword Spike
skills.desc:
Gusion throws a dagger in the target direction, dealing 200 (+50% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to the first enemy hit and marking them.

Use Again: Gusion moves behind the marked enemy, dealing 200 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to them. This skill cannot be interrupted while Gusion is moving behind the marked enemy.

skills.name: Shadowblade Slaughter
skills.desc:
Gusion throws a volley of daggers in the target direction, each dealing 100 (+40% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit and slowing them by 6% for 2s (when hit by multiple daggers, the slow effect can stack up to 30%).

Use Again: Gusion recalls the daggers, each dealing 65 (+40% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in its path.

skills.name: Incandescence
skills.desc:
Gusion dashes to the target location, resetting the cooldowns of Sword Spike and Shadowblade Slaughter. If Shadowblade Slaughter was cast once before the reset, Gusion can throw another five daggers on his next Shadowblade Slaughter cast, and recall a total of ten daggers afterward.

Use Again: Gusion dashes a short distance in the target direction.

strong.desc: Angela counters heroes with low mobility, such as Uranus and Belerick, by slowing them even further and speeding up her allies.

weak.desc: Angela is countered by nimble Assassins with burst damage, such as Ling and Harley.

assist.desc: Angela works best with highly mobile Assassins such as Lancelot and Aamon since she can provide CC and heals while increasing their mobility even further.

skills.name: Smart Heart
skills.desc: Angela gains 5% extra Movement Speed for 4s each time she casts a skill (up to 20%). When she's attached to an allied hero, the Movement Speed boost is transferred to the allied hero.

skills.name: Love Waves
skills.desc:
Angela sends Love Waves in the target direction, dealing 170 (+80% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit, revealing their positions briefly, and applying a stack of Lover's Mark (up to 5 stacks). Each stack of Lover's Mark increases the damage by 20% and slows enemies hit by 8% for 3s.
Love Waves also restore 150 (+75% Total Magic Power) HP to allied heroes hit. Angela can store up to 5 charges of this skill, and the charge time is affected by Cooldown Reduction.
When attached to allies, this skill's cooldown is reduced by 50%.

skills.name: Puppet-on-a-String
skills.desc:
Angela launches a Puppet String at the target enemy (excluding minions), dealing 300 (+40% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and gradually slowing them by up to 80%. If the enemy is still connected to the string after 3s, they'll be immobilized for 1.5s and take 450 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage.

Each stack of Lover's Mark increases the final damage by 20%.

skills.name: Heartguard
skills.desc:
Angela begins channeling, applying a 600 (+150% Total Magic Power) shield to the target allied hero regardless of distance. Once attached to an ally, the shield remains until detached. After the channeling is complete, she attaches herself to the allied hero for 20s, during which she can cast skills at zero Mana cost.
Angela can cast this skill again to cancel the channeling or detach herself from the allied hero. The attaching state will end early if the allied hero is killed.

strong.desc: Jawhead counters squishy heroes with no defensive skills that he can burst down after connecting with his Ultimate, such as Saber and Eudora.

weak.desc: Jawhead is countered by heroes with high sustained damage or high survivability, like Edith and Thamuz, because they can survive Jawhead's burst damage and beat him in a prolonged battle.

assist.desc: Jawhead works best with heroes that benefit from a quick engage like Odette and Belerick because he can throw them into the enemy for a surprise attack.

Jawhead.story: Intelligent robot used to protect little Ailee.

skills.name: Mecha Suppression
skills.desc: Each time Jawhead deals damage to an enemy hero or a Creep, he inflicts a stack of Mecha Suppression on them for 3s (up to 6 stacks). Each stack of Mecha Suppression increases all damage dealt to the target by 2% (limited to 50% for Creeps).

skills.name: Smart Missiles
skills.desc: Jawhead launches up to 12 missiles at random nearby enemies over 5s, each missile dealing 135 (+30% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the enemy hit.

skills.name: Ejector
skills.desc:
Jawhead gains 30% Movement Speed and a 500 (+100% Total Physical Attack) shield for 5s.

Use Again: Jawhead flings the nearest unit (prioritizes heroes) to the target location, dealing 300 (+80% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in the area and stunning them for 0.5s. Can be used on allied heroes and they will not take damage.

skills.name: Unstoppable Force
skills.desc: Jawhead charges into the target enemy hero, dealing 350 (+150% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and briefly stunning them. Enemies around the target are knocked back and dealt the same amount of damage. Jawhead is immune to control effects during the charge.

strong.desc: Lesley is strong against tank heroes who rely heavily on equipment. Because her attacks can deal True Damage that will ignore the defense these heroes get from the equipment. Such as Gatotkaca and Fredrinn.

weak.desc: Lesley is countered by Assassin heroes with multiple movement abilities, which will cause her to lose her range advantage. Such as Ling and Lancelot.

assist.desc: Lesley works best with tank heroes who can provide better attacking conditions for her, like Minotaur and Baxia.

Lesley.story: Adopted daughter of House Vance, a clandestine sniper.

skills.name: Lethal Shot
skills.desc:
If Lesley doesn't take damage for 5s, her next Basic Attack gains extra range and 50% Crit Chance and deals 100 (+100% Total Physical Attack) True Damage (20% extra damage against minions).
Each point of fixed Physical Penetration and each 1% Physical Penetration Lesley acquires is converted into 1% Crit Damage, but her base Crit Damage is reduced to 130%.
Lesley's Basic Attack restores 5 points of energy and her Lethal Shot doubles the amount of energy restored. The Lethal Shot effect will activate immediately after Lesley uses a skill.

skills.name: Master of Camouflage
skills.desc:
Lesley enters the Camouflage state, gaining double Energy Regen, 40% extra Movement Speed, and 85 extra Physical Attack.

The state lasts 3s or until Lesley takes or deals damage. Enemies can detect Lesley's position through distortions of their surroundings.

skills.name: Tactical Grenade
skills.desc:
Lesley throws a tactical grenade in the target direction while jumping back slightly, dealing 150 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in a fan-shaped area and knocking them back.

Lesley can cast this skill during Ultimate Snipe to cancel the state and immediately fire a Fatal Bullet at the target. The attack is only triggered when there are unfired Fatal Bullets left.

skills.name: Ultimate Snipe
skills.desc:
Passive: Lesley's Crit Chance is increased by 10%.
Active: Lesley locks onto the target enemy hero and fires 4 Fatal Bullets in succession, each dealing 250 (+80% Extra Physical Attack) (+5% target's lost HP) Physical Damage and restoring 10 energy on hit (Bullets can be blocked by other enemy heroes).
If this skill is interrupted, each remaining Bullet reduces the skill cooldown by 10%.

strong.desc: Pharsa can deal high burst damage from a very long range. A great counter to enemy back-row heroes with poor Mobility, such as Layla, Hanabi and Xavier.

weak.desc: Pharsa should beware of any displacement ability skill. Heroes like Franco, Zilong and Minsitthar can easily interrupt Pharsa's Ultimate.

assist.desc: Pharsa is best with heroes who can CC the enemy so that her skills can hit them. Atlas and Franco are good examples of this.

skills.name: Spiritual Unity
skills.desc: Every 5s to 8s (scales with Feathered Air Strike level), Verri enters the Hunting state. On Pharsa's next attack against an enemy hero, Verri deals extra Magic Damage equal to 8% of the target's Max HP (+1% per 200 Magic Power) and slows them by 60% for 1s.

skills.name: Curse of Crow
skills.desc:
Pharsa unleashes a blast of magic at the target location, dealing 300 (+120% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit and marking them for 4s.

When Pharsa hits a marked enemy with a Basic Attack or active skill, the mark is consumed, stunning them for 1s.

Marked enemies will have their positions revealed.

skills.name: Energy Impact
skills.desc: Pharsa releases magic energy in the target direction, dealing 475 (+145% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit.

skills.name: Feathered Air Strike
skills.desc:
Passive: Each level-up reduces Spiritual Unity's interval by 1s.
Active: Pharsa takes flight for 8s and bombards the target area, dealing 530 (+160% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies within. This skill can be cast up to 4 times during the flight.

skills.name: Wings by Wings
skills.desc:
Pharsa turns into mist and wraps around Verri, gaining 150% extra Movement Speed (decays over time) and the ability to fly over terrain for 5s.

This state ends immediately when she is controlled or uses a Basic Attack or skill.

strong.desc: Helcurt can swiftly approach enemies with his mobility and quickly take down heroes like Layla, Chang'e, and Vale with his burst damage.

weak.desc: Helcurt should avoid tank heroes that he can't burst down, such as Fredrinn, Belerick, and Gatotkaca. Once Helcurt is caught by their CC, they can make short work of him with their teammates.

assist.desc: Helcurt's Ultimate is a great tool to obscure the enemy's vision and makes it easier for him and his team to engage. Additionally, heroes with CC like Tigreal, Esmeralda, and Terizla work great with Helcurt to secure kills.

Helcurt.story: The nightmare of the Abyss who's willing to betray anyone in order to survive.

skills.name: Shadow of Styx
skills.desc:
Helcurt remains in Prowler state until spotted by the enemy team, gaining 15% extra Movement Speed, and continuously regenerating  (+30% Total Physical Attack) HP if he has not dealt any damage.
He gains 50% extra Attack Speed and 50% extra Movement Speed (decreasing over time) for 4s upon being spotted and leaves Prowler state 0.75s later.
Any hero killed after being hit by Helcurt leaves a dark fog that explodes and spreads with a cooldown of 10s. Helcurt Conceals himself while within the dark fog.

skills.name: Hidden Terror
skills.desc:
Passive: When Helcurt is in the Prowler state, Terrify also affects enemy heroes hit and briefly reveals them. Its cooldown decreases 3 times as fast as normal.
Active: Helcurt blinks to the target location and deals 150 (+70% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in the area, reducing their Movement Speed by 60% for 0.75s.

skills.name: Deadly Stinger
skills.desc:
Passive: Helcurt gains 1 Deadly Stinger for every 1.5s spent in Prowler state or on each Basic Attack. Up to 5 Deadly Stingers can be stored.

Active: Helcurt fires all Deadly Stingers forward, each dealing 180 (+50% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies (minions and creeps take only 70% of the damage).
Deadly Stinger counts as a Basic Attack but cannot Crit.

skills.name: Dark Night Falls
skills.desc:
Helcurt summons the Dark Night, reducing all enemies' sight range and making them lose sight of their allies for 3s. During Dark Night, Helcurt gains 40% extra Movement Speed.

The Dark Night makes it easier for Helcurt to leave enemies' sight range.

strong.desc: Zhask is strong against burst damage heroes like Aldous and Paquito because his high HP after fusing with his Nightmaric Spawn, makes him tough to burst down.

weak.desc: Zhask is countered by agile or long range heroes, such as Pharsa and Cecilion, because they can quickly step out of range of his immobile Nightmaric Spawn.

assist.desc: Zhask works best with heroes that can lock down enemies so he can have more time to deal damage, like Belerick and Lolita.

Zhask.story: King of the Swarm who disrupts the balance of the universe.

skills.name: Decimation
skills.desc: Upon death, Zhask summons a frenzied Nightmaric Spawn on the spot, which gradually loses HP over time until death.

skills.name: Nightmaric Spawn
skills.desc:
Zhask summons a Nightmaric Spawn that inherits 55% of his Attributes (HP, Hybrid Defense, and Magic Power). It automatically attacks nearby enemies by firing a Death Ray, dealing 55 (+20% extra Attack Speed) (+20% Magic Power) Magic Damage and slowing them by up to 30%. It disappears if Zhask is too far away.
Death Ray is considered a Basic Attack but cannot Crit.

Fusion Enhanced: The damage of Nightmaric Spawn is increased to 180%, and it can be repositioned.

skills.name: Mind Eater
skills.desc:
Zhask fires a penetrating psychic beam in the target direction, dealing 300 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage. Afterward, the Nightmaric Spawn attacks enemies hit by Mind Eater, dealing 120 (+80% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and stunning them for 0.5s.

Fusion Enhanced: Mind Eater damage is increased to 540 (+180% Total Magic Power).

skills.name: Hive Clones
skills.desc:
Zhask releases a swarm of Hive Clones in the target direction. The clones explode upon hitting an enemy, dealing 55 (+18% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and reducing their Movement Speed by 80% for 1s.
If the Hive Clones hit no target, they burrow into the ground to ambush passing enemies.

Fusion Enhanced: Zhask releases several swarms of Hive Clones.
Subsequent damage to the same target decays to 20%.

skills.name: Dominator's Descent
skills.desc:
Zhask selects a target area to summon an enhanced Nightmaric Spawn and fuses with it. Nightmaric Spawn can inherit 100% of his attributes and get extra attributes. His skills are enhanced during the fusion period.

Use Again: Zhask immediately gets off the Nightmaric Spawn and loses enhancement.

strong.desc: Hylos is strong against heroes without mobility skills that can't easily escape his 2nd Skill, like Layla and Gord.

weak.desc: Hylos is countered by high mobility heroes like Karrie and Lesley, as they are hard for him to lock down and can also take him out.

assist.desc:
Hylos works best with heroes that need faster Movement Speed to engage,
such as Terizla and Minsitthar.

skills.name: Thickened Blood
skills.desc:
Hylos gains 1.5 extra Max HP for every 1 extra Max Mana he possesses.

When out of Mana, Hylos can use his HP for skill casts.

skills.name: Law and Order
skills.desc:
Hylos gains 50% Movement Speed and causes his next Basic Attack within 3s to deal 300 (+80% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to the target, knocking back and stunning them for 1s.

The extra Movement Speed rapidly decays over the duration and vanishes after the next Basic Attack.

skills.name: Ring of Punishment
skills.desc:
Hylos releases the Ring of Punishment, dealing 100 (+20% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies per second while slowing them by 4% and reducing their Attack Speed by 7.5% for 2.5s (up to 8 stacks). Each stack also increases the enemy's damage taken from the Ring of Punishment by 5%. The Ring of Punishment consumes 30 Mana per second when it's active.

Use Again: Hylos cancels the Ring of Punishment.

skills.name: Glorious Pathway
skills.desc:
Hylos creates a huge pathway in the target direction, reducing enemies' Movement Speed by 75% for 1s. The pathway lasts 6s.
When on the pathway, Hylos gains Slow Immunity and recovers 3% of his Max HP per second. Allied heroes moving along the pathway gain 60% extra Movement Speed, while enemy heroes on the pathway will be slowed by 25%.

strong.desc: Diggie counters close range, CC focused heroes like Atlas and Lolita since he can pull them away and remove their CCs with his Ultimate.

weak.desc: Diggie is countered by agile and bursty Assassins like Ling and Aamon because his own lack of defense makes him an easy target.

assist.desc: Diggie works best with Marksmen that lack mobility or survivability like Layla and Miya because his skills can protect them very well.

Diggie.story: The owl scholar who can control time.

skills.name: Young Again
skills.desc: Upon death, Diggie reverses time and turns back to egg form, in which he can continue to move around and gains a new set of skills. He cannot be targeted in egg form and will revive after a period of time.

skills.name: Auto Alarm Bomb
skills.desc:
Diggie flings an owl alarm to the target location that remains stationary for 25s or until it reacts to the first enemy in range, chasing them and exploding upon collision. The explosion deals 350 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit and slows them by 30%.

Diggie gains 2 stacks of Dangerous Sparks each time an enemy hero is hit by the explosion (up to 60 stacks), each stack increasing the skill's damage by 1%. He loses half the stacks upon death, but can gain 1 stack of Dangerous Sparks each time his skill hits an enemy hero when in egg form.

Up to 5 owl alarms can exist at the same time.

skills.name: Reverse Time
skills.desc:
Diggie marks the target enemy hero for 4s and pulls them back to their previous location after the duration ends, dealing 150 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and briefly slowing them by 80%.

If the enemy moves out of the circle, the pull will be triggered early.

skills.name: Time Journey
skills.desc: Diggie removes all debuffs on nearby allied heroes (including himself) and grants them a 650 (+300% Total Magic Power) shield and Control Immunity for 2s. He also gains 50% Movement Speed for 0.5s.

strong.desc: Lancelot counters long-range Mages, such as Cecilion and Pharsa, because there is a delay to their skills and Lancelot can use his 1st Skill to dodge them while going in for the kill.

weak.desc: Lancelot is weak against heroes who can counter his mobility like Minsitthar and Phoveus, as his 1st Skill and Ultimate will be heavily restricted.

assist.desc: Lancelot works well with heroes who can pull enemies together, like Atlas and Tigreal, because they allow him to hit more enemies with his AOE skills.

Lancelot.story: Prodigal son and genius swordsman of House Baroque.

skills.name: Soul Cutter
skills.desc: Each time Lancelot charges, his damage is increased by 7.5% for 4s, up to 30%.

skills.name: Puncture
skills.desc:
Lancelot charges in the target direction, dealing 150 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies along the way and applying a Sword Mark to the first unmarked enemy hit. The Sword Mark lasts 3s.

If Lancelot successfully applies a Sword Mark to an enemy, the skill's cooldown will be reset.

skills.name: Thorned Rose
skills.desc: Lancelot strikes in the target direction 3 times, each time dealing 220 (+120% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit (damage is reduced when multiple enemies are hit). He's untargetable during this process. Enemies in the center of the area take all the 3 hits and are slowed by 20% for 0.5s. The slow effect can stack.

skills.name: Phantom Execution
skills.desc: After a short delay, Lancelot performs an executioner's strike in the target direction, dealing 350 (+170% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit. He is invincible throughout the process.

strong.desc: Odette's Ultimate is capable of unleashing massive damage and is effective against heroes lacking interruption skills, such as Layla and Irithel.

weak.desc: Odette is countered by heroes with Crowd Control skills, such as Minotaur and Edith, because they can easily interrupt her Ultimate.

assist.desc: Pair Odette with heroes who can relocate their teammates like Johnson and Jawhead to make it easier for her to find the perfect position for her Ultimate.

Odette.story: Innocent princess that grew up in the Swan Castle and genius mage that excels in sword and scepter.

skills.name: Lakeshore Ambience
skills.desc:
For 8s after casting a skill, Odette's next Basic Attack gains extra range and bounces between enemies (or an enemy and herself), dealing 144-200 (+50% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage per hit.

Odette's skills also trigger this effect on hit (up to 3 stacks).

skills.name: Avian Authority
skills.desc: Odette releases a magic swan in the target direction, dealing 300 (+150% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit and slowing them by 30% for 2s.

skills.name: Blue Nova
skills.desc: Odette releases magic energy balls in the target direction, dealing 250 (+50% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to the first enemy hit and immobilizing them for 1.5s. Upon hit, an energy ball splits off to attack a nearby enemy (prioritizes enemy heroes), dealing 250 (+50% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and immobilizing them for 1.5s.

skills.name: Swan Song
skills.desc:
Odette begins to dance, gaining a 1200 (+150% Total Magic Power) shield while dealing up to 2000 (+750% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in a large area around her for 5s. The bounce speed and frequency of Lakeshore Ambience's sound wave in the area are also enhanced when the skill is active.
Use again while the skill is active to dash in the target direction.
The skill is a channeling cast and will be interrupted if Odette is hit by high level control effects (knocked airborne, transformed, etc.) or if she performs another action.

strong.desc: Argus counters heroes with poor mobility who cannot escape his attacks once he closes in, such as Gord and Aurora.

weak.desc: Argus is countered by Tanks with high Physical Defense, like Gatotkaca and Tigreal, because a lot of his damage comes from Basic Attacks.

assist.desc: Argus works well with utility Supports like Angela and Rafaela who can provide extra Movement Speed and CC to allow Argus to stick to his targets and dish out maximum damage.

Argus.story: The angel who fell from light into darkness.

skills.name: Warmonger
skills.desc:
When Argus' Meteoric Sword reaches 100 Malice Energy, he will launch a Demonic Slash with his next Basic Attack, dealing 180 (+80% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and recovering 150 (+10% Total Physical Attack) HP. This attack ignores 40% of the target's Physical Defense.
Argus' Meteoric Sword gains 5 Malice Energy per second and 10 Energy per Basic Attack (critical strikes grant an additional 10 Energy).

skills.name: Demonic Grip
skills.desc:
Argus stretches out a demonic hand in the target direction, dealing 125 (+60% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the first enemy hero or Creep hit, stunning them for 0.7s, and pulling him and the target towards each other. If no enemy is hit, Argus pulls himself toward the hand's destination instead.
Use Again: Argus dashes and strikes in the target direction, dealing 175 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies along the path.

skills.name: Meteoric Sword
skills.desc:
After a short delay, Argus thrusts his Meteoric Sword in the target direction, dealing 120 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit (200% damage against minions) and reducing their Movement Speed by 80% for 0.8s. The delay time decreases as Argus's Attack Speed increases. Enemy heroes hit will be Cursed for 4s, during which they leave a Cursed Trail behind. Argus gains 20% Movement Speed while on a Cursed Trail.
Meteoric Sword damage counts as Basic Attack damage and can trigger Attack Effects. Hitting heroes restores 100 Malice Energy (excess is preserved).

skills.name: Eternal Evil
skills.desc:
Argus removes all debuffs on himself, dealing 150 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies and becoming the Fallen Angel for 4s (hitting heroes enhances Warmonger). Fallen Angel: Gains 40% Movement Speed that decays over 1s; when the transformation ends, he converts all his damage dealt in the Fallen Angel state into HP. If he takes fatal damage in the Fallen Angel state, the duration of the Movement Speed boost will be reset, and he will gain Death Immunity for 4s. Enhanced Warmonger: Demonic Slash grants 50 Malice Energy (50 extra Malice Energy with critical hits).
If Death Immunity isn't triggered, the cooldown will be reduced by 50%.

strong.desc: Grock works well against heroes who cannot Blink over his wallto escape his CC,such as Gord and Layla.

weak.desc: Grock is countered by Magic Damage heroes with Blink who can kite him with relative ease,such as Kimmy and Harley.

assist.desc: Grock's Ultimate is a powerful CC that covers a wide area,so he pairs well with Mages like Odette and Pharsa to maximize their AOE damage.

skills.name: Bastion of Stone
skills.desc:
Grock gains a Shield equal to 14% of his Max HP and empowers the next Basic Attack to deal Physical Damage equal to 4 (+2% Total Physical Defense)% of the target's Max HP (capped at 300(+20*Hero Level) against non-hero units). This effect has a 18-12s cooldown (decreases with level), which reduces by 200% when near terrain.

Grock's skills cannot be interrupted, and he gains 30(+6*Hero Level)% extra Physical Defense while the Shield is active.

skills.name: Mighty Swing
skills.desc: Grock strikes forward with massive force, dealing 240 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage. Enemies with obstacles behind them are knocked back into the obstacle, launched airborne, and take extra Physical Damage equal to 4 (+2% Total Physical Defense)% of their Max HP (capped at 300(+20*Hero Level) against non-hero units). If there are no obstacles behind, enemies are only knocked back slightly.

skills.name: Earthen Rampart
skills.desc:
After a brief delay, Grock summons a stone wall in the target area, dealing 100 (+60% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage and knocking enemies towards him.
Use Again: Cancel the wall early.

skills.name: Tectonic Charge
skills.desc: Grock charges forward and gains Control Immunity, dealing 100 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in his path. Upon hitting terrain, he immediately stops and triggers a massive explosion that knocks nearby enemies airborne for 1s and deals 650 (+300% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage. If this skill doesn't hit terrain, 50% of its cooldown is refunded.

strong.desc: Irithel counters heroes with a shorter Basic Attack range than her or ones that lack mobility and can be easily kited, such as Miya and Alpha.

weak.desc: Irithel is countered by heroes with strong CC capabilities or can restrict movement, such as Badang and Silvanna. Because Irithel does not have any Blink skills, her advantage vanishes once her mobility is restricted.

assist.desc: Irithel works best with heroes that can slow or provide hard CC such as Valir and Hylos because they allow her more opportunities to deal damage.

Irithel.story: The forest hunter with a smilodon.

skills.name: Jungle Heart
skills.desc:
Irithel and Leo's immaculate teamwork allows her to shoot while moving. Each Basic Attack will shoot 2 crossbow bolt(s) (but takes longer to refill) that deal  (+55% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage per bolt (inherits a portion of Attack Effects).
Moving will build up Jungle Heart, up to a maximum of 10 units, and cause the next Basic Attack to unleash 1 additional bolt(s).

skills.name: Strafe
skills.desc: Irithel launches a volley of arrows on the target area, dealing 330 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies within and reducing their Physical Defense by 15 for 3s.

skills.name: Force of The Queen
skills.desc: Leo lets out a piercing roar, dealing 200 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies and slowing them by 40% for 2s.

skills.name: Heavy Crossbow
skills.desc: Irithel orders Leo to leap in the target direction and empowers her crossbow for 10s. For the duration, she gains 30 Movement Speed and her Basic Attacks shoot enhanced crossbow bolts, dealing 100 (+65% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the target and nearby units. Jungle Heart also accumulates faster for the duration.

strong.desc: Harley counters squishy heroes with poor mobility, such as Cecilion and Ixia, since he can deal burst damage while being on the move.

weak.desc: Harley is countered by tanky heroes like Lolita and Uranus, because Harley can't deal enough meaningful damage to them and they can block and tank his Ultimate for their teammates.

assist.desc: Harley is best with heroes who can disrupt the enemy formation like Akai and Khufra. He can follow them into the fight and burst down the squishy targets.

Harley.story: The young mage whose powers awakened once thrust into danger and who now arrives at the Magic Academy to study.

skills.name: Magic Master
skills.desc:
Harley's mastery of magic allows his Basic Attacks and skills to reduce the enemy's Magic Defense by 2 and increase his Attack Speed by 5%-10% (scales with hero level) for 3s on hit. This effect stacks up to 10 times.
Harley's Basic Attacks deal 60 (+50% Total Physical Attack) (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage.

skills.name: Poker Trick
skills.desc:
Harley throws 3 waves of cards, 3 cards per wave. Each card deals 130 (+25% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies.
After the same target is hit 3 time(s), the damage will decay to 50% on subsequent hits.

skills.name: Space Escape
skills.desc:
Harley blinks in the designated direction, leaving a magic hat at his starting location. His Movement Speed increases by 60% for 2s, then rapidly decays to normal over 4s.

Use Again: Return to the hat's location.

skills.name: Deadly Magic
skills.desc: Harley casts a magic ring of fire at an enemy hero, dealing 200 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and a 40% slow effect that decays to 15% after 1.5s. The ring will stay around the target, during which Harley can hit the ring to damage the target. The ring will explode after 4s, dealing Magic Damage equal to 200 (+50% Total Magic Power) plus 30% of the damage taken by the target while the ring was active.

strong.desc: Due to his great CC and high Physical Defense, Gatotkaca counters Marksmen with no mobility skills like Layla and Popol and Kupa..

weak.desc: Gatotkaca is countered by heroes that can remove CC like Diggie and Wanwan who will greatly reduce his effectiveness.

assist.desc: Gatotkaca works best with heroes with strong CC and damage, such as Kagura and Vale who can give his Ultimate even more value.

skills.name: Steel Bones
skills.desc:
Gatotkaca gains Physical Defense equal to 2% of his lost HP (up to 200). For every 300 damage he takes (calculated before damage reduction), he gains 5 Rage (up to 100).

Upon reaching over 25 Rage, Gatotkaca's next Basic Attack becomes enhanced, consuming all Rage to deal extra Magic Damage and recover HP. The extra damage scales with his Rage, level, and Magic Power.

skills.name: Blast Iron Fist
skills.desc: Gatotkaca slams the ground, creating a shattered zone in the target direction while dealing 200 (+150% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies within. Enemies in the shattered zone will take 100 (+20% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage per second and be slowed by 30%.

skills.name: Unbreakable
skills.desc: Gatotkaca begins channeling, then sprints in the target direction with a battle cry, taunting enemies on the path to attack him, dealing 200 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage for 0.9 - 1.5s. The taunt duration and sprint distance scale with the channel time. Canceling the skill reduces 50% of the remaining cooldown.

skills.name: Avatar of the Guardian
skills.desc:
Gatotkaca jumps to the target location, dealing 500 (+300% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies and knocking them airborne for 1s. Enemies near the center of the area will be knocked airborne for a longer duration, while enemies on the fringes will be pulled to the center.

The camera will move with the skill indicator but won't grant extra sight.

strong.desc: Tanks such as Tigreal and Fredrinn that take more damage from her HP percent attacks.

weak.desc: Long range heroes such as Xavier, Layla and Nana due to Karrie's medium range.

assist.desc:
Assassins like Saber and Aamon who can eliminate 
ranged threats so Karrie can focus the frontline.

skills.name: Lightwheel Mark
skills.desc: Karrie's Basic Attacks and skills apply a stack of Lightwheel Mark on hit. Once a target has 5 stacks of Lightwheel Marks, the marks will turn into a lightwheel, dealing True Damage equal to 5 (+1% Extra Physical Attack)% of the target's Max HP to them (only up to 300 damage against Creeps).

skills.name: Spinning Lightwheel
skills.desc: Karrie releases a sphere of surging energy in the target direction, dealing 250 (+90% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in its path. The sphere will stop upon hitting an enemy hero or reaching its max travel distance, continuously dealing 150 (+20% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies and slowing them by 80% for 1s.

skills.name: Phantom Step
skills.desc:
Karrie dashes in the target direction while throwing a lightwheel at the nearest enemy, dealing 150 (+70% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and applying a stack of Lightwheel Mark on them.

When in Dual Wield state, Karrie throws two lightwheels instead.

skills.name: Speedy Lightwheel
skills.desc: Karrie enters Quadruple Wield state for 10s, gaining 20% Movement Speed. Each Basic Attack deals 40 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and throws 2 lightwheels that inherit a portion of Attack Effects.

strong.desc: Roger counters squishy heroes that lack mobility such as Hanabi and Odette because they cannot easily escape from him.

weak.desc: Roger is countered by heroes that can restrict his mobility and prevent him from jumping in and out using his two forms, like Minsitthar and Khufra.

assist.desc: Roger works best with heroes with high AOE damage that can nuke the enemy team to low HP for him to finish off, such as Pharsa and Vexana.

Roger.story: A werewolf wandering in the dark forest longing for revenge.

skills.name: Full Moon Curse
skills.desc:
Roger can freely switch between human and wolf forms.

In human form, Roger's Basic Attack and skills deal additional damage equal to 4% of the target's current HP.

In wolf form, Roger's Basic Attack and skills deal additional damage equal to 4% of the target's lost HP.

This effect can only deal up to 60 additional damage against Creeps.

skills.name: Open Fire
skills.desc: Roger fires two quick shots in the target direction, dealing 250 (+125% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage in total to the enemy hit. The first shot is a hunter net that slows the enemy by 80% for 1.5s, while the second is a bullet that reduces the enemy's Physical Defense by 10 for 5s.

skills.name: Hunter's Steps
skills.desc: Roger gains 20% Movement Speed and 20% Attack Speed for 2.5s.

skills.name: Wolf Transformation
skills.desc:
Roger lunges in the target direction and turns into wolf form, dealing 150 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit and slowing them by 90% for 0.8s.

Passive: In wolf form, Roger gains 20 Physical & Magic Defense and 25 Movement Speed.

skills.name: Full Moon Curse
skills.desc:
Roger can freely switch between human and wolf forms.

In human form, Roger's Basic Attack and skills deal additional damage equal to 4% of the target's current HP.

In wolf form, Roger's Basic Attack and skills deal additional damage equal to 4% of the target's lost HP.

This effect can only deal up to 60 additional damage against Creeps.

skills.name: Lycan Pounce
skills.desc:
Roger lunges at the target enemy, becoming untargetable while dealing 250 (+125% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to up to 3 enemies in the area.

Getting a kill or assist will reduce the cooldown of this skill by 80%.

skills.name: Bloodthirsty Howl
skills.desc: Roger lets out a howl, increasing his Attack Speed by 1.15 times for 5s and revealing nearby enemy heroes with HP below 40%. If there are enemy heroes with HP below 40% in range, Roger gains 50% Movement Speed.

skills.name: Restore Human Form
skills.desc: Roger rolls in the target direction and reverts back to human form, gaining a 200 (+200% Extra Physical Attack) shield for 1.5s.

strong.desc: Backline DPS heroes such as Layla, Irithel and Xavier.

weak.desc: Mobile Assassins such as Saber, Aamon and Karina.

assist.desc:
Heroes with powerful CC such as Atlas or
high damage output like Beatrix to burst down enemies.

skills.name: Nether Touch
skills.desc: Vexana and her Eternal Guard inflict Nether Touch on enemies hit. The mark lasts 5s and causes the affected enemy to explode upon death, dealing 108-500 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies (base damage scales with level).

skills.name: Deathly Grasp
skills.desc: Vexana unleashes a Deathly Grasp in the target direction, dealing 200 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in its path. It stops upon hitting an enemy hero, Terrifying and knocking them back. After a short delay, it explodes, dealing 200 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies and Terrifying them for 1s.

skills.name: Cursed Blast
skills.desc: Vexana marks the target area with the power of the undead, striking it after a 0.8s delay and dealing 580 (+165% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit.

skills.name: Eternal Guard
skills.desc: Vexana summons an Eternal Guard at the target location, dealing 480 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit and knocking them airborne for 0.8s. The Eternal Guard then joins Vexana in battle for 15s. Its attacks deal 200 (+40% Total Magic Power) (+ 5% target's Max HP) Magic Damage and Splash Damage in a large area.

strong.desc: Lapu-Lapu is strong against squishy heroes who lack movement abilities, such as Layla and Eudora, because he has high burst damage and can use his mobility to get on top of them.

weak.desc: Lapu-Lapu is countered by agile Marksmen who can knock him away, such as Lesley and Moskov. They can speed up or blink to avoid Lapu-Lapu's Ultimate, and use knockback effects to interrupt his 1st Skill in Great Sword stance or to keep him away.

assist.desc: Lapu-Lapu works well with heroes who can pull enemies together, like Tigreal and Atlas, to allow his AOE skills to hit more targets and get more Damage Reduction from his 2nd Skill in Great Sword stance by hitting more heroes.

Lapu-Lapu.story: Hero who defends the Vonetis Islands from outsiders

skills.name: Homeland Defender
skills.desc: Lapu-Lapu accumulates 10 Bravery Blessing each time he deals damage (halved for damage dealt to non-hero units). When Bravery Blessing is full, Lapu-Lapu's next Basic Attack will make him dash toward the target, dealing  (+150% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage (cannot Crit) and granting him a shield that absorbs 250 (+65*Hero Level) damage for 2.5s.

skills.name: Justice Blades
skills.desc:
Lapu-Lapu releases two boomerangs in the target direction, each boomerang dealing 140 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit on its way out and back.

Enemies hit by both boomerangs take 50% damage from the second hit.

skills.name: Jungle Warrior
skills.desc: Lapu-Lapu dashes in the target direction, dealing 100 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies along the way.

skills.name: Bravest Fighter
skills.desc:
Lapu-Lapu leaps to the target location while combining his twin blades into one, dealing 300 (+80% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies upon landing and slowing them by 60% for 1s. (This skill cannot be interrupted.)
Lapu-Lapu gains 3 times the Bravery Blessing from this skill and skills in transformed state.
For the next 10s, Lapu-Lapu enters Great Sword stance and gains enhanced skills, 30 extra Physical & Magic Defense, and his Basic Attack damage is increased to 120%.

skills.name: Homeland Defender
skills.desc: Lapu-Lapu accumulates 10 Bravery Blessing each time he deals damage (halved for damage dealt to non-hero units). When Bravery Blessing is full, Lapu-Lapu's next Basic Attack will make him dash toward the target, dealing  (+150% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage (cannot Crit) and granting him a shield that absorbs 250 (+65*Hero Level) damage for 2.5s.

skills.name: Land Shaker
skills.desc: Lapu-Lapu channels for 0.7s, slowing enemies in the target direction by 60%. He then slams his heavy sword to the ground, dealing 250 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit and stunning them for 1s. (This skill can only be interrupted by Suppression.)

skills.name: Storm Sword
skills.desc:
Lapu-Lapu whirls his heavy sword, dealing 250 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies.

When hitting an enemy hero, Lapu-Lapu gains 20% Damage Reduction, plus an additional 8% for each extra enemy hero hit, lasting 3s.

skills.name: Raging Slash
skills.desc:
Lapu-Lapu swings his heavy sword 3 times. The first two attacks deal 345 (+160% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage each, while the third deals 440 (+210% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in a large area.

Lapu-Lapu gains Control Immunity during this process and can change his attack direction and move a short distance on each attack.

strong.desc: Aurora counters heroes who like to charge in, such as Leomord, Thamuz, and Terizla, since her skills can stop them dead in their tracks.

weak.desc: Because most of Aurora's skills have delayed effects, she is countered by high mobility heroes who can dodge them with ease, such as Ling, Kadita, and Guinevere.

assist.desc: With her very powerful CC abilities, Aurora works well with heroes who deal high sustained damage, like Ixia, Freya, and Karrie.

Aurora.story: The Maiden of the Glacier who grants eternal remembrance in the legends of the North.

skills.name: Pride of Ice
skills.desc:
Upon taking fatal damage, Aurora freezes herself for 1.5s, becoming invincible and gradually recovering 30% Max HP. Cooldown: 150s.

Aurora's freeze effects can also affect Turrets.

skills.name: Hailstone Blast
skills.desc: Aurora summons an icy meteorite to strike at the target location, dealing 400 (+90% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and slowing targets hit by 40% for 1s. Afterward, 5 hailstones fall, each dealing 40 (+10% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage.

skills.name: Frosty Breeze
skills.desc:
Aurora blows Frosty Breeze in a fan-shaped area in the target direction, dealing 225 (+75% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit and creating a frozen area at the far end.

The area deals a total of 225 (+75% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies within and freezes them for 1s (increases by 0.06s for every 100 Magic Power).

skills.name: Frigid Glacier
skills.desc:
Aurora creates a frost path in the target direction, dealing 100 (+40% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in the path and slowing them by 80% for 1.2s. 

The frost path gradually expands into a glacier that shatters at maximum size, dealing 600 (+150% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to all enemies in the area and freezing them for 1s (increases by 0.2s for every 100 Magic Power).

strong.desc: Hilda excels in prolonged battles. She works well against heroes with lower burst damage. Such as Zilong and Layla.
