weak.desc: Hilda is countered by heroes with high sustain. Such as Thamuz and Uranus. This is because they can utilize their healing abilities to prevent being executed.

assist.desc: Hilda works well with tanky heroes, which can create room for her to dish out more damage. Such as Atlas and Tigreal.

Hilda.story: Inheritor of the Power of the Megalith.

skills.name: Blessing of Wilderness
skills.desc:
Hilda recovers 2% HP per second while in the bush. Entering a bush enhances her next Basic Attack, which deals 110 (+130% Total Physical Attack)(+12*Hero Level) Physical Damage to the target, reduces the target's Movement Speed by 40%, and deals 40 (+100% Total Physical Attack)(+10*Hero Level) Physical Damage to enemies behind the target. After using the enhanced Basic Attack, this effect enters a 6s cooldown.

Hilda's Basic Attack and skills apply a Wilderness Mark on hit, which lasts up to 4s and stacks up to 6 times. Each stack reduces the enemy hero's Physical Defense and Magic Defense by 3%.

skills.name: Combat Ritual
skills.desc: Hilda gains 60% Movement Speed for 3s and receives a shield that absorbs damage equal to 10% Max HP, lasting 3s.

skills.name: Art of Hunting
skills.desc: Hilda locks onto an enemy target, dealing 220 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage per hit. This skill can be cast up to 3 times. The 2nd attack deals extra damage to nearby enemies. The 3rd attack deals damage to the target and nearby enemies, knocking them back briefly.

skills.name: Power of Wildness
skills.desc:
Hilda launches a powerful slash at the target enemy, dealing 600 (+250% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage and stunning them for 0.2s. Nearby enemies take 60% of the damage.

Each stack of Wilderness Mark increases Power of Wildness's damage by 2.5% of the target's Max HP. At max stacks, the extra damage increases to 20% of the target's Max HP.

strong.desc: Estes counters heroes with no burst damage, he can easily heal through minor damages from heroes like Faramis and Alice.

weak.desc: Estes is countered by heroes with high burst damage. Heroes like Saber and Eudora can easily burst down Estes or the hero he protects in a few seconds.

assist.desc: Estes works best with typical Marksman heroes, his healing will allow them to deal more damage. Heroes like Lesley and Hanabi are good examples here.

Estes.story: Elf King who rules the Moon Elves.

skills.name: Scripture of the Moon Elf
skills.desc: Scripture of the Moon Elf slowly charges energy for Estes. Upon reaching 100 stacks, it enhances his next Basic Attack, dealing 250 (+100% Total Physical Attack) (+150% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to the target. The attack will ricochet to nearby enemies, dealing 125 (+50% Total Physical Attack) (+75% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and slowing them by 60% for 1.5s.

skills.name: Moonlight Immersion
skills.desc:
Estes recovers 200 (+110% Total Magic Power) HP for the target allied hero immediately and links himself with them for 5s. While linked, both heroes gain 15 Hybrid Defense, and the target slowly recovers 360 (+90% Total Magic Power) HP. Linking with an allied hero increases Estes' Movement Speed by 15%, as well as the energy charging speed of the Scripture of the Moon Elf.

Being too far from the target will break the link.

skills.name: Domain of Moon Goddess
skills.desc: Estes drops a flood of moonlight on the target area, dealing 350 (+70% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemy units within it. Afterwards, it turns to a domain of the Moon Goddess and reveals enemies inside it. Enemies will be slowed by 90% for 1.5s if they touch the edge of the domain area. (The slow effect will decay over time.)

skills.name: Blessing of Moon Goddess
skills.desc:
Estes casts an enhanced Moonlight Immersion on himself and all nearby allied heroes, refreshing Moonlight Immersion on them and enhancing it for 8s.

Enhanced Moonlight Immersion: Doubles the Instant Basic Healing and Hybrid Defense Increase, and heals the target for 1020 (+225% Total Magic Power) HP over time.

strong.desc: Cyclops' short skill cooldown makes him strong against mid-ranged heroes with poor Mobility, such as Terizla and Alpha.

weak.desc: Cyclops lacks Dash or Blink skills, making him the preferred target for heroes with gap closers and CC skills. Such as Lancelot, Alucard and Kagura.

assist.desc: Cyclops is best with tanky heroes with strong CC skills, like Belerick and Minsitthar.

Cyclops.story: A celestial traveler in the universe.

skills.name: Starlit Hourglass
skills.desc: Each time Cyclops hits an enemy with his skills, all his skill cooldowns are reduced by 0.5s.

skills.name: Stardust Shock
skills.desc: Cyclops casts two Stardust Disks in the target direction, each dealing 240 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in its path.

skills.name: Planets Attack
skills.desc: Cyclops conjures Starlit Spheres around himself, gaining 50% Movement Speed that decays over 2s. The spheres automatically seek and attack nearby enemies (prioritizes enemy heroes), exploding on collision to deal 200 (+65% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage. Damage is reduced for subsequent hits on the same target.

skills.name: Star Power Lockdown
skills.desc: Cyclops creates a Planetary Sphere that tracks the target enemy hero or creep, dealing 500 (+120% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and immobilizing them for 0.75-1.5s (scales with travel distance).

strong.desc:
Johnson counters melee heroes with high burst like Alucard and Zilong
because of Johnson's high Physical Defense and CC ability.

weak.desc: Johnson is countered by agile heroes who deal Magic Damage such as Harith and Kagura because they can easily dodge Johnson's skills while their damage is not affected by his Physical Defense.

assist.desc: Johnson works well with heroes that need help initiating like Odette and Kadita since they can just jump into Johnson's Ultimate for a ride.

Johnson.story: A super cyborg who can transform into a car and lead his teammates into battle.

skills.name: Electro Airbag
skills.desc: When Johnson's HP drops below 30%, he gains a 300 (+700% Total Physical Defense) shield for 10s. Cooldown: 100s.

skills.name: Impact Wrench
skills.desc: Johnson throws his wrench toward the target location, dealing 120 (+220% Total Physical Defense) Magic Damage to enemies in its path. Enemies around the landing point are stunned for 0.8s.

skills.name: Electromagnetic Waves
skills.desc:
Johnson raises his shield, continuously dealing 120 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in a fan-shaped area in the target direction, reducing their Movement Speed and Magic Defense by 10% (stacks up to 3 times) for 2s.
While his shield is raised, Johnson can perform Basic Attacks and cast skills.

skills.name: Full Throttle
skills.desc:
Passive: Johnson's Physical Defense is increased by 10%.
Active: Johnson transforms into a car. Nearby allied heroes can use Hop In to ride with him (only 1 at a time). When the car hits an enemy hero, it explodes and deals 300 (+240% Total Physical Defense)–450 (+360% Total Physical Defense) Magic Damage to nearby enemies, stunning them for 0.5–1s (scales with driving time). It also creates an electrified zone at the location that continuously deals 60 (+20% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage and slows enemies within. Johnson's location is revealed to enemies for 3s when he casts this skill.

strong.desc: Moskov's 1st Skill provides a blink effect, making him a threat to fighters with poor mobility like Thamuz and Balmond.

weak.desc: Moskov is countered by heroes with high mobility, such as Lancelot and Natalia, who can quickly close the gap and take him out.

assist.desc: Moskov works well with heroes like Atlas and Gatotkaca who can CC groups of enemies so he can focus on maximizing the damage of his piercing AOE.

Moskov.story: A warrior of the desert that embraced the darkness to seek his revenge.

skills.name: Spear of Quiescence
skills.desc: Moskov's Basic Attacks can penetrate the target and deal  (+68% Total Physical Attack)- (+110% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies behind them (scales with hero level). Each Basic Attack hit reduces the cooldowns of Abyss Walker by 0.8s and Spear of Misery by 0.8s.

skills.name: Abyss Walker
skills.desc: Moskov teleports to the target location, increasing his Attack Speed to 1.15 times for 3s. Meanwhile, his Basic Attack deals  (+10% Total Physical Attack) more damage to enemies behind the primary target.

skills.name: Spear of Misery
skills.desc:
Moskov launches a powerful strike at the target enemy hero or Creep, dealing 300 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage, knocking them back, and revealing their position for 2s.

If the target collides with an enemy hero when knocked back, they'll both take 200 (+30% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and be stunned for 1.2s. If the enemy is knocked into a wall, they will also be stunned for the same duration.

skills.name: Spear of Destruction
skills.desc:
After a short delay, Moskov throws the Spear of Destruction in the target direction, dealing 340 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in its path and to the first enemy hero hit. Upon hitting an enemy hero, the spear pierces them, dealing 10% extra Physical Damage to enemies behind them. The target and enemies hit by the piercing damage will be slowed by 50% for 1s (the slow duration scales with the spear's travel time, up to 3s).

When Spear of Destruction hits the first enemy hero, it creates a shadow behind them and refreshes the cooldown of Abyss Walker.

Use Again: Moskov casts Abyss Walker from the shadow's location (shares the same cooldown as the skill).

strong.desc: Yi Sun-shin counters heroes that lack CC like Uranus and Terizla who are at the mercy of his ability to jump in and out of melee range using his 2 weapons.

weak.desc: Yi Sun-shin is countered by heroes with CC skills, such as Franco and Kaja who can lock him down and stop him from freely swapping weapons.

assist.desc: Yi Sun-shin works best with Fighters who can initiate, such as Alpha and Khaleed, so he can follow up and unleash his full damage after they have CC'd the enemy.

Yi Sun-shin.story: Leader of the City of the Dragon Fleet.

skills.name: Heavenly Vow
skills.desc:
Yi Sun-shin attacks with his longbow or glaive based on his distance from the target.
Weapon Mastery: After each weapon swap, his next 2 Basic Attacks gain extra Attack Speed and deal 70% and 55% Crit Damage respectively. He also gains 15% Movement Speed for 1s.

skills.name: Traceless
skills.desc:
Yi Sun-shin dashes forward and slashes with his glaive, dealing 150 (+40% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and gaining Control Immunity for 0.4s.
Each Weapon Mastery triggered reduces the cooldown of Traceless by 1s.

skills.name: Blood Floods
skills.desc:
Tap: Yi Sun-shin slashes swiftly with his glaive, dealing 240 (+80% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.
Hold: Yi Sun-shin shoots a powerful arrow forward, dealing 240 (+80% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the target (damage scales with hold time, up to 200%).
Casting this skill immediately triggers Weapon Mastery.

skills.name: Mountain Shocker
skills.desc:
Yi Sun-shin summons a turtle ship and reveals the whole map for 2s. The turtle ship lasts up to 10s, during which he gains 70% burst Movement Speed (decays to 20% over 2s).

Use Again: Yi Sun-shin commands the turtle ship to ram the target area, dealing 160 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in the center and stunning them for 0.8s. Meanwhile, he commands the naval fleet to launch 3 waves of cannon attacks over a larger area. Each wave deals 160 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and slows enemies by 30%.
Casting this skill immediately triggers Weapon Mastery.

strong.desc: Ruby is strong against Fighter heroes with high burst damage. She can easily survive their burst with her excellent Regen and Lifesteal abilities. Such as Aldous and Guinevere.

weak.desc: Ruby is countered by Marksman heroes with good Mobility. Because they can easily dodge her 2nd and Ultimate skills. Like Wanwan and Irithel.

assist.desc: Ruby works best with heroes who can deal high AOE burst damage. Their skills can deal more damage to enemies who are stunned and pulled by her 2nd and Ultimate skills. Such as Pharsa and Kadita.

Ruby.story: A girl who grew up with Roger's help.

skills.name: Let's Dance!
skills.desc:
Ruby starts with 6%-20% Spell Vamp (scales with level) and her skills have a 125% Spell Vamp Ratio (but her Basic Attacks cannot trigger Lifesteal).

After each skill cast, Ruby can dash to another location and gains 8-25 Physical & Magic Defense (scales with level) for 4s (stacks up to 3 times).

skills.name: Be Good!
skills.desc: Ruby swings her scythe, dealing 90 (+70% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit. She then unleashes a shockwave in the same direction, dealing 90 (+70% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit and slowing them by 40% for 1s.

skills.name: Don't Run, Wolf King!
skills.desc: Ruby swings her scythe around herself twice, each swing dealing 60 (+55% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies, stunning them for 0.5s, and slowly pulling them toward her.

skills.name: I'm Offended!
skills.desc: Ruby sweeps her scythe in the target direction, dealing 200 (+200% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit, pulling them toward her, and stunning them for 0.5s.

strong.desc: Alpha counters heroes that lack mobility, such as Layla and Ixia, because these heroes have a hard time avoiding his skills.

weak.desc: Alpha is countered by heroes that are very mobile, such as Chou and Gusion, because they have a lot of ways to dodge Alpha's skills.

assist.desc: Alpha works well with heroes that can initiate with CC like Vexana and Selena. With their help, he'll have a much easier time landing his skills.

Alpha.story: A pure cyborg made by Laboratory 1718.

skills.name: Beta, Advance!
skills.desc:
When Alpha uses a skill, Beta will be summoned to attack. Enemies that are hit by Beta 2 time(s) will be Locked On.

Locked On: After Alpha deals damage to the target, Beta will fire a laser at them, dealing 80 (+80% Extra Physical Attack) (+8*Hero Level) True Damage and briefly applying a powerful slow effect.
This skill only deals 75% damage to Creeps.

skills.name: Rotary Impact
skills.desc:
Alpha unleashes an energy wave in the target direction, dealing 100 (+90% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in its path and slowing them by 40% for 1s.

Beta then strafes enemies along the same path, dealing 60 (+30% Extra Physical Attack) True Damage.

skills.name: Force Swing
skills.desc:
After a short delay, Alpha swings his blade in the target direction, dealing 180 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in a fan-shaped area and restoring 40 (+30% Extra Physical Attack) HP for each enemy hit.

Beta then strafes enemies in the same area, dealing 60 (+30% Total Physical Attack) True Damage.

skills.name: Spear of Alpha
skills.desc:
Beta dives to the target location, dealing 100 (+20% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit and stunning them for 1s. Alpha then leaps towards the same location, knocking enemies in his path airborne and carrying them to the landing location. Alpha then slams down, dealing 250 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage and slowing the enemies by 40% for 1.5s.

Beta then attacks the same area 5 times, dealing 15 (+30% Extra Physical Attack) True Damage each time.

strong.desc: Sun can deal great sustained damage and has armor-busting capabilities, making him a good counter to tanks with low damage output, such as Franco and Gatotkaca.

weak.desc: Fighter heroes with Lifesteal can counter Sun, however. For example, heroes like Ruby can steal a lot of HP by hitting Sun and his Dopplegangers.

assist.desc: Pair Sun with tanks who have strong CC abilities such as Belerick or Tigreal to create opportunities for Sun to deal damage.

Sun.story: The immortal Monkey King, resolute and unyielding like the mountain that bore him.

skills.name: Simian God
skills.desc:
Enemies hit by Sun and his Doppelgangers will have their Physical Defense reduced by 5% (stacks up to 6 times). Sun recovers HP equal to 75 plus 25% of the Doppelganger's Physical Attack each time a Doppelganger deals damage.
Sun and his Doppelgangers' Basic Attacks deal 50 extra damage to Creeps.

skills.name: Endless Variety
skills.desc:
Sun hurls his Golden Staff in the target direction, dealing 200 (+40% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in its path. Upon hitting an enemy hero or Creep, or reaching the maximum range, the Golden Staff morphs into a Doppelganger that lasts 5s and inherits 40% of Sun's attributes (and a portion of his Attack Effects).

Endless Variety and Swift Exchange are learned and upgraded together and share the same cooldown.

skills.name: Swift Exchange
skills.desc:
Sun hurls his Golden Staff in the target direction, dealing 200 (+40% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in its path.
He conjures a Doppelganger at his location that lasts 5s and inherits 40% of his attributes (and a portion of Attack Effects). Meanwhile, Sun conceals himself and moves with the Golden Staff.

skills.name: Instantaneous Move
skills.desc: Sun commands his Doppelgangers to strike at the target enemy with him, dealing 270 (+40% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and slowing the target and enemies behind them by 20% (can be stacked) for 1.5s. This counts as Basic Attack damage and can trigger Attack Effects but cannot Crit.

skills.name: Clone Techniques
skills.desc: Sun summons a Doppelganger that lasts 8s and inherits 70% of his attributes and a portion of his Attack Effects. The Doppelganger takes increased damage.

strong.desc: Chou counters slower Fighters like Terizla and Alpha that have a hard time dealing with his quick hit and run tactics.

weak.desc: Chou is countered by heroes with very long-range like Lesley and Pharsa that can take out a chunk of his HP before he can get close.

assist.desc: Chou works best with Healers that can boost his survivability and speed, such as Angela and Rafaela.

Chou.story: A talented fighter with a heart of chivalry.

skills.name: Only Fast
skills.desc: After traveling 5 units, Chou deals 180% damage on his next Basic Attack (cannot Crit), briefly slows the target by 90% and reduces their Physical Defense by 10(+1*Hero Level) for 3s (up to 2 stacks).

skills.name: Jeet Kune Do
skills.desc:
Chou strikes in the target direction, dealing 180 (+70% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit. This skill can be cast 3 times before it goes on cooldown. On the 3rd cast, Chou also knocks enemies hit airborne.

Hitting an enemy hero with the 3rd cast resets the cooldown of Shunpo.

skills.name: Shunpo
skills.desc: Chou charges in the target direction, gaining Control Immunity and a 200 (+200% Extra Physical Attack) shield.

skills.name: The Way of Dragon
skills.desc:
Chou strikes a roundhouse kick at the target enemy hero, dealing them 400 (+200% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage and knocking them back.

Cast the skill again to chase up the target, dealing a total of 480 (+240% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage.

strong.desc: Kagura's Ultimate can deal high burst damage, making her strong against back-row heroes with no dash or blink, such as Layla and Eudora.

weak.desc: Kagura is countered by heroes with more than one control immunity abilities that allow them to escape her various CC skills, heroes like Joy and Martis are good examples.

assist.desc: Kagura works well with tank heroes with strong CC skills that allow her to deal more damage with her Ultimate, such as Atlas and Tigreal.

Kagura.story: A young spirit born of the deep longings stored within the Seimei Umbrella, she has finally stepped out of her dream, like a radiant bloom, in defiance of the dark night.

skills.name: Yin Yang Gathering
skills.desc: Upon retrieving the Seimei Umbrella, Kagura gains a 450 (+80% Total Magic Power) shield, stuns nearby enemies for 0.5s, and slows them by 60% for 1s. Cooldown: 4.5s.

skills.name: Seimei Umbrella Open
skills.desc:
Kagura sends the Seimei Umbrella to the target location, dealing 365 (+105% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in its path and slowing them by 60% for 0.5s. The Umbrella will automatically return to Kagura when she's too far away.
This skill deals 130% damage to non-hero enemies.

skills.name: Rasho Umbrella Flee
skills.desc: Kagura removes all debuffs on her and leaps in the target direction, while leaving the Seimei Umbrella behind.

skills.name: Yin Yang Overturn
skills.desc: Kagura invokes the power of the Seimei Umbrella, dealing 250 (+95% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies, knocking them back, and slowing them by 60% for 0.5s.

skills.name: Yin Yang Gathering
skills.desc: Upon retrieving the Seimei Umbrella, Kagura gains a 450 (+80% Total Magic Power) shield, stuns nearby enemies for 0.5s, and slows them by 60% for 1s. Cooldown: 4.5s.

skills.name: Seimei Umbrella Open
skills.desc:
Kagura sends the Seimei Umbrella to the target location, dealing 365 (+105% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in its path and slowing them by 60% for 0.5s. The Umbrella will automatically return to Kagura when she's too far away.
This skill deals 130% damage to non-hero enemies.

skills.name: Rasho Umbrella Flee
skills.desc: Kagura teleports to the Seimei Umbrella's location and retrieves it, dealing 300 (+90% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to the nearby enemies.

skills.name: Yin Yang Overturn
skills.desc: Kagura resets the cooldown of Seimei Umbrella Open, dealing 235 (+60% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies around the Seimei Umbrella and slowing them by 60%. After 1.5s, enemy heroes around the umbrella are pulled to the center and take 480 (+155% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage.

strong.desc: Natalia counters squishy heroes who lack CC and burst like as Novaria and Irithel since they can't stop Natalia's burst damage.

weak.desc: Natalia is countered by heroes who can restrict her mobility like Minsitthar and Kaja since they can easily shutdown her main advantage.

assist.desc: Natalia works best with heroes with high AOE damage such as Yi Sun-shin and Vexana. She can stay hidden while they poke the enemy, then pounce on targets that get low on HP.

Natalia.story: An assassin exiled by the Empire.

skills.name: Assassin Instinct
skills.desc:
If Natalia stays in a bush without taking damage for 1s, she will enter Camouflage state and gain 15% Movement Speed. In this state, Natalia's next Basic Attack will teleport her behind her target and deal 200 (+110% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage while silencing the target for 0.25s.

Dealing damage, taking damage or using skills will immediately end the Camouflage state, but her next Basic Attack will remain enhanced for another 1s.

Natalia's base Crit Damage is reduced to 150%, but her damage to Creeps is increased to 150%.

skills.name: Claw Dash
skills.desc:
Natalia dashes in the target direction, dealing 250 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in her path.

After hitting an enemy, she can cast this skill again within 5s.

skills.name: Smoke Bomb
skills.desc: Natalia releases a Smoke Bomb at her location, slowing enemies within by 25% while Natalia becomes immune to enemy Basic Attacks for 4s.

skills.name: The Hunt
skills.desc:
Passive: Natalia gains 15% Crit Chance.

Active: Natalia immediately enters the Assassin Instinct state. She can store up to 2 Ultimate charges, and each charge has a recharge time of 30s. Killing creeps reduces the remaining recharge time by 50% of the full recharge time.

strong.desc: Gord is strong against melee heroes with poor Mobility, such as Barats and Fredrinn.

weak.desc: Gord is countered by heroes with gap closers, such as Fanny and Lancelot.

assist.desc: Gord works well with tanky heroes with reliable CCs, like Hylos and Belerick.

Gord.story: Dean of the Icarus School of the Magic Academy.

skills.name: Mystic Favor
skills.desc: After Gord hits an enemy 4 times with skills within a short period of time, the next skill damage will deal an additional 140 (+8*Hero Level)  (+40% Total Magic Power) True Damage and slow them by 20% for 1s (the slow effect stacks up to 2 times).

skills.name: Mystic Projectile
skills.desc: Gord conjures a Mystic Orb that bounces in the target direction and explodes on the first enemy hit, dealing 225 (+65% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in the area, while stunning them and revealing their locations for 1s.

skills.name: Mystic Injunction
skills.desc: Gord creates an energy field in the target area, dealing 30 (+10% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage every 0.25s to enemies within (up to 16 times).

skills.name: Mystic Gush
skills.desc:
Gord unleashes an energy beam in the target direction, dealing 110 (+32% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit every 0.2s (up to 16 times).

The direction of the beam can be adjusted using the Joystick.

strong.desc: Freya not only has exceptional mobility, she also excels in prolonged engagements, making her highly effective against fragile, low-HP Marksmen, such as Lesley and Ixia. Her excellent sustained damage and mobility also allows her to counter Tanks with low damage, like Uranus and Baxia.

weak.desc: However, all of Freya's skills have movement components, making her vulnerable to Phoveus's unique counter. Using her skills near Phoveus will allow him to quickly gain energy for his enhanced Basic Attacks. Freya is vulnerable to CC and movement-disabling effects, so Minsitthar and Obsidia's Ultimates can lock her down completely.

assist.desc: In order for Freya to maximize her damage, she prefers heroes with CC like Belerick and Gatotkaca to lock down the enemy for her.

Freya.story: The Valkyrie who guards the Northern Vale.

skills.name: Power of Einherjar
skills.desc:
Freya gains 1 Sacred Orb with each Basic Attack (up to 6 Sacred Orbs). Consuming 1 Sacred Orb grants Freya 100% Attack Speed for her next Basic Attack (max 6 stacks).
Sacred Orbs gradually recover when out of combat.

skills.name: Leap of Faith
skills.desc: Freya leaps to the target area and strikes the ground with her shield, gaining 500 (+100% Total Physical Attack) shield and dealing 200 (+80% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies within while knocking them airborne for 0.4s.

skills.name: Valkyrie Slash
skills.desc:
Freya charges forward and slashes, dealing 150 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and slowing enemies by 30% for 0.5s. This skill has no base cooldown but consumes 2 Sacred Orbs per cast.
This skill deals 50% damage to Creeps.

skills.name: Valkyrie Descent
skills.desc:
Freya selects a target area and dives down as a Sacred Orb (gaining Control Immunity and 50% Damage Reduction), dealing 300 (+75% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and 40% slow to enemies in her path and at the landing point for 2s.

Upon landing, Freya enters the Valkyrie state for 12s, during which her Basic Attacks deal 30 extra damage (extra damage cannot Crit) and she recovers 40 (+40% Total Physical Attack) HP per Basic Attack, and her Basic Attacks deal splash damage.

strong.desc: Hayabusa counters squishy heroes that lack CC to shutdown the blinks from his 2nd Skill, and who are also easy targets for his Ultimate, such as Chang'e and Cecilion.

weak.desc: Hayabusa is countered by heroes with targeted CC like Franco and Kaja who can lock him down before he can blink away.

assist.desc: Hayabusa works best with Fighters like Lapu-Lapu and Alpha who can initiate with him to quickly focus down key targets.

Hayabusa.story: The young leader of the Shadow Sect who wields his blade through moonless nights, walking the path of nindo without fear.

skills.name: Ninjutsu: Trace of Shadow
skills.desc: Hayabusa's attacks apply a stack of Shadow Mark on hit (up to 4 stacks). Each stack lasts 6s and increases Hayabusa's damage to the enemy by 5%.

skills.name: Ninjutsu: Phantom Shuriken
skills.desc:
Passive: Hayabusa permanently gains 3% Spell Vamp.
Active: Hayabusa throws three returning shurikens in the target direction, each dealing 220 (+75% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit and slowing them by 35% for 2s. Subsequent hits on the same enemy deal 70% less damage (only 50% less to creeps). He also restores 10 Energy when hitting non-minion enemies.

skills.name: Ninjutsu: Quad Shadow
skills.desc:
Hayabusa dashes in the target direction and releases four phantoms that travel in separate directions. The phantoms will remain at the end of their paths or attach themselves to the first enemy hero hit, dealing 130 (+30% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage and slowing them by 40% for 2s. Hayabusa will immediately stop if he hits an enemy hero during the dash.

Use Again: Hayabusa teleports to a phantom's location and reduces the cooldown of Ninjutsu: Phantom Shuriken by 1s. If the phantom is attached to an enemy hero, he also deals 130 (+30% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the enemy.

skills.name: Ougi: Shadow Kill
skills.desc:
Hayabusa blends into the shadows and launches 6 single-target attacks on enemies in the area, each dealing 140 (+32% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage.

The attacks prioritize enemies with Shadow Mark and each will consume a mark to deal 90 (+40% Extra Physical Attack) extra Physical Damage. Shadow Kill does not apply Shadow Mark.

strong.desc: Lolita's shield makes her strong against heroes who rely on projectiles and ranged skills, such as Kimmy and Cyclops.

weak.desc: Lolita is countered by melee heroes with high burst damage like Ling and Paquito.

assist.desc: Due to her powerful defensive and CC abilities, Lolita is great at protecting Marksmen like Hanabi and Layla.

Lolita.story: Member of the Eruditio Rangers and protector of the Leviathan workshop.

skills.name: Noumenon Energy Core
skills.desc: Every 3s to 6s (scales with Noumenon Blast level), the Noumenon Energy Core grants Lolita 20 (+2.5% Total HP) (+20% Total Magic Power) shield. When Lolita casts a skill, the Noumenon Energy Core grants Lolita and nearby allied heroes an extra shield. This shield can stack up to 3 times and lasts 30s.

skills.name: Power Charge
skills.desc: Lolita dashes in the target direction. Her next Basic Attack within 4s will perform a short dash to the target, dealing 400 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and stunning them for 0.5s. This damage is doubled against Minions.

skills.name: Guardian's Reflection
skills.desc:
Lolita raises her shield and reflects all incoming ranged Basic Attacks and Projectiles in the direction of the shield for 3s. The shield will break after taking 1000 (+15% Total HP) damage.

The damage and effects from the Unique Passives of an attacker's equipment will not apply to the shield and will not be reflected. This skill can be interrupted by high level control effects.

skills.name: Noumenon Blast
skills.desc:
Passive: Reduces the interval of Noumenon Energy Core by 1s each time this skill is upgraded.

Active: Lolita begins charging for 2s while slowing enemies in a fan-shaped area by 60%. When the charging is complete or is stopped, Lolita slams her hammer on the ground, dealing up to 600 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in the area and stunning them for up to 2s (damage and stun duration scale with charge time).

Use Again: Lolita immediately stops charging and slams her hammer on the ground.

strong.desc: Minotaur is strong against heroes that rely on chaining skills or attacks, like Paquito and Julian because he can interrupt their combos with his CC.

weak.desc: Minotaur is countered by long-range Marksmen like Layla and Lesley since his lack of speed makes him an easy target.

assist.desc: Minotaur works best with burst damage Marksmen, such as Granger and Melissa, since his CC's give them more opportunities to deal damage.

Minotaur.story: Minoan prince determined to restore his homeland.

skills.name: Rage Incarnate
skills.desc:
After casting a skill, Minotaur gains Motivation, recovering HP equal to 5 (+1*Hero Level)% of damage taken from enemy heroes for 3s.

Enraged State: Motivation HP recovery is increased to 200%.

skills.name: Despair Stomp
skills.desc:
Minotaur jumps to the target location, dealing 280 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in the area and briefly knocking them airborne. Enemies hit will also be slowed by 40% while Minotaur enhances his Basic Attacks to deal  (+3% Total HP) extra Physical Damage for 2s.

Enraged: Increases the AOE range and the damage dealt by  (+3% Total HP) Physical Damage.

skills.name: Motivation Roar
skills.desc:
Minotaur heals himself and the nearby ally with the lowest HP, recovering 220 (+60% Total Magic Power) HP for both of them and healing himself for an additional 8% of his lost HP.

Enraged: Minotaur heals himself and all nearby allied heroes.

skills.name: Minoan Fury
skills.desc:
Minotaur enters Enraged state and smashes the ground 3 times. The first 2 hits deal 180 (+85% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies and slow them by 60%, while the final hit deals 200 (+85% Total Physical Attack) True Damage and knocks targets airborne for 0.8s. Minotaur is immune to control effects during this attack.

The Enraged state lasts 12s and enhances Minotaur's skills for the duration.

strong.desc: Layla's relatively longer attack range lets her counter slow Tanks like Tigreal and Belerick.

weak.desc: Layla is countered by Assassins with high burst damage, such as Karina and Ling, as they can get the jump on Layla and melt her easily.

assist.desc: Layla works best with Tanks that can protect backline heroes, such as Minsitthar and Belerick, since their protection gives Layla more opportunities to deal damage.

Layla.story: The backbone of the Eruditio Rangers who is also known as the Shining Star.

skills.name: Malefic Gun
skills.desc: Layla deals increased damage to enemies farther away from her (starting at 100% and increasing to 115% at 6 units away). This does not include Turrets.

skills.name: Malefic Bomb
skills.desc:
Layla fires a Malefic Bomb in the target direction, dealing 200 (+80% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the first enemy hit (can Crit).

Upon hitting an enemy, Layla's Basic Attacks and Void Projectile gain extra range for 3s, and she gains 60% extra Movement Speed that decays over 1.2s. The duration of the Movement Speed boost is doubled if an enemy hero is hit.

skills.name: Void Projectile
skills.desc:
Layla fires an orb of Malefic Energy at the target enemy that explodes on hit, dealing 170 (+65% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to targets in the area and applying a Magic Mark on them for 3s.
When Layla hits an enemy with a Magic Mark, she deals 100 (+35% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies and stuns them for 0.25s.

skills.name: Destruction Rush
skills.desc:
Passive: The range of Void Projectile and Basic Attacks is increased by 0.6 units. Layla's sight range is slightly increased each time this skill is upgraded. 
Active: Layla fires a blast of Malefic Energy in the target direction, dealing 500 (+150% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in a line.

strong.desc: Fanny counters squishy heroes that lack targeted CC like Karrie and Irithel who cannot stop Fanny's extreme mobility while she bursts them down.

weak.desc: Fanny is countered by heroes that can restrict mobility, such as Kaja and Khufra, since they can easily shutdown her main advantage.

assist.desc: Fanny works best with heroes that can chain AOE CC to keep the enemy pinned, such as Minotaur and Gatotkaca, so she can deal damage without interruption.

Fanny.story: A blade dancer in the battlefield.

skills.name: Air Superiority
skills.desc: During flight, Fanny deals 10%-20% extra damage to enemies hit (scales with flying speed) and applies a stack of Prey Mark to them (up to 2 stacks) on each attack. Her subsequent skill hits on marked enemy heroes restore 8 energy per stack for her. The energy restoration is reduced if she hits multiple enemy heroes within a short period of time.

skills.name: Tornado Strike
skills.desc: Fanny whirls her blades, dealing 275 (+85% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies.

skills.name: Steel Cable
skills.desc:
Fanny shoots a cable in the target direction that pulls her to the first obstacle hit. She can cast this skill again within 2s until her energy runs out. Each successive cast reduces the skill's energy cost by 2.

Fanny automatically casts Tornado Strike upon hitting an enemy mid-flight, as long as her energy is sufficient.

skills.name: Cut Throat
skills.desc: Fanny leaps at the target enemy hero or Creep with her blades, dealing 400 (+200% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage. Each stack of Prey Mark on the target increases this damage by 30%.

strong.desc: The immunity to Slow effects that Zilong gets from his Ultimate makes him strong against squishy heroes without CC skills, such as Layla and Lylia.

weak.desc: Zilong is still vulnerable to non-slow CC skills, making him weak against heroes with powerful single-target CC skills, such as Franco and Nana.

assist.desc: Zilong works best with heroes with strong defensive abilities that can hold off enemy attacks while he solos the lane, such as Pharsa and Hanabi.

Zilong.story: Guardian of the Dragon Altar and the Oriental Fighter who inherited the Cloud Dragon's power.

skills.name: Dragon Flurry
skills.desc:
After dealing damage 3 time(s) with Basic Attacks or skills, Zilong triggers Dragon Flurry on the next Basic Attack, hitting the target 3 time(s). Each hit deals 80 (+30% Total Physical Attack) Basic Attack Damage and heals himself for 50 (+20% Total Physical Attack) HP.

Zilong's Basic Attack deals 100 (+80% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.

If the target's HP is below 50%, all damage dealt by Zilong's skills and Basic Attacks is increased by 30.

skills.name: Spear Flip
skills.desc: Zilong flings the target enemy over his head, dealing 250 (+80% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.

skills.name: Spear Strike
skills.desc:
Zilong lunges at the target enemy, dealing 250 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and reducing their Physical Defense by 15 for 2s.

The cooldown of Spear Strike is reset each time Zilong kills an enemy.

skills.name: Supreme Warrior
skills.desc:
Zilong removes all slow effects on himself and gains 40% Movement Speed, 35% Attack Speed, and Slow Immunity for 7.5s.

During this time, Dragon Flurry triggers every 2 attacks (instead of the previous 3).

strong.desc: Eudora possesses strong targeted CC and burst capabilities, allowing her to keep squishy heroes like Moskov and Obsidia in check.

weak.desc: Eudora lacks self-preservation and struggles against dive heroes or Assassins who can negate targeted CC, such as Hayabusa and Kadita.

assist.desc: Eudora's skills can deal massive AOE damage, so pairing her with heroes who can group enemies, like Diggie and Tigreal, will make it easier to maximize her damage.

Eudora.story: The elven mage who commands lightning at her fingertips to pierce prejudice and illuminate the path forward.

skills.name: Superconductor
skills.desc: Eudora's skills inflict Superconductor for 3s on non-minion units hit and can trigger additional effects against enemies affected by Superconductor.

skills.name: Forked Lightning
skills.desc:
Eudora casts Forked Lightning in a fan-shaped area, dealing 275 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage (increased to 200% against minions) to enemies within.

When hitting targets affected by Superconductor, Eudora forms a lightning chain with the target, gaining 40% Movement Speed during the chain for up to 1s.
The lightning chain deals 10 (+4% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage over time and an additional 275 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage when it ends. If this hit lands, it reduces this skill's cooldown by 1.5s.

skills.name: Ball Lightning
skills.desc:
Eudora hurls an orb of lightning at the target enemy, reducing their Magic Defense by 10 for 1.8s, dealing 300 (+50% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage, and stunning them for 1s.

If the target is affected by Superconductor, the lightning orb reduces the Magic Defense of nearby enemies, deals AOE damage centered on the target, and stuns all nearby enemies.

skills.name: Thunder’s Wrath
skills.desc:
Eudora calls down a blast of lightning on the target area, dealing 600 (+160% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to targets at the center. Then it strikes targets outside the center, dealing 300 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage.

Every time Thunder's Wrath hits a target affected by Superconductor, it triggers a Thunderburst centered on the Superconductor target after a short delay, dealing 330 (+110% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage.

strong.desc: Rafaela has great healing and decent mobility, and performs well against heroes that lack burst damage or mobility, such as Masha and Barats.

weak.desc: Rafaela struggles against bursty and agile Assassins, like Ling and Hayabusa.

assist.desc: When teamed up with Fighters like Freya and Lapu-Lapu, Rafaela can boost their offensive power and survivability.

Rafaela.story: An angel loved and respected by the people of this world.

skills.name: Divine Resurrection
skills.desc:
Rafaela can cast a special skill every 40s. After channeling for 2.5s, she instantly resurrects an ally and increases their Movement Speed by 30% for 5s (taking damage from heroes and Legend Creeps interrupts channeling and puts the skill on base cooldown).
This skill's cooldown is increased by 500% of the target's remaining respawn time, capped at 240s. This skill is not affected by Cooldown Reduction.

skills.name: Light of Retribution
skills.desc: Rafaela strikes the three nearest enemies with Light of Retribution, dealing them 225 (+120% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage, briefly revealing their positions, and slowing them by 40% for 1.5s. Enemies hit by Light of Retribution again within 6s will take 20% extra damage (this effect stacks up to 3 times).

skills.name: Holy Healing
skills.desc:
Rafaela calls upon Holy Light, recovering 100 (+35% Total Magic Power) HP for nearby allied heroes, plus an additional 150 (+65% Total Magic Power) HP for herself and the most injured allied hero in range. She also increases the Movement Speed of nearby allied heroes by 30% and grants Slow Immunity for 1s.

Every 10 point(s) of Magic Power will add 1% to the Movement Speed bonus.

skills.name: Holy Baptism
skills.desc: Rafaela unleashes the true power of Holy Light in the target direction, dealing 460 (+120% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies in a line and stunning them for 1.2s.

strong.desc: Clint counters heroes with comparatively shorter range like Moskov and Miya thanks to the superior range of his skills and Basic Attacks.

weak.desc: Clint is countered by Marksmen with burst damage like Melissa and Karrie since he lacks burst in the early and mid game to trade blows with them.

assist.desc: Clint works best with Supportsthat can enhance his kiting ability, such as Rafaela and Angela.

Clint.story: A sharpshooter full of righteousness and justice.

skills.name: Double Shot
skills.desc: After each skill cast, Clint's next Basic Attack within 4s penetrates a line of enemies, dealing 150 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage. This damage can trigger attack effects, and the part affected by Physical Attack can Crit.

skills.name: Quick Draw
skills.desc:
Clint fires 5 bullets in quick succession in the target direction, each dealing 250 (+85% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the enemy hit.

The bullets will spread evenly across enemies in a fan-shaped area. Enemies hit by multiple bullets will take less damage after the first bullet.

skills.name: Trapping Recoil
skills.desc:
Clint shoots a trap net in the target direction, slightly jumping back while dealing 140 (+30% Total Physical Attack) (+150% Total Magic Power) Physical Damage to the first enemy hit and immobilizing them for 1.2s.

Successfully hitting an enemy reduces the cooldown of this skill by 40%.

skills.name: Grenade Bombardment
skills.desc:
Clint launches a grenade in the target direction that explodes on the first enemy hit, dealing 280 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and slowing them by 50% for 0.5s.

Clint gains a grenade charge every 10s (up to 3).

strong.desc: Bruno is strong against Marksmen that are weaker in the early game like Hanabi and Miya who Bruno can out-trade thanks to his high damage.

weak.desc: Bruno is countered by Tanks with high Physical Defense like Gatotkaca and Fredrinn that take greatly reduced damage from his attacks.

assist.desc: Bruno is best with Supports that can force enemies to engage and allow him to make use of his early game damage, such as Franco and Khufra.

Bruno.story: The captain of the Eruditio Rangers that protects the city with his amazing football skills.

skills.name: Mecha Legs
skills.desc:
Bruno gains 2.5% extra Crit Chance each time he deals damage with his skills (up to 8 stacks).

Bruno has higher Physical Attack but only gains 80% extra Attack Speed from all sources.

skills.name: Volley Shot
skills.desc:
Bruno kicks a Powerball on his next Basic Attack, dealing 100 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the target, slowing them by 30%, and speeding up by 30% for 0.5s.

The Powerball then bounces back toward Bruno and lands on the ground. He or allied heroes can retrieve the Powerball for another attack and reduce the cooldown of Flying Tackle by 1s.

skills.name: Flying Tackle
skills.desc:
Bruno makes a slide tackle in the target direction, dealing 140 (+40% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in his path and stunning them for 0.5s.

If the Powerball is bouncing back, Bruno also draws the Powerball toward him.

skills.name: Worldie
skills.desc: Bruno kicks his Energy Ball at the target enemy hero, dealing 250 (+83% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and knocking them back. The Energy Ball then bounces between nearby enemies up to 10 times, each time dealing 150 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage. Each hit also reduces the enemy's Physical Defense by 4% for 8s (up to 3 stacks).

strong.desc: Bane counters Tanks with high HP but low damage, such as Hylos and Belerick, because he can out-sustain them and deals HP percentage-based damage.

weak.desc: Bane is countered by Mages that deal heavy damage from afar, such as Vale and Xavier who can deal significant damage to him before he can get in range.

assist.desc: Bane works best with heroes like as Tigreal and Luo Yi who can pull enemies together so he can do even more damage with his AOE.

Bane.story: Cruel pirate leader, Franco's sworn enemy.

skills.name: Shark Bite
skills.desc: Bane infuses his weapon with a stack of Tidal Energy each time he uses a skill (up to 2 stacks). Each of his subsequent Basic Attacks consumes a stack to deal Physical Damage equal to  (+95% Total Physical Attack) plus 4(+0.4*Hero Level)% of the target's Max HP to nearby enemies.

skills.name: Crab Claw Cannon
skills.desc:
Bane fires his Crab Claw Cannon in the target direction, dealing 150 (+160% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the first enemy hit and then 150% damage to a random enemy behind them (prioritizes enemy heroes). Enemies hit are slowed by 10 (+10% Total Magic Power)% for 1s.

Every 100 Physical Attack Bane possesses reduces this skill's cooldown by an additional 8%.

skills.name: Sea Ink
skills.desc:
Bane swallows a cup of sea ink, recovering 200 (+150% Total Magic Power) (+ 5 (+1.5% Total Magic Power)% his lost HP), gaining 50% Movement Speed that decays over 2.5s.

Use Again: Bane spits corrosive ink in a fan-shaped area, dealing 140 (+130% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage (up to 200% with charging time).

Every 100 Magic Power Bane possesses reduces this skill's cooldown by 7%.

skills.name: Deadly Catch
skills.desc:
Bane summons a school of sharks to charge in the target direction, dealing 700 (+80% Total Physical Attack) (+150% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies hit, knocking them airborne for 0.6s, and slowing them by 75% (decays over 3s).
This skill deals 40% damage to turrets.

strong.desc: Heroes with low mobility like Vale, Estes and Layla.

weak.desc: Heroes with high mobility or Control Immunity such as Wanwan, Fanny and Badang.

assist.desc: Heroes with high burst damage such as Vale, Karina and Brody to take advantage of his CC.

Franco.story: Brave permafrost warrior, Bane's sworn enemy.

skills.name: Wasteland Force
skills.desc:
If no damage is taken within 5s, Franco gains 10% Movement Speed, recovers 1% Max HP per second, and begins accumulating Wasteland Force (up to 10 stacks).

Franco will consume all Wasteland Force stacks on his next skill cast to increase the skill's damage by up to 150%.

skills.name: Iron Hook
skills.desc: Franco launches an iron hook in the target direction. The hook will snag the first enemy unit hit, dealing 400 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and dragging them to Franco.

skills.name: Fury Shock
skills.desc: Franco lashes out, dealing Physical Damage equal to 300 plus 4% of his Max HP to nearby enemies and slowing them by 70% for 1.5s.

skills.name: Bloody Hunt
skills.desc: Franco suppresses the target enemy hero for 1.8s and strikes them 6 times over the duration, each time dealing 50 (+70% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.

strong.desc: Akai is strong against heroes with poor Mobility since he can easily catch and eliminate them with his Ultimate, such as Eudora and Odette.

weak.desc: Akai is countered by heroes with CC removers or transformation abilities that allow them to escape or counter Akai's Ultimate, such as Nana and Diggie.

assist.desc: Akai works best with heroes who can deal high burst damage as they can help him take down enemies caught in his Ultimate, such as Saber and Eudora.

Akai.story: The almighty panda hero of Stream Valley.

skills.name: Tai Chi
skills.desc:
Akai gains a 25 (+5% Total HP) shield for 4s on each skill cast and marks enemy heroes and Creeps hit by his skills.

Akai's Basic Attacks deal 25 (+5% Total HP) extra Physical Damage to marked enemies.

skills.name: Headbutt
skills.desc:
Akai charges in the target direction, dealing 300 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies along the way. If Akai hits an enemy hero during this process, he'll knock them airborne for 0.5s and be able to roll in the Joystick's direction once.

Akai can cast Headbutt during Heavy Spin to quickly adjust his position.

skills.name: Body Slam
skills.desc: Akai smashes the ground with his body, dealing 270 (+6% Total HP) Physical Damage to nearby enemies and slowing them by 45% for 2s.

skills.name: Heavy Spin
skills.desc:
Akai removes all debuffs on him and spins for 4s, gaining Slow Immunity while continuously dealing 100 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies and knocking them back. Enemy heroes knocked back will knock back other heroes they collide with during the process.

Akai also gradually increases his Movement Speed by 70% over the duration. This skill can only be interrupted by Suppression.

strong.desc: Karina counters Marksmen that lack mobility, such as Layla and Hanabi, since her 1st Skill can neutralize their Basic Attacks and they can't escape her high mobility and burst damage.

weak.desc: Karina is countered by Tanky heroes with CC abilities, like Akai and Khufra because they can tank her damage, and protect their allies from her.

assist.desc: Karina works well with heroes who can pull enemies together, such as Tigreal and Faramis, since her Ultimate and 2nd Skill also hits anyone near her main target, setting them up to be chain killed by her Ultimate.

Karina.story: Killer from the Dark Elf tribe who is fighting to rescue her sister Selena.

skills.name: Shadow Combo
skills.desc:
Karina's third consecutive attack on the same enemy deals extra True Damage equal to 100 plus 5 (+2.5% Total Magic Power)% of their lost HP (up to 2000 True Damage against creeps).

If the target is an enemy hero, the cooldowns of Karina's non-Ultimate skills are reduced by 1.5s.

skills.name: Dance of Blades
skills.desc:
Karina enters the Dance of Blades state for 3.5s, during which she gains 45% extra Movement Speed, blocks all incoming Basic Attacks, and reflects 100 (+30% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage back to the attacker (the damage reflection effect has a cooldown of 0.4s).

Karina's next Basic Attack within the duration deals 150 (+55% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage, slows the target by 45% for 1s, and is guaranteed to Crit. Casting the enhanced Basic Attack or Shadow Assault ends the Dance of Blades state early.

skills.name: Dance of Death
skills.desc: Karina launches a spinning slash, dealing 375 (+125% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies.

skills.name: Shadow Assault
skills.desc:
Karina dashes to the target enemy hero, dealing them 350 (+160% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage, applying a Shadow Mark to them, and leaving a Shadowform behind them. The Shadow Mark and Shadowform both last 5s. If the enemy dies within the duration, the cooldown of this skill is reset.

Use Again: Karina dashes back to the Shadowform's location, dealing 150 (+50% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies along the way.

strong.desc: Alucard has great chase abilities and damage resistance, making him powerful against squishy Assassins like Harley and Aamon.

weak.desc:
But he can be countered by heroes with powerful control abilities like Franco and Johnson. If controlled, Alucard won't be able to steal HP
and can be easily eliminated.

assist.desc: He's best with heroes with crowd control, such as Gatotkaca and Xavier, which makes up for his lack of powerful control abilities.

Alucard.story: Demon hunter who detests the darkness.

skills.name: Pursuit
skills.desc:
After each skill cast, Alucard's next Basic Attack allows him to dash to the target's location and deal  (+125% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.
Demon Hunter: Alucard deals 10% extra damage to Creeps.

skills.name: Groundsplitter
skills.desc: Alucard rolls to the target location and slams his blade on the ground, dealing 270 (+85% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit and slowing them by 40% for 2s.

skills.name: Whirling Smash
skills.desc: Alucard launches a whirling slash, dealing 345 (+120% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies.

skills.name: Fission Wave
skills.desc:
Passive: Alucard permanently gains 10% Hybrid Lifesteal.
Active: Alucard absorbs the energy of enemies in the target area, reducing their Movement Speed by 30% and Hybrid Defense by 10. Alucard gains 10 Hybrid Defense for each enemy hero hit, and reduces the cooldown of his other skills to 50% for 6s.
Use Again: Alucard releases a shockwave in the target direction, dealing 400 (+200% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies hit.

strong.desc: Tigreal's Passive can negate enemy Basic Attacks, making him strong against heroes that deal damage with their Basic Attacks, such as Zilong and Freya.

weak.desc: But as a melee hero, Tigreal is countered by Ranged Mage heroes like Chang'e and Zhask.

assist.desc: Tigreal works well with heroes with strong initiation skills, that gives him a better chance to hit the enemies with his CC skills, such as Vexana and Odette.

Tigreal.story: Leader of the Light's Order, guardian of the Moniyan Empire.

skills.name: Fearless
skills.desc:
Tigreal gains a stack of Fearless each time he uses a skill or is hit by a Basic Attack. After getting 4 stacks, Tigreal will consume all Fearless stacks to block the next incoming Basic Attack (including attacks from Turrets).

Attacks from Minions do not grant Fearless stacks nor trigger its effect.

skills.name: Attack Wave
skills.desc: Tigreal smashes the ground with his hammer and sends a shockwave that erupts 3 times in the target direction, each time dealing 270 (+70% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies along the fan-shaped area and slowing them by 20%/40%/60% for 1.5s.

skills.name: Sacred Hammer
skills.desc:
Tigreal charges in the target direction, dealing  (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies along the way and pushing them to the end of the charge.

Use Again: Tigreal can use this skill again within 4s, dealing 280 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies in front of him and knocking them airborne for 0.6s.

skills.name: Implosion
skills.desc:
Tigreal unleashes the power of his hammer, pulling nearby enemies to him and stunning them for 1.8s while dealing 600 (+130% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage.

The first half of the channeling can be interrupted by control effects. The second half of the channeling can only be interrupted by Suppression.

strong.desc: Nana's Passive gives her great survivability, making her strong against heroes with deadly combos, like Eudora and Kadita.

weak.desc: Nana can be countered by heroes with strong chasing abilities, like Zilong, Natalia, because they can chase Nana through her Passive duration.

assist.desc: Nana works best with heroes with CC skills, that will help her get the kills, such as Rafaela and Franco.

skills.name: Molina's Gift
skills.desc:
Upon taking fatal damage, Nana removes all debuffs on her and transforms, becoming untargetable and invincible, and gains 30% Movement Speed for 2s. She also recovers 10% Max HP over the duration.

Nana can only transform once every 150s and cannot cast skills in the transformed form.

skills.name: Magic Boomerang
skills.desc: Nana hurls her Magic Boomerang in the target direction, dealing damage to enemies hit on its way out and back. The boomerang deals 300 (+100% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to the first enemy hit. For every additional enemy hit, the damage is reduced by 10% (max reduction of 30%).

skills.name: Molina Smooch
skills.desc:
Nana throws Molina at the target location, dealing 250 (+50% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies within range and Transforming enemy heroes hit into small animals.

If Molina does not hit an enemy hero, she will remain on the ground and chase the nearest enemy hero that comes into range, Transforming the first enemy hero she touches.

Transform: Reduces the target's Movement Speed by 50% and Magic Defense by 30% for 1.5s. Transformed heroes cannot attack or use skills.

skills.name: Molina Blitz
skills.desc: Nana summons Molina to strike the target area 3 times, each time dealing 440 (+140% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies within and slowing them by 30% for 1s. Enemies hit consecutively will be stunned for 1s.

strong.desc: With her great sustainability in combat plus her mobility and CC, Alice counters heroes that lack burst and can't escape from melee enemies, such as Cecilion and Chang'e.

weak.desc: Alice is countered by heroes with Execute or Healing Reduction who can impede her from healing herself during the Ultimate. Heroes like Baxia or Martis can reduce her healing or kill her before she can heal.

assist.desc: To compensate for Alice's weaknesses in the early game and form a balanced team, pair her with strong early game heroes, like Balmond or Dyrroth.

Alice.story: Queen of the Blood Demons and one of the two dominant powers in the Abyss.

skills.name: Crimson Blood Banquet
skills.desc:
When Alice's skills hit, she gains 1 stack(s) of Crimson, lasting 4s. Once Crimson reaches 2 stacks, Blood Banquet activates, dealing 25 (+30% Total Magic Power) plus 0.5%-2.5% of the target's Max HP (scales with level) as Magic Damage to nearby units (hitting heroes with skills refreshes the duration) and recovering 10 (+3% Extra Max HP) HP for Alice every 0.5s.
While Blood Banquet is active, Alice gains 8% Movement Speed. Blood Banquet can deal up to 70(+10*Hero Level) Magic Damage to creeps.

skills.name: Crimson Gleam
skills.desc: Alice fires blood energy forward, dealing 100 (+50% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage. Before the blood energy dissipates, this skill can be cast again to activate Scarlet Shadow, allowing Alice to blink to the blood energy's location.

skills.name: Doom Waltz
skills.desc: Alice performs a deadly dance, dealing 350 (+130% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to nearby enemies and slowing them by 70% for 1s. If Blood Banquet is active at this time, it also scorches enemies, dealing 300% of Blood Banquet's damage and healing Alice for the same amount.

skills.name: Throne of Ruin
skills.desc:
Alice surrounds herself with blood, gaining Control Immunity and 50% Damage Reduction while slowing herself by 50%. After 1.5s, she descends with force, dealing 500 (+150% Total Magic Power) (+20% Extra Max HP) Magic Damage to all enemies in range and immobilizing them for 1s.
While charging her Ultimate, Alice can only use Scarlet Shadow.

strong.desc: Saber's deadly combo makes him strong against squishy heroes, such as Layla and Eudora.

weak.desc: Saber is countered by Support heroes with Survivability buffs, like Estes and Faramis.

assist.desc: Saber works well with high-damage heroes who can help him burst down enemy heroes in teamfights, such as Kadita and Cyclops.

Saber.story: A swordsman in pursuit of invincibility, used by Laboratory 1718.

skills.name: Enemy's Bane
skills.desc: Each time Saber deals damage, he reduces the target's Physical Defense by 3-8 for 5s (up to 5 stacks).

skills.name: Orbiting Swords
skills.desc:
Saber releases 5 swords that orbit around him and deal 80 (+30% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to nearby enemies on hit. The swords will retract back to him after the duration.

While active, Sword attacks deal 210 (+60% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the target and 50% damage to enemies along the path (damage reduced to 50% against minions). Each sword attack also reduces the cooldown of Charge by 1s.

skills.name: Charge
skills.desc:
Saber dashes in the target direction, dealing 75 (+50% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies along the path.

After the dash, his next Basic Attack is enhanced, allowing him to dash to the target, dealing 75 (+120% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and slowing them by 60% for 1s.

skills.name: Triple Sweep
skills.desc: Saber charges at the target enemy hero, knocking them airborne for 1.2s (unaffected by Resilience). He strikes them 3 times over the duration, dealing 120 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage with the first 2 strikes and 240 (+200% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage with the third.

strong.desc: Balmond is strong against squishy heroes who lack the ability to escape once he closes the gap on them, such as Layla and Eudora.

weak.desc: Balmond is countered by ranged heroes who can deal damage to Balmond outside of his attack range, such as Lesley and Irithel.

assist.desc: Balmond is best with Support heroes that can boost his Mobility and Survivability. Examples include Angela and Rafaela.

Balmond.story: One day, the Orcs will break free from their shackles.

skills.name: Bloodthirst
skills.desc: Balmond recovers 5% Max HP after killing a Minion or Creep, and 20% Max HP after killing a hero.

skills.name: Soul Lock
skills.desc: Balmond charges in the target direction for a set distance or until he hits an enemy hero, dealing 150 (+60% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies along the way. The enemy hero hit will be knocked back slightly, and will have their Movement Speed reduced by 40% and Physical Defense reduced by 40% for 3s.

skills.name: Cyclone Sweep
skills.desc:
Balmond gains 15% Movement Speed that decays over time and spins with his axe for 3s, dealing 25 (+2% Extra Max HP)  (+25% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage per hit to nearby enemies up to 14 times. Each subsequent hit on the same enemy will deal 8.5% increased damage (up to 68%).

The Physical Attack bonus portion of the damage can Crit.

skills.name: Lethal Counter
skills.desc:
Balmond unleashes a huge strike in the target direction (this skill can only be interrupted by Suppression), dealing True Damage equal to 150 (+70% Total Physical Attack) plus 30% of the target's lost HP.

This skill only deals up to 1000 damage to non-hero units.

strong.desc: Miya counters heroes who rely on targeted CC effects, such as Saber and Eudora, because she can easily escape their CC and respond with a strong counterattack. The split arrows from her 1st Skill are also very effective against heroes that summon units, such as Sun.

weak.desc: Miya is countered by heroes with a lot of AOE damage, such as Pharsa and Yve, because they can still hit her while she is concealed.

assist.desc: Miya works well with Tanks who have powerful CC abilities like Belerick and Tigreal since their CCs give her more opportunities to dish out damage.

Miya.story: The Priestess of the Moon and protector of the Moonlit Forest.

skills.name: Moon Blessing
skills.desc:
Each time Miya hits a target with her Basic Attack, she gains 5% Attack Speed for 4s. Stacks up to 5 times.

After reaching full stacks, Miya summons a Moonlight Shadow with each Basic Attack that deals 30 (+25% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and inherits a portion of Attack Effects.

skills.name: Moon Arrow
skills.desc: Miya shoots two extra arrows with each Basic Attack, dealing 10 (+100% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the target enemy and 30% damage to nearby targets. This effect lasts 4s. Each extra arrow inherits a portion of Attack Effects.

skills.name: Arrow of Eclipse
skills.desc: Miya launches an empowered arrow on the target area, dealing 270 (+45% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies within and immobilizing them for 1.2s. The arrow then splits into 6 scattering minor arrows, each dealing 40 (+20% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage to the first enemy hit and slowing them by 30% for 2s.

skills.name: Hidden Moonlight
skills.desc:
Miya removes all debuffs on her and conceals herself, gaining 65% extra Movement Speed. This state lasts 2s or until she launches an attack.

Miya gains full stacks of Moon Blessing upon leaving the state.
